title: Student Guide
content: Student Guide Welcome to Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) We are delighted to welcome all international students to IZU. To ensure a smooth transition into university life, we highly recommend reviewing International Student Guide. This guide contains essential information, and students are expected to familiarise themselves with its contents as they will be held responsible for details provided. It is also mandatory to join official WhatsApp group for international students. If you have not yet been added, please get in touch with International Office, located at entrance of School of Languages. Contact International Office Phone : +90 212 692 9622 WhatsApp : +90 505 182 4316 Email : international@izu.edu.tr Address : Halkali Campus, School of Languages, The International Office is located in rooms DL 033-034, on right side when you enter School of Languages building. Our office hours are from 09:30 to 16:30, Monday to Friday. We provide support in multiple languages to assist you in any way we can. We are here to help you settle in and make most of your time at IZU!

title: Library working hours changed until the university's education start date.
content: Library working hours changed until the university's education start date. 16.02.2023 Due to the earthquake disaster, we are in; it is not yet clear when formal education will begin. During this period, many of our students are out of the city due to earthquake victims or taking part in relief efforts. Our libraries' working hours until the university's start of education are arranged as follows until a second announcement. * IZU Central Library Weekdays :08.30 - 20.00 Saturday - Sunday:10.00 - 20.00 * IZU Altunizade Library: Weekdays :08.30 - 20.00 Saturday - Sunday:10.00 - 20.00 * ISEFAM Library: Weekdays:7/24 Saturday - Sunday: 7/24 Department of Library and Documentation

title: IZU Central Library, The Greatest Library in Istanbul has been opened.
content: IZU Central Library, The Greatest Library in Istanbul has been opened. 20.05.2018 After completion of its construction, new Central Library of Sabahattin Zaim University in Istanbul has been opened. With a 13.500 square meters of area of usage, 1500 researching attendees and 1 million book capacity, IZU Central Library is now greatest library in Istanbul and stands out as one of largest in Turkey. IZU Central Library now being heart of University continues to maintain scientific legacy of Halkali Agriculture and Veterinary School and Mehmet Akif Ersoy. The Central Library provides access to knowledge to universitys academicians and students in a very comfortable environmant and is already one of most essential place in institutions grounds. IZU Central Library will continue to serve for 24 hours during final exams.

title: About Library working hours due to Eid al-Adha public holiday
content: About Library working hours due to Eid al-Adha public holiday 21.06.2024 Due to the official holiday of Eid al-Adha, the Library working hours have been arranged as follows. Central Library; It will be open until 13:00 on Saturday, June 15. It will be closed between June 16-23. It will be open during working hours on Monday, June 24. ISEFAM Reading Hall; It will be closed between June 15-18. It will be open 24/7 between June 19-23. Registered external users can also use it.

title: International Collaborations
content: Collaborations The Erasmus + Student Mobility Program is a higher education program aimed at increasing quality of higher education in Europe envisaging "international mobility of university students" by encouraging cooperation between universities from different countries. The Erasmus program is financed by European Union students receives a grant chance to study in different European countries for a certain period of time. The Erasmus program is coordinated by IZU Office. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim has Erasmus + agreement with 38 universities in 20 countries. Up to now(today), a total of 70 students have successfully completed Erasmus study internship mobility program. Our students benefited from mobility in almost all European countries, especially in Germany, France, Italy Poland. At same time IZU accepts a great number of exchange students from Europe every semester with programs offering 100% 30% English. In addition to Erasmus program, we are developing collaborations with important universities from other parts of world. These cooperation programs include student-academic exchanges, joint symposia conferences, joint research projects, master's PHDs.Some of these cooperation agreements are; Major American universities such as Duke Mary Washington, our students have opportunity to be a student of change, take (taking)summer courses take(taking) advantage of English language education.For Department of Sciences, where Arabic language education is important,we have agreements with of Jordan World Science & Education in Jordan.In Addition conferences are held every year in with Universiti Utara Malaysia. title: About the 2020-2021 Spring Term PhD Qualifying(Comprehensive) Exam Schedule
content: About 2020-2021 Spring Term PhD Qualifying(Comprehensive) Exam Schedule 29.03.2021 Dear Student, Students who wish to apply for PhD qualifying exam to be held in spring semester of 2020-2021 academic year are required to fill in application form below in computer environment between 19 April 2021 and apply to Graduate School Secretariat together with their language proficiency and ALES results. Applications can be made to relevant Institute official by mail or by hand delivery to Institute Secretariat at Halkali Campus. Click for PhD Qualifying Exam Application Form. (Unsigned documents will not be accepted.) Click for e-mail addresses of relevant Institute officials. Doctoral Qualifying Exams will be held in June 2021, and date and time will be decided by relevant head of program. How exams will be conducted will be announced later on. (online or face to face) ISSUES TO BE CONSIDERED Students who have completed their courses and seminars, have a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.00 and have fulfilled their Foreign Language Proficiency may apply for doctoral proficiency exam. Students who are accepted with a master's degree have to take doctoral qualifying exam by end of fifth semester at latest, following relevant regulations. Students who did not apply for PhD Qualifying Exam held in fall semester of 2020-2021 academic year or failed exam must apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be deemed unsuccessful and will be dismissed from University.

title: IZU Call Center
content: IZU Call Center ABOUT US IZU Solution Center aims to provide solutions as quickly as possible for all applicants that are related to IZU. Providing suggestions and giving fast results with easy approachable and reliable management support. IZU Solution Center targets to increase the satisfaction level of the services provided in IZU by informing the applicants about their requests and complaints as soon as possible. All negotiations with IZU Solution Center are recorded because of service quality standards and communication security. title: Staff Mobility
content: Erasmus+ Staff Mobility Activities STAFF From ABROAD Are you interested in teaching or training at IZU? View pages for incoming staff STAFF From IZU Are you interested in teaching or training abroad? View pages for outgoing staff General Information Staff Mobility for Teaching Erasmus+ supports teaching periods at higher education institutions (HEIs) abroad. If you work in higher education, you can spend time teaching at an institution in an Erasmus+ Programme or Partner country. Teaching opportunites are also available to staff working in Programme country organisations. Examples of such organisations include businesses, public bodies, social partners, research institutes. Staff Mobility for Training Erasmus+ supports training periods for staff working in higher education institutions. With Erasmus+, you can train at a higher education institution in an Erasmus+ Programme or Partner country or at an organisation outside the sector in a Programme country. Examples of such organisations include businesses, public bodies, social partners, research institutes and non-governmental organisations.

title: Student Mobility
content: APPLICATION CALENDAR Click here for Erasmus+ Student Mobility for studies and tranieeships applications! View Erasmus+ Student Mobility Activities STUDENT From ABROAD Are you interested in studying or doing internship at IZU? View pages for incoming student STUDENT From IZU Are you interested in studying or doing internship abroad? View pages for outgoing student General Information STUDENT MOBILITY FOR STUDIES Student mobility for studies, the centrepiece of the programme, enables students at higher education institutions to spend an integrated period of study of between 3 months and 12 months in Erasmus+ Program Countries. STUDENT MOBILITY FOR TRANIEESHIPS Student Mobility for traineeships is which gives students a chance to broaden their vocational and training curriculum by working abroad in any public sector company or a private commercial enterprise located in Erasmus+ Program Countries. F.A.Q Click here to view the most frequently asked questions about the Erasmus+! View

title: Student Guide
content: Student Guide Welcome to Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) We are delighted to welcome all international students to IZU. To ensure a smooth transition into university life, we highly recommend reviewing International Student Guide. This guide contains essential information, and students are expected to familiarise themselves with its contents as they will be held responsible for details provided. It is also mandatory to join official WhatsApp group for international students. If you have not yet been added, please get in touch with International Office, located at entrance of School of Languages. Contact International Office Phone : +90 212 692 9622 WhatsApp : +90 505 182 4316 Email : international@izu.edu.tr Address : Halkali Campus, School of Languages, The International Office is located in rooms DL 033-034, on right side when you enter School of Languages building. Our office hours are from 09:30 to 16:30, Monday to Friday. We provide support in multiple languages to assist you in any way we can. We are here to help you settle in and make most of your time at IZU!

title: Education in Turkey
content: Education in It is highly recommended foreigners wish to reasons, should overview related our countrys visa procedures in advance before arrival. Visa procedure touristic business purposed visits to a country differ in relative to work visits. Also Turkeys visa policies change country to country according to reciprocity principle. Some applicants be exempted getting a visa to if it is touristic or business purposes. Others obtain touristic Visa through electronic Visa. All applicants want to obtain a visa working purposes to apply to our Representations abroad. According to countries, an note Turkeys visa regime is available in following address: www.mfa.gov.tr Foreigners wish to enter to carry a visa, a visa exemption, date of resident permit duration ends. They to request a passport or documentations shows a passport is valid atleast 60 days. Exampe: A Foreign visitor owns a visa 90 days resident permit duration, they to 150 days (90 days + 60 days/ 5 month) valid passport or a travel document at time of entrance in Turkey. Passports must a validity of 6 months atleast expiration date of visa. They 90 days to do so. visa information, please Ministry of Foreign Affairs official website: http://www.mfa.gov.tr/sub.en.mfa?cc4e437c-6769-4d79-9017-10b63c651224 regarding residence permit, please National Polices official website: http://www.egm.gov.tr/EN/Pages/Residence-Permit-Procedures.aspx Please keep in mind university assist residence permit procedures. do to do it alone, help is there. Many Universities in provide campus accommodation to wish to stay on campus or in state dormitories. Meals generally included in fee but dormitories canteens where eat breakfast meals a nominal, relatively low cost. All fees fixed in Liras. Regardless of nationality, must pay same amount as students. In addition, most dormitories segregated by sex. It is advised bring enough Money as cash as credit card so it be easily converted to Lira. Credit cards be accepted. In Turkey, processes related to accommodation in state dormitories carried out by General Directorate of Higher Education Credit Hostels Institution. detailed information, please www.kyk.gov.tr web page. If looking a room share, find vacancies posted in hostels, language schools, or similiar areas regarding accommodation. In addition to sources, a chance to find an apartment in universitys campus. All recieving in take advantage of general insurance coverage in Turkey. Students "general insurance", use state hospitals without any fee, use private hospitals by paying a moderate fee. To benefit General Health Insurance System in Turkey, go to schools Student Office to get information. Then necessary documents, go to Social Security Institution to apply. When complete application, pay general insurance fee which is determined on an annual basis within 1 month date of Social Security records. If don't apply general insurance system within given time period (3 months) cannot benefit general insurance services of State. But, if an insurance they use it or get benefit private insurances in Turkey. Some offer special insurance policy students. Please bear in mind is known providing high quality services at low costs. reason patients neighbouring countries as well as developed western countries prefer to come to to get healthier cheaper treatments surgery, dental, eye examinations. Students In order to face any problems until they benefit healt insurance in Turkey, we recommend them to go checkups, dentists.We recommend to bring along glasses, a spare pair of contact lenses optical prescription them. Lastly bringing along a first-aid kit be really useful. is home to worlds earliest settlements numerous civilizations ranging smallest of communities to greatest of empires. is cradle of cultures civilizations connecting Europe Asia capital of civilizations reined lands of Anatolia centuries. Since beginning of history, Anatolia, well known as one of earliest settlements, continued to flourish migration of various tribes, accumulated a large cultural heritage through a line of succeeding empires civilizations. Many empires ranging Sumerians to Hittites, Lydians to Byzantines Seljuks to Ottomans once thrived expired within borders of Turkey. To get holiday cities of to decide which cities to in Turkey, please official tourism portal of http://www.goturkeytourism.com Mediterranean Aegean climates a cool moist winter season, a warm rainless summer season. climate in Aegean region of is great throughout year, even during winter. weather in eastern region of Turkey, bordering Georgia Iraq, hot summers snowy icy winters. Turkeys climate weather along coast of Black Sea is a continental climate. In Turkey, humidity levels be quite high. During winter, especially in months of December January, weather is cold temperatures drop below zero. weather in Turkeys capital, Ankara, as well as central Anatolian region is cold in winters hot dry during summer. Turkeys central climate is known as a mixture between a continental climate a steppe climate. To get on Turkeys weather please State Meteorological Services official website: http://www.mgm.gov.tr/ homeland to numerous communities civilizations beginning of history of mankind Anatolia is hosting magnificent Culture hundreds of years. Due to geographical continents connects Europe Asia, culture evolving influence of civilizations. Anatolia cradle of civilizations throughout history effects of cultures reflect cuisine social life. If curious about; customs, traditions, folk dances, theatre, cinema, fine arts, literature, traditional modern music,please official tourism portal of http://www.goturkeytourism.com One of best things culture is variety of its cuisine! find only food, but delicious samples of food western eastern kitchen cuisine. Eating habits shaped according to prevalent cultural, geographical, ecological, economic characteristics is shaped according to historical duration. In summary, variety of products offered by lands of Asia Anatolia, interaction numerous cultures over a long historical process, new tastes developed in palace kitchens of Seljuk Ottoman empires played a part in shaping our new culinary culture, which would very much enjoy. traditional Ottoman cuisine more, please official website of Ministry of Culture Tourism: http://www.kultur.gov.tr/EN,35306/culinary-culture.html These afew popular native foods in Turkey: 1- Kebap 2- Simit 3- Yogurt Ayran 4- Kahvalti (Balli-Kaymakli) 5- Lokum 6- Baklava 7- Findik Fistik (Kuruyemisler) 8- Meyveler 9- Pide 10- Lahmacun 11- Tursu 12- Maras Dondurmasi 13- Manti 14- Dolma  Sarma 15- Kuru Fasulye 16- Kestane Sekeri / Kebab 17- Tavukgögsü Tatlisi 18- Türk Kahvesi  Lokumu 19- Börek 20- Sahlep (Vahsi orkide köklerinden kremali  tarçinli mucize içecek) 21- Asure 22- Boza monetary unit is Lira (TL). Travelers checks favored nor accepted in Turkey. ATMs be found in even smallest towns. Most accept credit cards or bank cards (a strip of logos is usually displayed above ATM). Almost ATMs a language key to enable to read instructions in English. Most commercial establishments accept credit cards such as Visa, Master Card American Express. banking systems highly developed, easily find a bank ATM anywhere. make payments at any ATMs by using credit cards. open an account on name, to get an ATM card a resident document. value-added tax, is known as KDV, is 18%. Value-added tax is nearly always included in quoted prices. Certain shops deduct tax, therefore to ask whether any deductions be made. electrical current in is 220 volts as Alternating current (AC) 50 hertz cycles alternating current (AC); wall outlets take Continental-type plugs, two or three round prongs. Jan 1: First Day of New Year Apr 23: National Sovereignty Children's Day (anniversary of establishment of Grand National Assembly in 1920) May 1: Labour Solidarity Day (since 2009) May 19: Atatürk Commemoration Youth & Sports Day (the arrival of Atatürk in Samsun in 1919, beginning of War of Independence) Aug 30: Victory Day (victory over invading forces in 1922). Oct 29: Republic Day (anniversary of declaration of Republic in 1923). Ramazan Bayrami: Three-day festival is celebrated end of fast of Ramadan month. Also known as "Seker (sweets) Bayrami" because it's customary to offer candies to family members friends come to visit. Kurban Bayrami: Four-day festival where sheeps or cows sacrificed meat distributed to poor. dates of religious festivals change according to Islamic calendar thus occur 10-11 days earlier each year. (This is due to 10 days 21 hrs difference between Gregorian Lunar calendars). According to this; Standard time zone: UTC/GMT + 3 hours To make a long-distance call (any call outside of city code), to dial long-distance prefix (0) before seven-digit local phone number. (The address of new university, plus telephone number of person related be helpful to you). Health travel insurance: Firstly, get advice university  they offer a special insurance policy students. Asking insurance companies be helpful. vaccinations health-related considerations, check out our section. Do forget most airlines charge excess baggage. Temperatures in vary season to season region to region. Turkeys weather, check our Weather section. Bridge to World reasons to come to Turkey! Each outskirt its own spiritual geographical features attracts people around world. example, Istanbul is only city in world connects two continents, Asia Europe. city, which is located in both Asia Europe, capital city civilizations thousands of years throughout its history. is than a bridge between two continents. Actually, is a country in which East West meet in a much broader sense. successful in blending two cultures being home to cultural historical richness legacy thousands of years. A Lot of Scholarship Opportunities chance to study abroad is a dream students, especially in todays global era. According to UNESCO, 3,6 million were enrolled in higher abroad in 2010, number is increasing every year. In challenging competitive environment, there a wide variety of scholarship opportunities in available you. Many of scholarships provided by republic of State. information, please http://www.turkiyeburslari.gov.tr/index.php/en/turkiye-burslari/burs-programlari There scholarship processes in private universities. (%100 - %75 - %50 Scholarships An Ideal Place Living experience of studying abroad is than just attending a school in a foreign country. So, deciding where to study abroad, should first ask yourself where want to live. With its cosmopolitan cities small towns, offers a variety of cultural experiences to students. Bringing cultures, religions, people, lifestyles definitely cuisines together. served as a gate to Western World Eastern cultures to Eastern World Western societies. Weather is another important consideration to take deciding to study abroad. is one of few countries experience enjoy four seasons thanks to its geographical position climate. Being surrounded by three seas, is an ideal destination where see lots of sun during summer enough snow to do skiing in winter. Costs of Living Studying costs of studying living abroad is another factor to take into consideration while making decision studying abroad. Compared to student destinations, offers significantly lower annual tuition fees much lower living expenses. According to Mercers 2013 Global cost of living rankings, Turkeys metropolitan cities in very bottom row of most expensive cities list. A Door to Your Dream Job One of main questions after graduation is what happen after this?International graduate a lot of options. example choose to continue by doing a masters degree, return home a widely accepted diploma ease them to find a job either in or out of Turkey. In addition, able to work part-time on campus during education. is progressing towards being one of leading economies of world its developing growing economy. number of Global brands doing business in working together brands is increasing. As of June 2013, number of companies operating in is 34,000, 50% of companies European Union countries. As is a favorite country investors, chance to work in one of companies. witness classmates or friends achieve significant success in careers in even in world. So, use contacts to open doors to new opportunities yourself. Quality of Education In last 10 years, higher system developed significantly both in terms of quality quantity. now offer a rich field of choices students. quality of system reflected ranking lists. According to Times Higher Education BRICS & Emerging Economies Rankings 2014, 7 entered in top 100 university list, 3 of them in top 10. In addition, 9 took place among top 800 world ranked by Quacquarelli Symonds (QS) in UK. 19 took place among top 1000 world ranked by METU Informatics Institute. offer high quality studies a integral part of European space. There is an increasing number of offering programs taught in English, include medicine, engineering, humanities, business, finance. is part of Bologna Process since 2001 a member of platforms. is important in two ways. First being continue in countries ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) second they get diplomas recognized by countries. University  Industry Cooperation Regarding link between university-industry, starting year 2000, techno-parks technology development centers established within university campuses. Law on Technology Development Centers, which entered into force in 2001, draws framework legal status of institutions. As of 2014, there still 32 technology development centers operating an additional 11 under construction in universities. Associate Degree qualifications included in higher system. Within degree, offer two-year programs, of which vocational oriented at end of two year study period, graduates expected to be employed as intermediate staff in related sectors. curriculum design of programs is done in such a way gives student opportunity to do internships in firms of related sectors. Language In Turkey, most common languages used in higher institutions are; Turkish, English, Arabic. There higher institutions where is taught in German or French. However, if applied to an academic program is taught in Turkish, expected to learn Turkish. is one of most common spoken languages in World. those International want to enhance Turkish, is an ideal place studying in English to learn aswell. There some alternative ways to do so: Language Teaching Research Center at respective university (TÖMER) Language Center at respective university Yunus Emre Institute Cultural Centers, please visit: http://www.yee.org.tr/en/ Private Language Schools Student Clubs Hello Merhaba mehr-hah-bah Good morning Günaydin gew-naye-dun Good evening Iyi aksamlar ee ahk-shahm-lahr Good-bye (said by person leaving) Hosçakal Hosh cha kal Good-bye (said by person staying) Güle güle guele guele How you? Nasilsin? nah-suhl-suhn Whats up / Howre doing? (Informal) Naber? na berr I am fine Iyiyim ee-yeem Thank Tesekkür ederim. te-shek-kewr-lehr Youre welcome Birsey degil. (in response to Thank you) beer-shey deh-eel Please Lütfen. lewt-fehn I dont understand Anlamadim an-la-ma-duhm Pleased to meet Memnum oldum mem-noon oll-doom I am sorry Pardon puhr-dohn Excuse me Pardon puhr-dohn Yes Evet. eh-vet No Hayir. hah-yuhr Bon Appetite! Afiyet olsun! af-ee-yeht ul-sun What? Ne? neh Help! Imdat! im-duht When? Ne zaman? neh-zah-mah One bir beer Two iki ee-kee Three üç ewch Four dört dirt Five bes besh Six alti ahl-tuh Seven yedi yeh-dee Eight sekiz seh-keez Nine dokuz doh-kooz Ten on ohn Literal Translation 1. Hos geldiniz Your arrival is lovely/welcome Said as a greeting to visitors to make them feel at home. 2. Seytan tüyü The devils feather What seems like an insult is actually a compliment used to describe a mysterious, devilish charm. 3. Tas atti da kolu mu yoruldu? Did he throw a stone so his arm got tired? Said to berate whos being lazy. 4. Bir yastikta kocasinlar they grow old one pillow By they mean to tell couples to never go to bed angry, is said to newlyweds traditionally share a single, long pillow in a marital bed. 5. Etekleri zil çaliyor The bells on hems ringing I recently learned evocative saying used to describe is very excited. 6. Pabucu dama atildi His shoe thrown onto roof Often said of a father a new baby monopolises mothers attentions, or by anyone passed over in favour of another. 7. Balik etli Fish fleshed men usually like women a bit of meat on bones, is a lovely compliment to curvy girls. 8. Havadan sudan Of air water Turks love to chat, often at length, nothing in particular. is a great way of describing idle chatter  a lot like Irish craic. 9. Elini sallasa ellisi If he waves his hand, fifty come A great way to comfort a friend after a breakup, it means there plenty of fish in sea. 10. Cigerimin kösesi The corner of my liver anatomic description is actually used to describe is very precious to you. might hear it featured as a lyric in love songs. 11. Armut pis agzima düs pear be cooked on tree fall into my mouth describes a person doesnt like to work, to whom everything comes ready done  or falls literally in lap. 12. Iyi ki dogdun Its good were born Happy Birthday. I think expression is a lot meaningful than English version, I even use literal English translation on my non-Turkish friends birthdays. 13. Bosver Give empty Perhaps my favorite saying, Bosver means let it go, a la Frozen. 14. El elden üstündür A hand is superior to another persons hand Said to show theres always do a better job. 15. Batti balik yan gider A sunken fish goes sideways ones pretty hard to parse, but it means worst-case scenario already happened (the fish sunk), so might as well stop worrying some fun! 16. Ates almaya gelmek Coming over just a light cheekily accuse a visitor of want them to stay a while longer. 17. Cami yikilmis ama mihrab yerinde The mosque is a ruin but mihrap is standing One of my personal favorites, phrase describes an older woman retains her charms. Its like saying a church is in ruins but pulpit is standing. Basically, its opposite of mutton dressed as lamb. 18. Geçmis olsun it be past Said is sick or had a bad experience. I love how it recognizes someones pain, but expresses hope it soon be behind them. 19. Nazar degmesin be touched by evil eye Said after giving a compliment, particularly to a child. Although Im a pretty rational person, I do believe in power of evil eye use saying a lot. 20. Hayirli olsun it be auspicious Said whenever a new undertaking, such as a new job. 21. Allah anali babali büyütsün God let him/her grow up both a mom dad Perhaps most meaningful thing could wish a newborn child. 22. Kolay gelsin May it be easy you is a great way to acknowledge another persons labors, even a stranger on street, a difficult task at hand. 23. Çok yasa live long bless you, çok yasa is said after sneezes. sneezer then replies sende gör (may see my life) or hep beraber (may we years to live). 24. Kafayi üsüttü Theyve caught a cold in head Turks a congenital fear of catching a chill, which strike any part of body. If youve caught a cold in head, it means youve gone crazy. 25. Sihhatler olsun it bring health Older generations grew up before hot running water in homes, only had baths once or twice a week, say come out of shower in case catch a chill (see above). 26. Basiniz sagolsun head stay healthy A thoughtful way of wishing condolences they grieving loss of a loved one. 27. (Benim) ilk göz agrim The first pain of my eye is said to a first love or first child to avoid giving them evil eye (I used to feel very jealous my grandmother said to my brother, her oldest grandchild.) 28. Ellerine saglik Health to hand Said to created something beautiful hands, particularly to a cook or a mom after shes cooked up a feast her family. 29. Bir musibet bin nasihatten iyidir One bad experience is worth a thousand warnings No explanation needed. 30. Güle güle gidin go laughing is said to departing visitors to help take edge off sad goodbyes.

title: Equivalence Certificate
content: Certificate It certifies that taken is equivalent to in Turkey. You cannot enroll to a university in Turkey without of equivalence. Here is how obtain of equivalence. Here steps to obtain of equivalence: 1-Select place to apply which suits best: It is better easier to apply of while in country. You need find office of Educational Attaché in country. Please click here to see list of educational attaché in country. If country is on list, need to apply of as soon as come to Turkey. 2-Prepare required documents: Original of diploma transcripts of all years should be ready when apply of equivalence. Please do forget to get stamp on original documents. Because either Educational Attaché in country or Provincial Directorate of do accept documents, which not, verified by Ministry of Foreign Affairs. 3-Applying in Turkey Students may apply online at https://edenklik.meb.gov.tr/ . All of detailed information is available at https://edenklik.meb.gov.tr/ . Suggestion: It is recommended to choose PROVINCIAL: Istanbul, DISTRICT: Büyüksehir in selection of Center when applying online Certificate. 4-Take original back Provincial Directorate of Education: After evaluate application of equivalence, either will issue of or will be able to issue it. If issue certificate, should bring all required to Student Affairs to complete registration. Important Notes: If there is any Educational Attache in home country or if taken of equivalence, yet should apply as soon as arrive to Turkey. Bear in mind that application process of may take a long time. The students who did submit their valid residence permit at time of application will most probably get temporary of equivalence. Then, if bring residence permit, their permanent of will be issued. What is Certificate? A report taken primary secondary institutions abroad. Transcript, certificate, schools examined to find out equivalent in Turkey such as area, department, branch. (Ministry of Regulation) Where do I need to apply Certificate? processing; provincial national directorates in Turkey, counselors attache offices abroad. Applications made to these institutions. (Ministry of Regulation) What required processing? For those who applied certificate, originals photocopies of following shall be attached to Application Form. (Ek-1 Form) a) Documents last primary school year received schools attended, of separation if there is a learning certificate. b) Documents last secondary school year received schools attended, of separation if there is a learning certificate. c) Secondary school completion certificate, of study last academic year received institution of study abroad, diploma (original "Apostille" certified diploma). d) Translation made by sworn translators of passport used during study abroad, or entry exit dates pages of passport. e) In case that there is no field instructor or specialist in institution to perform process, Turkish translations made by sworn translators of foreign accepted. (Ministry of Regulation) * Translation of required in these languages: English, German, Arabic, Persian, Russian, Bulgarian, Bosnian Romanian. Do I to a valid residence permit or study visa foreigners? Yes. (MEB. TTKB. No. 2236435 dated 28/08/2013) What do I need to do to get Certificate? Complete all needed application. Documents originals photocopies should be put in a transparent file. The application form is completed signed. The applications shall be made to Higher Overseas Branch (Office 1) by person or a legal guardian on request Certificate. After files examined, delivered to "Document Registration" unit with special file (transparent cover) necessary processing. At end of necessary examinations made by relevant experts, equivalents of those deemed appropriate prepared. (Approximately 14 days) The originals of handed over at application stage submitted to applicant or his legal representative in special file (transparent cover) together with Certificate. Certificate Application Form Please click here to download application form. Where is Istanbul Provincial Directorate of Education? Address : Binbirdirek M. Imran Öktem, Eski Toptasi Caddesi Eski Adliye Binasi D: 1, 34122 Sultanahmet / Fatih / Istanbul Working hours: Monday 08: 30-17: 00 Tuesday 08: 30-17: 00 Wednesday 08: 30-17: 00 Thursday 08: 30-17: 00 Friday 08: 30-17: 00 Saturday Closed Sunday Closed Phone: (0212) 455 04 00 Map : I'm coming schools opened abroad by Republic of Turkey Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus. Do I to get an Certificate? The students coming schools opened abroad by Republic of Turkey Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus subjected to this process. These students will be enrolled in same kind of school according to of attestation, diploma other educational documents, files requested school. Registration admission to schools that receive students with a central exam aptitude test in our country carried out according to provisions of special regulations of schools. (Ministry of Regulation) I completed my via internet or distance system. Can I get an certificate? For electronic education/distance methods operations performed. (Ministry of Regulation) I gained right to continue to a higher-level class where I had in a country/institution. Would there be a drawback to process? Those who earned right to attend a higher by completing school level in country where arrive in a shorter period of time, their rights to attend a higher level will be protected. (Ministry of Regulation) How to confirm Certificate? The Equivalency Documents can be confirmed by going through following steps website " evraksorgu.meb.gov.tr ". - Enter security code. - Enter document number. (The last 7 digits in heading 'Number' at top of Certificate) - Enter document code. (Located at bottom of Certificate) - Click on Send button.

title: Step by Step Registration
content: Step by Step Registration IZU registration dates are in August-September. After being accepted to an undergraduate program at Istanbul Zaim University, should immediately start working on registration. should pay utmost attention to below instructions to experience a smooth process. Remember, registration process is time sensitive. lose accepted status in case are late to register. Be sure to finish registration process in advance. 1 st Step: The Certificate of Equivalence (Denklik Belgesi) The of equivalence certifies that education have taken is equivalent to education in Turkey. cannot enroll to a university in Turkey without of equivalence. Thus, it should be first step registration. Here is how obtain of equivalence. are required to submit original high school diploma transcripts of all years of education to one of following offices: The education counselors education attaché office of Turkey located in country where reside. Please click here to see list of educational attaché in country. may find this office inside Turkish Embassies or consulates. This is easiest, quickest surest way to obtain certificate. By obtaining before travelling to Turkey ensure that are eligible to enroll at a Turkish university. The offices of provincial national education directorate in Turkey. The closest one is located in Sultanahmet District, Istanbul. Work load of ministry in Istanbul spikes on August September. We strongly recommend to apply well in advance . Please click here to see location of Provincial Directorate of National Education. Important : consider this option only if there is not an attaché office in country. If have to apply in Istanbul then this cannot be first step; however, should understand that this is most important document registration. If live in Turkey, this should absolutely be 1 st step 2 nd Step: Payment of Tuition Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is a non-profit foundation university. It is a type of university that is funded by charity student fee. Therefore, students are required to pay tuition prior to start of classes each semester. are required to present proof of payment at enrolment. The fees scholarships page explains important information regarding payment of fees scholarship. 3 rd Step: Accommodation should consider where to live in Istanbul before coming. If would like to apply IZU Student Guesthouses, should follow its application dates. Istanbul is second largest city in world with number of universities. So may find many options accommodation. 4 th Step: Travelling to Istanbul be allocated with a mentor before travel. It is best if purchase tickets journey with consultation as mentor might want to be there arrival. In case did not obtain of equivalence before travel, we would recommend to arrive in Istanbul at least 3 weeks before end of registration period. be allocated with a mentor before travel. It is best if purchase tickets journey with consultation as mentor might want to be there arrival. In case did not obtain of equivalence before travel, we would recommend to arrive in Istanbul at least 3 weeks before end of registration period. Be mindful of important dates when decide on travel plans. Participation orientation program is not option but a prerequisite registration. It is required to be present at orientation. If do not hold proficiency in language of program, then should take IZU language proficiency exam. Please follow updates regarding dates of proficiency exam as exam not be repeated. We urge to check International Student section of website. find  WELCOME KIT  there which is a booklet prepared with much effort to make everything easier you. Our team at international office provided answers to questions might address. shall find every little detail that may be useful in next four to five years. find which door to knock on which request in coming pages. especially find part how do I succeed at IZU crucial to learn how to avoid pitfalls be a successful student. Finally, find a brief introduction to life in Istanbul Turkey, which be guide not to survive but to thrive. 5 th Step: Registration Come to IZU Halkali Campus take along required documents others submitted in application. First, international office check authenticity of documents. Then be directed to office of student affairs where complete registration. Here is list of required documents registration; Acceptance Letter High School Diploma: Original apostilled copy. Transcript: Apostilled, translated, notarized. Passport: Identity validity pages, translated notarized. Dual Citizenship Documents: Relevant certificates. Citizenship Exit Documents: Exit or Blue Card. Entry-Exit Document: Travel dates Turkish citizens studying abroad. Legal Stay Document: Proof of legal stay in Türkiye. Language Proficiency Certificate: Approved by Turkish consulate. Tuition Fee Receipt Passport Photos: Four photos. Signed International Student Commitment Health Insurance: Proof within three months. Students missing original documents must submit them by end of academic year; otherwise, their registration be cancelled without a refund. Please keep us informed throughout process so that we may help you: international@izu.edu.tr

title: Apply to IZU
content: Apply to IZU Application Requirements Applications candidates graduated school or are in final year meet following criteria be considered: Foreign nationals, Those were nationals but later acquired citizenship, Those were born as but later renounced citizenship with permission Ministry of Interior, as well as minor children registered in renunciation document, provided they can certify that they hold Document on Exercise of Rights Granted under Citizenship Law (According to Article 7 of Citizenship Law No. 5901: (1) A child born in or outside Turkey to a citizen mother father within a marriage is considered a citizen.), entire in a country (excluding Republic of Northern Cyprus - TRNC) or in located in countries, reside in there with GCE A-Level exam results, as well as enrolled in colleges or between 2005-2010 obtained or are eligible to obtain GCE A-Level results, or entire in a country hold a TR-YÖS exam result. Applications candidates meeting following conditions be accepted: entire in Turkey or TRNC, (except entire in obtained GCE A-Level results, enrolled in colleges or between 2005-2010 obtained or obtain GCE A-Level results, or entire in a country hold a TR-YÖS result), Dual whose first nationality at birth was (except entire in a country excluding or in abroad), Dual citizens, one of whose nationalities is (except entire in obtained GCE A-Level results, enrolled in colleges or between 2005-2010 obtained or obtain GCE A-Level results, or entire in a country hold a TR-YÖS result), former (including Blue Card holders) nationals enroll in institutions 2022-2023 academic year onward (except studying in embassy schools, international private educational institutions listed in MOBIS system, nationals brought to Turkey under projects conducted by Ministry of National Education), or dual whose first nationality at birth was are studying at affiliated with embassies in Turkey or at in Turkey, Those been expelled a higher institution in Turkey due to disciplinary action. Application Documents Candidates must upload following documents to online application form: Application Form (The online application form must be through university's application system), Original a copy of High School Diploma , Official document school (for candidates yet received diploma) confirming that they graduate before universitys final registration date stated in academic calendar (in English or Turkish). If document is provided or graduation date is after final registration date, registration be cancelled , Transcript (An official document listing courses taken grades received in school, approved by relevant school administration. If in or English, it must be translated into one of these languages certified by a notary or a consulate), A copy of a valid identity document showing citizenship information, National or international school graduation exam results (e.g., Abitur, International Baccalaureate, Tawjihi, etc.). A list of accepted exams required scores is provided in Annex-1 , University entrance exam results (if applicable), such as SAT, ACT, TCS, etc. A list of accepted exams required scores is provided in Annex-2 , One passport-sized photo , Proof of legal stay in Türkiye applicants applying within country ( visa-exempt countries, legal stay period must expired ), Civil Registry Record (Vukuatli Nüfus Kayit Örnegi) dual were born as nationals later acquired citizenship, showing date of citizenship acquisition, along with a Name Equivalency Certificate or Citizenship Acquisition Certificate , Entry-exit records graduated abroad, proving that they were physically present in country while receiving education. Exams Validity Periods (1) High school graduation exams (e.g., General Secondary Education Certification, Abitur, International Baccalaureate, Tawjihi, etc.) no expiration period . (2) University entrance exams (e.g., SAT, ACT, TCS, etc.) are valid two years . Admission Calendar Listed below are application registration deadlines Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University undergraduate programs 2024-2025 Academic Year. Fall Intake Online Application (The results be notified to candidates within 2-4 weeks following submitting a complete application) January - June Deposit Payments The non-refundable deposit must be paid within 2 weeks after conditional acceptance is sent. IZU-YÖS 1 May IZU-YÖS 2 July Enrolment July NOTE: candidates applying inside of Türkiye must complete registration by August at latest. Language Placement / Proficiency Exams Class Adjustments (The student does take these exams or does present a valid language proficiency document is placed in A1 level of relevant Language Preparatory Class). First Week of September (Will be announced in August) Spring Intake Online Application (The results be notified to candidates within 2-4 weeks following submitting a complete application) (The results be notified to candidates within 2-4 weeks following submitting a complete application) July - October IZU-YÖS 3 November Deposit Payments The non-refundable deposit must be paid within 2 weeks after conditional acceptance is sent. Enrolment November - December NOTE: (Candidates applying within country are registered until 15 December at latest). Language Placement / Proficiency Exams Class Adjustments (The student does take these exams or does present a valid language proficiency document is placed in A1 level of relevant Foreign Language Preparatory Class). Second Week of January (Will be announced in December/January)

title: Residence Permit
content: Residence Permit Residence Permit ????? ??????? Ikamet Izni Students enrolled in an academic program in Türkiye three months or longer are obliged to acquire a student per Law on Foreigners International Protection. The Student Residence Permit allows to stay in Türkiye during study period. All international students, regardless of their status, must apply student within a month of their arrival to Türkiye. To do this, need to make an online submit files. Our Office is responsible taking our students' files to Directorate General of Migration Management. Therefore, student must submit their documents to International Office within A WEEK after applying, in a complete accurate manner . Required Documents: Pink file (Legal Transactions File) Four (4) passport-size photographs Two (2) FILLED Declaration Form s Student Application Form obtained official website of Immigration Department with fingerprint criminal number -Copy of passport or equivalent documents (pages containing personal information) - Last entry stamp to Turkey (for first-time applications) - Copy of previous (for extension transfer applications) Student Certificate in Turkish (obtained through Campus System ) Document that shows information where reside: For extension requests: ? Residence document obtained ( E-Government ) For first-time applications: ? If are house owner : A copy of property deed ? If are a tenant : A copy of valid notarized lease agreement ? If residing in a student dormitory/hotel or similar : ? Document proving in a student dormitory (with QR code or "Signature Circulars" document) ? Or a document proving stay in hotel a receipt indicating accommodation payment ? If residing with a sponsor : ? A notarized commitment letter sponsor to student (if sponsor is married, a notarized commitment letter spouse as well) ? A document sponsor obtainable ( E-Government ) ? Birth certificate model sponsor if sponsor holds Turkish citizenship. ? Residence document obtained ( E-Government ) Valid health insurance: ? General health insurance (made within first three (3) months after entering country), ? Or Private Health Insurance covering period. Residence Permit Payment Receipt ( Payment be made online or at Tax Office) Addresses of foreigners UETS: * Passport foreign ID number * GSM number * You obtain it PTT branches after providing a valid email filling out UETS form Foreigners with an electronic signature be able to apply UETS online at https://www.etebligat.gov.tr/ . Any other document that may be requested by Immigration Office. Make sure to bring passport with to International Office. To Provide Fingerprints, Students Must Bring Following Documents:(If Its written in Application Form dont need to obtain it again) Student Application Form obtained website https://e-ikamet.goc.gov.tr/. Copy of passport or equivalent documents (pages containing personal information). Student Certificate - in Turkish (obtained through campus system electronically signed). And go to one of these centers: Directorate General of Migration Management in Istanbul (Istanbul Il Göç Idaresi Müdürlügü) Presidency of Working Group in Esenyurt (Esenyurt Ilçe Çalisma Grubu Baskanligi) Kumkapi Coordination Center (Kumkapi Koordinasyon Merkezi) Presidency of Working Group in Pendik (Pendik Ilçe Çalisma Grubu Baskanligi) Presidency of Working Group in Sultanbeyli (Sultanbeyli Ilçe Çalisma Grubu Baskanligi) Note: Files without fingerprints be separated as incomplete documents, files be returned submission after fingerprints are taken. For Students Under 18 Years Old: For those arriving with visa exemption or a visa a different purpose: Document containing parent/guardian information (birth certificate, family certificate, etc.). Consent letter parent/guardian/legal representative, a commitment letter third party provided by parent/guardian. For those arriving with a student visa: No consent letter or document containing parent/guardian information be required. For Erasmus students, situation is exactly same no different procedures be applied to them. RESIDENCE PERMIT CARD FEE PAYMENT You make payment online via Credit Card by following below instructions: 1. Visit Dijital Vergi Dairesi (gib.gov.tr) 2. Click on  Göç Idaresi Ikamet Tezkeresi Harci Ödeme  button. 3. Type Tahakkuk no (Accrue no) into  Basvuru Numarasi  box then verification code as seen below in  Güvenlik Kodu  box. Accrue no is indicated in online form (1st page). 4. Please download print receipt once are done with payment. If cannot pay online: You must first obtain a tax ID number then pay it at any Tax Office. The personnel might not be able to speak English. Therefore, bring this message to anyone, they help you: Merhaba! Ben Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim Üniversitesinden geliyorum. Yabanci Uyrukluyum. Ikamet kart ücreti ödemeye geldim. Yardiminiz icin tesekkurler. (Hello! I study at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University am a foreigner. I came here to pay my fee. Thank help in advance.) PRELIMINARY INFORMATION HOW TO FILL IN THE PRE-REGISTRATION FORM, ACTIVATION, VERIFICATION, RESIDENCE PERMIT TYPE SELECTION PAGE? HOW TO FILL IN THE PERSONAL INFORMATION PAGE? HOW TO FILL IN THE TRAVEL INFORMATION AND APPLICATION SHEET ON BEHALF OF A FOREIGNER? HOW TO FILL IN ABROAD AND Türkiye CONTACT INFORMATION? HOW TO FILL IN EMPLOYMENT INFORMATION, EDUCATION GRADUATION / CONTINUING, INCOME AND INSURANCE INFORMATION? HOW TO ENTER STUDENT DECLARATION AND RESIDENCE INFORMATION? HOW TO FILL IN THE ONGOING APPLICATION PROCEDURES PAGE? Important Notes: Turkish citizens / blue cardholders do not need to get a permit. An only be made after entering Türkiye registering at university. Residence applications made abroad are invalid. Students cannot leave Türkiye before obtaining document. Departing Türkiye before receiving results in rejection. Students are responsible prolonging their according to their study period if needed. Student passport must be valid at least 60 days beyond requested ending date of permit. Make sure passport is valid six months minimum. If already have a short-term permit, make a transfer application student permit. If are coming another university in Türkiye to Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, must update registration information within 20 days at Kumkapi Migration Coordination. It is essential to maintain accurate current information in university records at all times. This includes promptly updating at Student Affairs whenever any changes occur. Due to high number of applications, we suggest complete online in evening, out of working hours. Please keep a copy of form yourself so that track status. WHEN DO I GET MY RESIDENCE PERMIT CARD? The Immigration Administration is fully authorized to approve or reject to mail to you. The period between processing of receipt of may take up to 30-45 days. Once Immigration Office goes over files approves file, confirmation SMS / email approval be sent to you. After receiving message, be printed posted by Immigration Office. Your is delivered to contact by official carrier, PTT. You must be ready at provided in form to receive card. The mail carrier not hand over to anyone other than you. If postman cannot find at address, PTT return to Immigration Directorate. You see result on http: //e-ikamet.goc.gov.tr/Ikamet/DevamEdenBasvuruGiris only after evaluation of immigration authority. Login to system by entering mobile number or e-mail passport number that used in along with number. The result of evaluation (positive or negative) appear on screen. If is approved, click on "post tracking" link on same page to find out printing status of card. If has not been printed yet, barcode number give an error. If it is printed, follow delivery stage of according to barcode number. To receive card, make sure provide correct on form. After receiving card, please bring it to Student Affairs. Attention: You cannot leave Türkiye without obtaining card. If do so, receive a penalty depending on case; moreover, be cancelled by Immigration Office.

title: Home
content: ABOUT US Health, Culture Sports Department was established with Higher Education Law 46 47 article. The Department of Health, Culture Sports aim to meet social needs of students staff, including; protection of physical mental health, nutrition, recreation leisure time. For this purpose, Department of Health, Culture Sports is responsible for construction operation of canteen, cafeteria, meeting conference rooms, sports facilities, health facilities in proportion to general facilities budget possibilities of university. In this direction, Health, Culture Sports Department targets to make best use of social, cultural sports activities, counseling guidance services for students to conduct research implementation in order to facilitate vital activities of disabled students to support education training at same time. SERVICES . To plan, conduct supervise students, academic administrative staff, health, culture sports services. . To provide services to students in fields of scholarship credit, nutrition, accommodation, work, rest leisure, transportation employment. . To provide follow-up, editing delivery of statistical information to relevant places, . To ensure that available resources such as people materials are used in most appropriate efficient way in order to fulfill service activities economically effectively, . Providing information flow by controlling activities of connected units transmitting results to General Secretary. . Establish, execute develop an effective recording file system within unit. . To prepare annual work programs in cooperation with relevant units, to present General Secretariat, . Ensuring that subordinates understand specified policies implementation of specified activities. . To set goals standards for future with personnel, place, materials other resources assigned to unit for execution, direction evaluation of unit activities, . Providing necessary guidance for subordinates, coordinating subordinates work, helping when necessary. . To examine, evaluate approve recommendations on personnel movements communicated to him by his subordinates. . To present General Secretary to identify tasks, powers responsibilities related to current staff, to identify any changes in special qualifications required to be assigned to these staff positions. . To establish a consistent coordinated communication cooperation arrangement between staff at front staff of other units with organic duties, to present Secretary General for approval. title: Department of Student Affairs
content: Department of Student Affairs ABOUT US The Department of Student Affairs is responsible for; keeping, updating archiving student files, moreover all transactions that are related to within framework of law, regulation general legislation. Student Affairs follows healthy execution of education training, informing staff directing them towards correct units according to their needs. It contributes to increase of institutional qualification by providing correct, fast modern services to students. SERVICES . The registration of who are placed in ÖSYM undergraduate programs of our university, . Registration of who are entitled to enroll in undergraduate programs of University by way of Horizontal Transit, . Registration of international to undergraduate programs at our university, . Registration renewal of students, class registration, dropping of courses and. following processes, . Requesting sending documents of related higher education institutions of coming going from horizontal transfer, . The Preparation of graduation transcripts submission to relevant departments, . Provisional graduation documents, preparation dissemination of diploma diploma documents, . YÖK, ÖSYM, KYK etc. related to education students. sending information documents to other institutions organizations, . Preparation of statistical data reports, student achievement status, credit, scholarship etc. Follow-up of transactions correspondence with related institutions, . Making necessary correspondences with units in university, MISSION The Department of Student Affairs works with our university during course study within framework of relevant laws regulations in shortest time possible. Also works in harmony with other units of University. VISION To be ranked first among units that are acclaimed by our by serving our a superior quality within national international standards.

SERVICES The secretary, who has undertaken an important role in carrying out services correctly effectively within framework of legislation is expected to have full knowledge of administrative legal information that is in their fields; to have maximum attention care during performance of tasks; to have harmonious with colleagues, to become respectful, genial kind in their interpersonal relationships; to make understandable explanations to authorities in related matters; to ensure that transactions made are fully recorded to take necessary precautions for protection of said transctions; to possess characteristics of confidentiality, sense of belonging responsibility; to become careful, hard-working, honest, constantly open to development innovation; to train other staff; to adopt institution culture; to comply with ethical institutional quality rules, work programs strategic plans.

title: ECHE and Policy Statement
content: ECHE Policy Statement Policy Statement As a newly established university in 2010, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) gives a high priority to have a multicultural multilingual campus. Being a part of Programme provides us more potentials in order to achieve campus environment certain level of student staff mobility in order to develop multilateral cooperation, an understanding of compatible European Education Area recent innovations. Acting vision of being an research university, closely follows dynamics has set itself following objectives within Programme: 1-To create an campus environment by contributing to recognition of University at national level: Although is a university can be considered young, it offers a multinational campus environment 20% 11% academics. currently has more than 2200 from 91 countries. We believe participation in Programme contribute to multinational multilingual campus environment. 2-To strengthen in foreign languages as well as increasing numbers of academics quantitatively qualitatively: 27% of study programs at provide in a foreign language, this lead University to increase its ability in cooperating at an level. In addition, we created a common portfolio of courses all can follow, to support in a foreign language. Thanks to these courses University has a more preferable position by stakeholders. 3-To increase number of staff participating in Programme: We are fully aware of importance of mobility for both staff, is why we plan to expand activities in this area. We wish to send host from each of three cycles, but main target for next years are 1st 2nd cycle students. As an internationalizing institution, welcomes staff from all around world. In such a multicultural environment, we are committed to ensure non-discrimination of gender, race, religion, disabled etc., zero tolerance is implemented for discrimination of any kind as of establishment of institution. The Executive Board ensure it stays in way. 4-To partner many institutions aiming to strengthen cooperation relationships diversify them in different geographies: Partnerships established within Programme enable student staff mobility, as well as providing intellectual scientific cooperation between institutions. permanently searches for new partners to catch up new developments in higher education. We believe increased level of quality by working new different partners brings benefits both for institution partners. aims to cooperate at least one university in each Programme Country at least one university from each region in Partner Countries. 5-To interexchange good practices innovation in cooperation partners: intends to increase its internationalization through mobility activities as well as projects within Programme. We aim to attract cooperation projects researchers from across European extend learning opportunities to every place higher is valued. KA-2 projects, which are among future plans, support internationalization of institution as well as its modernization increase in knowledge, quality, innovation capacity. 6-To support personal professional development of staff: One of internationalization objectives is staff acquire personal professional skills through activities. Participation in Programme significantly contribute to foreign language skills future employability of staff. Promoting active citizenship civic engagement, tolerance cultural awareness mobility activities are in line mission values of IZU. 7-To reach European standards continuous improvement in service quality: confirms participating in Programme is part of its modernization internationalization strategy. It acknowledges contribution of student staff mobility as a key to quality of its higher programmes student experience. Our efforts to adapt European Education Area, starting Bologna Declaration, be supported by mobility activities as a part of process. In this context, activities be open for testing developing Programme in order to improve service quality processes. We also take necessary steps for Erasmus Without Paper, Online Learning Agreement Online IIA etc. under European Student Card Initative to digitize implementation processes become more efficient. Objectives mentioned above are guidelines for achieving aims of high standards are in line Internationalization Strategy of IZU. Our modernization efforts, which started process of Bologna Declaration, be supported participation as a beneficiary institution of Programme. The implementation of Programme contribute significantly to targets stated in Strategic Plan of by reaching intercultural communication, increasing visibility by developing cooperation institutions, creating joint projects in all study cycles increasing numbers of exchanging staff.

title: Counseling and Guidance
content: Psychological Counseling Guidance Psychological Counseling Guidance Unit is a unit affiliated to Department of Health, Culture Sports. The counseling service is provided in office which is in Halkali Campus. The Psychological Counseling Guidance Unit has two psychologists working full-time part-time. In Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Psychological Counseling Guidance Unit, purpose is to support students; individual, social, academic professional development as well as to help their adaptation to university dormitory life, to help them gain necessary skills to cope with various problems that may arise during this process. Our Psychological Counseling Guidance Unit helps our students to understand themselves their environment better, helps them to discover their skills to develop their interpersonal communication skills. The academic administrative staff of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University can also receive individual counseling from Psychological Counseling Guidance Unit. All services provided in Psychological Counseling Guidance Unit are free of charge. All activities carried out in unit are based on principle of confidentiality. However, if life of interviewee or someone they know is in danger, necessary people are contacted, student will be informed about contact. The Issues That Can Be Given Help Through Psychological Counselling (The Issues That Students Might Need Support from The Counselling Office)  Adaptation to University and/or Dormitory  Self-Confidence Issues  Test Anxiety  Fears Anxiety  Negativism Trust Issues  Conflicts within Family  Relationship Issues  Depression  Eating Disorders  Substance Abuse  Similar Issues Above If you are facing any of issues above or similar issues, please contact counselling services between 09:30  17:00 Merve ÖZKAN, Psychologist Phone: (0212) 692 9748 E-Mail: ozkan.merve@izu.edu.tr Days Available: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday Huriye ÇEVIK, Clinical Psychologist Office: Health Culture Sports Department Phone: (0212) 692 9748 E-Mail: huriye.cevik@izu.edu.tr Only available on Wednesdays

title: Student Clubs
content: Student Clubs We have 33 student clubs active in IZU. In addition to these clubs, we have a total of 9 Students Community's. Student Clubs and Student Communities have the opportunity to work with a team spirit by organizing many activities in; cultural, social, and sports area both inside and outside the campus. The experience and achievements of our students in these club and society activities, form an important place in their educational lives, as they also contribute positively to their professional skills. During the year, various activities such as; conferences, sports tournaments, workshops and trips suitable for the purpose of the clubs are organized. Clubs and Communities work intensively at student festivals held at the end of the year.

title: President's Message
content: How IZU made me feel like home Being in Istanbul makes feel like are in heart of world, but being at IZU makes heart of world feel like home. Even after only spending a few weeks here, I just know that Erasmus- but especially Erasmus here, has been one of best decisions of my life. Apart from amazing University programs classes, IZU has made most welcoming heartwarming atmosphere for me. Attending classes club programs University has prepared for us gives emphasis on how much they care about diversity of social educational life of their students. No matter what study what your days look like, can always find time get interested into something that is going on in campus. Who would even dare to describe Istanbul all its wonders? If I may, first thing I would say that it is tireless makes fall in love with life even more. It is impossible to get to know it in a short time, it seeks out to wander around. Orientation week gave us a perfect glimpse into adventure that was ahead of us. From mesmerizing cuisine, epic history tradition, cultural sights charming overall energy of city. Being alone in a city that could easily be described as capital of world has not bee lonely even for a moment. Just knowing that have a such an amazing group of friends made here, most wonderful university where are never bored, someone looking out for at all times is a thing that will stick with you- always. Dear IZU, I just got here, I already don't know how I will ever leave Sincerely, Edita Krulin

Especially those of you who have faith in yourself power of vision to build your future. UDG mission is to create conditions studying upon principles of high quality European education. A several generations of graduates who have successfully continued their masters doctoral studies in West or already very successful in companies institutions where they found employment soon after graduation, shows it is possible to achieve this. A number of them, help of UDG opened private companies during their studies, many considering to do so. One of important advantages of UDG is it is developing multidisciplinarity. Of course, fact they in a modern building, all together, of ten undergraduate study programs, about 25 postgraduate 3 PhD programs, or close to 2,000 students, allows synergistic friction of various occupations professions. Many foreign teachers managers praise UDG studies because they not massive studies. Indeed, studies oriented to student as an individual, a student whose character we building through individual communication group work. In addition to investments in fundamental technical knowledge, much is invested in skills of students.
