title: ECHE and Policy Statement
content: ECHE Policy Statement Policy Statement As a newly established university in 2010, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) gives a high priority to have a multicultural multilingual campus. Being a part of Programme provides us more potentials in order to achieve campus environment certain level of student staff mobility in order to develop multilateral cooperation, an understanding of compatible European Education Area recent innovations. Acting vision of being an research university, closely follows dynamics has set itself following objectives within Programme: 1-To create an campus environment by contributing to recognition of University at national level: Although is a university can be considered young, it offers a multinational campus environment 20% 11% academics. currently has more than 2200 from 91 countries. We believe participation in Programme contribute to multinational multilingual campus environment. 2-To strengthen in foreign languages as well as increasing numbers of academics quantitatively qualitatively: 27% of study programs at provide in a foreign language, this lead University to increase its ability in cooperating at an level. In addition, we created a common portfolio of courses all can follow, to support in a foreign language. Thanks to these courses University has a more preferable position by stakeholders. 3-To increase number of staff participating in Programme: We are fully aware of importance of mobility for both staff, is why we plan to expand activities in this area. We wish to send host from each of three cycles, but main target for next years are 1st 2nd cycle students. As an internationalizing institution, welcomes staff from all around world. In such a multicultural environment, we are committed to ensure non-discrimination of gender, race, religion, disabled etc., zero tolerance is implemented for discrimination of any kind as of establishment of institution. The Executive Board ensure it stays in way. 4-To partner many institutions aiming to strengthen cooperation relationships diversify them in different geographies: Partnerships established within Programme enable student staff mobility, as well as providing intellectual scientific cooperation between institutions. permanently searches for new partners to catch up new developments in higher education. We believe increased level of quality by working new different partners brings benefits both for institution partners. aims to cooperate at least one university in each Programme Country at least one university from each region in Partner Countries. 5-To interexchange good practices innovation in cooperation partners: intends to increase its internationalization through mobility activities as well as projects within Programme. We aim to attract cooperation projects researchers from across European extend learning opportunities to every place higher is valued. KA-2 projects, which are among future plans, support internationalization of institution as well as its modernization increase in knowledge, quality, innovation capacity. 6-To support personal professional development of staff: One of internationalization objectives is staff acquire personal professional skills through activities. Participation in Programme significantly contribute to foreign language skills future employability of staff. Promoting active citizenship civic engagement, tolerance cultural awareness mobility activities are in line mission values of IZU. 7-To reach European standards continuous improvement in service quality: confirms participating in Programme is part of its modernization internationalization strategy. It acknowledges contribution of student staff mobility as a key to quality of its higher programmes student experience. Our efforts to adapt European Education Area, starting Bologna Declaration, be supported by mobility activities as a part of process. In this context, activities be open for testing developing Programme in order to improve service quality processes. We also take necessary steps for Erasmus Without Paper, Online Learning Agreement Online IIA etc. under European Student Card Initative to digitize implementation processes become more efficient. Objectives mentioned above are guidelines for achieving aims of high standards are in line Internationalization Strategy of IZU. Our modernization efforts, which started process of Bologna Declaration, be supported participation as a beneficiary institution of Programme. The implementation of Programme contribute significantly to targets stated in Strategic Plan of by reaching intercultural communication, increasing visibility by developing cooperation institutions, creating joint projects in all study cycles increasing numbers of exchanging staff.

title: Education in Turkey
content: Education in It is highly recommended foreigners who wish to different reasons, should overview related about our countrys visa procedures in advance before arrival. Visa procedure touristic business purposed visits to a country may differ in relative to work education visits. Also Turkeys visa policies may change country to country according to reciprocity principle. Some applicants may be exempted getting a visa to if it is touristic or business purposes. Others obtain touristic Visa through electronic Visa. All other applicants who want to obtain a visa working education purposes will need to apply to our Representations abroad. According to different countries, an note about Turkeys visa regime is available in following address: www.mfa.gov.tr Foreigners who wish to enter need to carry a visa, a visa exemption, date of when resident permit duration ends. They will need to request a passport or documentations shows a passport is valid atleast 60 days. Exampe: A Foreign visitor owns a visa has 90 days resident permit duration, they will need to 150 days (90 days + 60 days/ 5 month) valid passport or a travel document at time of entrance in Turkey. Passports must a validity of 6 months atleast expiration date of visa. They 90 days to do so. For more visa information, please Ministry of Foreign Affairs official website: http://www.mfa.gov.tr/sub.en.mfa?cc4e437c-6769-4d79-9017-10b63c651224 For more regarding residence permit, please National Polices official website: http://www.egm.gov.tr/EN/Pages/Residence-Permit-Procedures.aspx Please keep in mind university assist residence permit procedures. You do not to do it alone, help is there. Many Universities in provide campus accommodation to all international who wish to stay on campus or in state dormitories. Meals generally not included in fee but dormitories canteens where eat breakfast other meals a nominal, relatively low cost. All fees fixed in Liras. Regardless of nationality, all international must pay same amount as students. In addition, most dormitories segregated by sex. It is advised bring enough money be easily converted to Lira. Credit cards may not be accpeted. In Turkey, all processes related to accommodation in state dormitories carried out by General Directorate of Higher Education Credit Hostels Institution. For detailed information, please www.kyk.gov.tr web page. If looking a room share, may find vacancies posted in hostels, language schools, or other similiar areas regarding accommodation. In addition to other sources, also a chance to find an apartment in universitys campus. All international who recieving education in take advantage of general insurance coverage in Turkey. Students who "general insurance", use all state hospitals without any fee, use private hospitals by paying a moderate fee. To benefit General Health Insurance System in Turkey, go to schools international Student Office to get more information. Then necessary documents, go to Social Security Institution to apply. When complete application, will pay general insurance fee which is determined on an annual basis within 1 month date of Social Security records. If don't apply general insurance system within given time period (3 months) cannot benefit general insurance services of State. But, if has an international insurance they use it or get benefit private insurances in Turkey. Some universities may also offer special insurance policy international students. Please also bear in mind is known providing high quality services at low costs. For this reason many many patients neighbour countries as well as developed western countries prefer to come to to get healthier cheaper treatments surgery, dental, eye examinations. Students benefit insurance in Turkey, in order to not face any problems we recommend to go checkups, dentists.We also recommend to bring along glasses, a spare pair of contact lenses optical prescription them. Lastly bringing along a first-aid kit be really useful. is home to worlds earliest settlements numerous civilizations ranging smallest of communities to greatest of empires. is cradle of cultures civilizations connecting Europe Asia also capital of civilizations reined lands of Anatolia centuries. Since beginning of history, Anatolia, well known as one of earliest settlements, has continued to flourish migration of various tribes, accumulated a large cultural heritage through a line of succeeding empires civilizations. Many empires ranging Sumerians to Hittites, Lydians to Byzantines Seljuks to Ottomans once thrived expired within borders of Turkey. To get more about holiday cities of to decide which cities to in Turkey, please official tourism portal of http://www.goturkeytourism.com Mediterranean Aegean climates a cool moist winter season, a warm rainless summer season. climate in Aegean region of is great throughout year, even during winter. weather in eastern region of Turkey, bordering Georgia Iraq, has hot summers sonwy icy winters. Turkeys climate weather along coast of Black Sea is a continental climate. In Turkey, humidity levels be quite high. During winter, especially in months of December January, weather is cold temperatures drop below zero. weather in Turkeys capital, Ankara, as well as central Anatolian region is cold in winters hot dry during summer. Turkeys central climate is known as a mixture between a continental climate a steppe climate. To get more on Turkeys weather please State Meteorological Services official website: http://www.mgm.gov.tr/ has been homeland to numerous communities civilizations beginning of history of mankind Anatolia has been harbor of magnificent culture more hundreds of years. Due to geographical continents connects Europe Asia, culture has been evolving influence of all these civilizations. Anatolia has been cradle of different civilizations throughout history effects of these cultures also reflect cuisine social life. If curious about; customs, traditions, folk dances, theatre, cinema, fine arts, literature, traditional modern music,please official tourism portal of http://www.goturkeytourism.com One of best things about culture is variety of its cuisine! You will find not only food, but also many delicious samples of food western eastern kitchen cuisine. Eating habits been shaped according to prevalent cultural, geographical, ecological, economic characteristics is shaped according to historical duration. In summary, variety of products offered by lands of Asia Anatolia, interaction numerous different cultures over a long historical process, new tastes developed in palace kitchens of Seljuk Ottoman empires all played a part in shaping new our culinary culture, which would very much enjoy. For more about traditional Ottoman cuisine more, please official website of Ministry of Culture Tourism: http://www.kultur.gov.tr/EN,35306/culinary-culture.html These afew popular native foods in Turkey: 1- Kebap 2- Simit 3- Yogurt Ayran 4- Kahvalti (Balli-Kaymakli) 5- Lokum 6- Baklava 7- Findik Fistik (Kuruyemisler) 8- Meyveler 9- Pide 10- Lahmacun 11- Tursu 12- Maras Dondurmasi 13- Manti 14- Dolma  Sarma 15- Kuru Fasulye 16- Kestane Sekeri / Kebab 17- Tavukgögsü Tatlisi 18- Türk Kahvesi  Lokumu 19- Börek 20- Sahlep (Vahsi orkide köklerinden kremali  tarçinli mucize içecek) 21- Asure 22- Boza monetary unit is Lira (TL). Travelers checks not favored nor accepted in Turkey. ATMs be found in even smallest towns. Most accept international credit cards or bank cards (a strip of logos is usually displayed above ATM). Almost all ATMs a language key to enable to read instructions in English. Most commercial establishments accept international credit cards such as Visa, Master Card American Express. banking systems highly developed, easily find a bank ATM anywhere. You make payments at any ATMs by using credit cards. You open an account on name, to get an ATM card need a resident document. value-added tax, is known as KDV, is 18%. Value-added tax is nearly always included in quoted prices. Certain shops deduct tax, therefore need to ask wheter any deductions be made. electrical current in is 220 volts, 50 cycles alternating current (AC); wall outlets take Continental-type plugs, two or three round prongs. Jan 1: First Day of New Year Apr 23: National Sovereignty Children's Day (anniversary of establishment of Grand National Assembly in 1920) May 1: Labour Solidarity Day (since 2009) May 19: Atatürk Commemoration Youth & Sports Day (the arrival of Atatürk in Samsun in 1919, beginning of War of Independence) Aug 30: Victory Day (victory over invading forces in 1922). Oct 29: Republic Day (anniversary of declaration of Republic in 1923). Ramazan Bayrami: Three-day festival is celebrated end of fast of Ramadan month. Also known as "Seker (sweets) Bayrami" because it's customary to offer candies to family members friends come to visit. Kurban Bayrami: Four-day festival where sheeps or cows sacrificed meat distributed to poor. dates of these religious festivals change according to Islamic calendar thus occur 10-11 days earlier each year. (This is due to 10 days 21 hrs difference between Gregorian Lunar calendars). According to this; Standard time zone: UTC/GMT + 3 hours To make a long-distance call (any call outside of area code), to dial long-distance prefix (0) before seven-digit local phone number. Below area codes Turkey´s major cities: Adana 322 Ankara 312 Antalya 242 Bursa 224 Istanbul (Asian side) 216 Istanbul (European side) 212 Izmir 232 Each mobile provider assigned a range of area codes (or mobile prefixes). Turkcell 530-539, Vodafone 541-549, Turk Telekom 502-506 Important Note: You exactly 120 days of duration to use mobile phones bring along you. Before this duration ends, will need to go to a GSM Operator passport in order to register mobile device. This operation will cost 140TL, or 38 $. If purchase device do not to do this process. Useful telephone service numbers: 155 Police 112 Ambulance 110 Firefighter 170 Tourism Info Planning travel to is exciting, however before going to Turkey, may find tips below useful: Documents need to bring along you: passport, a valid visa; travel documentations, tickets a acceptance letter university. (The address of new university, plus telephone number of person related will be helpful to you). Health travel insurance: Firstly, get advice university  they may offer a special insurance policy students. Asking different insurance companies also be helpful. For about vaccinations other health-related considerations, check out our section. Do not forget most airlines charge excess baggage. Temperatures in vary season to season region to region. For about Turkeys weather, check our Weather section.

title: Before Mobility
content: Before Mobility Students who successfully complete their can start their preparations to come to IZU. Documents to be Provided Before Mobility 1-Letter of Acceptance Acceptance letter indicating that have been accepted to IZU in relevant academic semester will be automatically uploaded to system when is approved. If need original of letter of acceptance for visa application, must notify this situation by sending an e-mail to erasmus.incoming@izu.edu.tr after has been approved. Your original letter will be mailed to address specified in form. Postage time varies from 10 days to 30 days. 2-Learning Agreement for Studies - Before Mobility After selecting courses from "Course Catalog" section, Learning Agreement signed by "Student" and "Home University Official" will be signed by IZU and will be uploaded to system. Erasmus students in IZU can take courses from any faculty/institute or department. 3-Application Form Once is complete, must download form from system. Required Documents During Registration 1-Application Form You must bring form during registration when is completed. 2-Letter of Acceptance You must bring Letter of Acceptance that have downloaded from system during registration. 3-Learning Agreement 4-Passport The original of passport, which have uploaded at time of application, must be with at time of registration. 5-Passport Photo (3 pieces) In order to print student ID and transportation card, 3 photographs are needed during registration. Visa Application Students who are eligible for Erasmus+ Student Mobility for Studies, if necessary should apply for their visa before mobility. For visa procedures, it is recommended that apply for a visa 6 to 8 weeks before mobility start date. For visa exempted citizens, we recommend students to enter country as a tourist and after registration at IZU, students can apply for a short-term residence permit. title: How to Apply
content: How to Apply? Information E-mail After nomination is submitted you receive an information e-mail with web link to access your online application. Online Application Now you can fill in application. See deadlines in Calendar section. Please watch out for deadline for applications, as late applications not be accepted. To fill in online application you need unique link that you received by e-mail. Letter of Acceptance After you upload all your application documents, your Learning Agreement (LA) be sent for approval to department. As soon as your LA is approved we send it to you by e-mail together with Acceptance Letter (students who require a visa to enter Turkey additionally receive other important documents  original documents to apply for visa be send by mail). Then you have to wait for our instruction e-mails which we start sending in late July / late November.

title: Who can Apply
content: Who can Apply? If you wish to study at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University under the Erasmus+ Program, first check with your home university to see if it has an agreement with a particular faculty or department at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. Then you should follow the nomination selection process at your home university. Nomination Only students who are nominated by the host university may apply to our university. Your home coordinator has to nominate you online (without nomination you cannot access the online application).

title: Mobility for Studies
content: Mobility for Studies The aim of Erasmus+ is to contribute to the Europe 2020 strategy for growth, jobs, social equity and inclusion, as well as the aims of ET2020, the EU's strategic framework for education and training. In order to contribute to the Europe 2020 strategy, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University will provide you the experience of studying at a historical campus, which was established as an imperial agricultural school in 1848. Study at an international environment with more than 10.000 students of whom 15% are international students from over 70 countries around the world.

On other hand, thanks to central location of campus its proximity to public transport, you will soon be able to reach everywhere you want. For students, open closed sports complexes offer courses in sports such as golf, basketball, fitness, football, volleyball, taekwondo, tennis, shovel, handball, wrestling, traditional olympic archery, in which has many national titles championships. For a healthy stress free student life, courses such as zumba, pilates other events being held throughout year. Social Spots Social Spots Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, located in one of most central regions of Istanbul, has social spaces in which can socialize spend quality time on campus. There conference seminar halls in campus where all kinds of culture arts events can be arranged. Approximately 600 programs organized throughout year by 30 active student clubs in university. Built on a land of 650 acres, can study in shadow of centuries-old plane trees enjoy time friends. Health Health Health Unit was established to carry out personal health issues of employees in a rigorous confidential manner within framework of patient rights. health services of university carried out by directly contacting health center personnel being examined free of charge referred to health institutions when necessary. Providing medical intervention care emergency medical problems to administrative/academic staff daily policlinic services, directing patients to full-fledged hospitals further examination treatment when necessary, is one of main objectives of Health Unit. Library Library new Central Library has been built. Central Library is 13.500 m2, room 1500 researchers has a capacity 1 million books, which makes it biggest library in Istanbul one of biggest in Turkey. Central Library is heart of IZU, continues to maintain legacy of Mehmet Akif Ersoy all scholars lore passengers. library, which provides great convenience to academicians university who get access to information, has become one of IZU's indispensable places thanks to its comfortable working environment. Career Office Career Office Career Center was established to help build career awareness, develop interests skills make career plans first year they enter IZU. Our Career Center aims to educate guide leading graduates high employability in country in world. As you work engage in your lessons, your children enjoys a qualitative education. It enables to better understand themselves surroundings, discover abilities develop interpersonal communication skills. All work done in unit is based on privacy principle. Council Council Council is a formal unit linked to Rector's Office that is not affiliated any kind of interst/influence, taking into consideration student's rights in meeting developing educational, health, sports cultural needs of students. At Sabahattin Zaim University in Istanbul, elected student representatives come together to form faculty student representatives' boards Council. To further develop conditions of university, to conduct research evaluation education, research solution in student services, to present results to relevant departments only a few of aims of these boards. title: Staff from Abroad
content: Erasmus+ Staff Mobility Activities With Erasmus+, opportunities are available to spend time teaching at an education institution abroad. These opportunities are available to both staff working in the education sector and to individuals working outside the sector invited to share their knowledge and experience. Training periods abroad can consist of job shadowing, observation periods or specific training courses abroad. title: What's Erasmus+
content: is EU's programme to support education, training, sport in Europe. Its budget of 14.7 billion will provide opportunities over 4 million Europeans to study, train, gain experience, volunteer abroad. Video TR NATIONAL AGENCY The Center European Union Education Youth Programmes It runs Erasmus + Program, one of European Commission's community programs. It actively supports work, both in country abroad, including in workgroups networks organized by European Commission other national agencies as well as other international organizations. Website Contact Mevlana Bulvari No.181 06520 Balgat - ANKARA www.ua.gov.tr T: +90 (312) 409 60 00 F: +90 (312) 409 60 09 bilgi@ua.gov.tr @ulusalajans Logos Program Ministry of Foreign Affairs National Agency Activities The activities supported under Program are basically under "3 Key Actions" "2 Special Actions": Target groups: In scope of education, teaching students (students, interns, apprentice, volunteers youth) staff (teachers, administrative, academicians, trainers. personnel, personnel working at companies/establishments active at activities) Purposes: to improve work personal skills, a better chance in employment to improve English language skills of participants giving them opportunity to obtain an international network to improve capacities of to strengthen relationship between formal informal to develop cooperation between vocational business world to encourage entrepeneurship Sample Activities: on-the-job training, observation, internship teaching, training exchange, European Voluntary Service Target Groups: In scope of education, teaching public organizations non goverment organizations private sector organizations Purposes: to develop, transfer or implement innovative applications at institutional, local, regional, national or international level to ensure recognition validity of acquired knowledge, skills competences through formal informal learning to strengthen cooperation between local authorities, support active citizenship Sample Activities: activities promoting development, transfer or implementation of innovative applications transnational activities to promote entrepreneurship skills to promote active citizenship intersectoral cooperation long-term partnerships Target Groups: In scope of education, teaching public organizations non-goverment organizations private sector organizations Purposes: to develop innovative policies needed in fields of involvement of all stakeholders, especially young people, in policy development processes Sample Activities: meetings between young people decision-makers collaborations that develop dialogue with non-EU countries partnerships sharing of innovative practices in policy development processes The Jean Monnet Program aims to support work at level of higher in field of European integration. Supported Activities: Jean Monnet Academic Modules Jean Monnet Lectures Jean Monnet Excellence Centers Jean Monnet Support Institutions, Organizations Associations Jean Monnet Networks Jean Monnet Projects Sport funding aims to contribute to systematic development of sports fields in Europe. Supported Activities: Cooperation Partnerships Not-For-Profit Sports Events Small Cooperation Partnerships Target Groups School Education offers teachers other educational staff opportunity to join teaching assignments, job shadowing, structured courses or training events abroad. also provide school to participate in Strategic Partnerships. Vocational offers vocational students staff to study, train or gain Professional experience abroad. also provide vocational to participate in Strategic Partnerships. Higher Education offers undergraduate postgraduate students opportunity to study, train or academic, administrative staff to work, train. also provide higher to participate in Strategic Partnerships. Adult Education offers adult staff to join teaching assignments, job shadowing, structured courses or training events abroad. also provide adult to participate in Strategic Partnerships. Youth offers a wide range of opportunities young people to expand their horizons by acquiring new skills through apprenticeships, volunteering exchanges. also provide organizations to participate in Strategic Partnerships. WITH Breaking boundaries together! The Erasmus + Program encourages participation disadvantaged participants' in program. The Erasmus + Program contributes to additional costs with guidance, welcoming, physical access, pedagogical technical support participants with physical, mental or other disabilities. SPECIAL NEEDS AND COMPANION SUPPORT Grant support is provided special needs of disabled participants. 100% of eligible costs are covered under grant support special needs. In addition to support additional grants, support is provided persons with disabilities their companions in guidance, pedagogical technical assistance, reception, physical access.

content: HOW TO APPLY TO IZU Please click to apply to IZU immediately get detailed information. International Student Exam Click Here For Detailed Information About International Student Exam Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability) ERASMUS Project 26.07.2024 Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability) ERASMUS Project With support of National Agency coordinated by Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education, multiplication meeting ERASMUS Project titled "Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability)" with project number 2020-1-TR01-KA226-SCH-098336, in which Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is a partner, took place on 05.09.2023. Academic staff from our university, project partners from abroad, teachers from Ministry of National Education attended multiplication meeting. The goal of project is to develop a digitally supported educational platform students aged 6-9 diagnosed with special learning disabilities. The multiplication meeting was conducted both face-to-face simultaneously online. The opening speeches were made by our Vice Rector, Prof. Dr. Ibrahim GÜNEY. Following this, Mr. Özer TOPLU, coordinator on behalf of Provincial Directorate of National Education, provided information about project introduction results. Then, our university faculty member Dr. Nüket AFAT gave a presentation on target audience of project, children diagnosed with special learning disabilities. The last presentation before noon was given by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Aras BOZKURT from Anadolu University, who introduced digital platform prepared children with special learning disabilities. In afternoon sessions, Romanian team, one of project partners, presented on teacher training professional development. Following this, Bulgarian team gave a presentation titled "Self-Esteem Socialization" within scope of children with special needs. Finally, Portuguese team shared satisfaction surveys of students with special learning disabilities who tested digital platform during Transnational Project Meeting held in Portugal. After presenting certificates to project partners participants, multiplication meeting concluded. title: Apply to IZU
content: Apply to IZU Application Requirements Applications candidates graduated school or are in final year meet following criteria be considered: Foreign nationals, Those were nationals but later acquired citizenship, Those were born as but later renounced citizenship with permission Ministry of Interior, as well as minor children registered in renunciation document, provided they can certify that they hold Document on Exercise of Rights Granted under Citizenship Law (According to Article 7 of Citizenship Law No. 5901: (1) A child born in or outside Turkey to a citizen mother father within a marriage is considered a citizen.), entire in a country (excluding Republic of Northern Cyprus - TRNC) or in located in countries, reside in there with GCE A-Level exam results, as well as enrolled in colleges or between 2005-2010 obtained or are eligible to obtain GCE A-Level results, or entire in a country hold a TR-YÖS exam result. Applications candidates meeting following conditions be accepted: entire in Turkey or TRNC, (except entire in obtained GCE A-Level results, enrolled in colleges or between 2005-2010 obtained or obtain GCE A-Level results, or entire in a country hold a TR-YÖS result), Dual whose first nationality at birth was (except entire in a country excluding or in abroad), Dual citizens, one of whose nationalities is (except entire in obtained GCE A-Level results, enrolled in colleges or between 2005-2010 obtained or obtain GCE A-Level results, or entire in a country hold a TR-YÖS result), former (including Blue Card holders) nationals enroll in institutions 2022-2023 academic year onward (except studying in embassy schools, international private educational institutions listed in MOBIS system, nationals brought to Turkey under projects conducted by Ministry of National Education), or dual whose first nationality at birth was are studying at affiliated with embassies in Turkey or at in Turkey, Those been expelled a higher institution in Turkey due to disciplinary action. Application Documents Candidates must upload following documents to online application form: Application Form (The online application form must be through university's application system), Original a copy of High School Diploma , Official document school (for candidates yet received diploma) confirming that they graduate before universitys final registration date stated in academic calendar (in English or Turkish). If document is provided or graduation date is after final registration date, registration be cancelled , Transcript (An official document listing courses taken grades received in school, approved by relevant school administration. If in or English, it must be translated into one of these languages certified by a notary or a consulate), A copy of a valid identity document showing citizenship information, National or international school graduation exam results (e.g., Abitur, International Baccalaureate, Tawjihi, etc.). A list of accepted exams required scores is provided in Annex-1 , University entrance exam results (if applicable), such as SAT, ACT, TCS, etc. A list of accepted exams required scores is provided in Annex-2 , One passport-sized photo , Proof of legal stay in Türkiye applicants applying within country ( visa-exempt countries, legal stay period must expired ), Civil Registry Record (Vukuatli Nüfus Kayit Örnegi) dual were born as nationals later acquired citizenship, showing date of citizenship acquisition, along with a Name Equivalency Certificate or Citizenship Acquisition Certificate , Entry-exit records graduated abroad, proving that they were physically present in country while receiving education. Exams Validity Periods (1) High school graduation exams (e.g., General Secondary Education Certification, Abitur, International Baccalaureate, Tawjihi, etc.) no expiration period . (2) University entrance exams (e.g., SAT, ACT, TCS, etc.) are valid two years . Admission Calendar Listed below are application registration deadlines Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University undergraduate programs 2024-2025 Academic Year. Fall Intake Online Application (The results be notified to candidates within 2-4 weeks following submitting a complete application) January - June Deposit Payments The non-refundable deposit must be paid within 2 weeks after conditional acceptance is sent. IZU-YÖS 1 May IZU-YÖS 2 July Enrolment July NOTE: candidates applying inside of Türkiye must complete registration by August at latest. Language Placement / Proficiency Exams Class Adjustments (The student does take these exams or does present a valid language proficiency document is placed in A1 level of relevant Language Preparatory Class). First Week of September (Will be announced in August) Spring Intake Online Application (The results be notified to candidates within 2-4 weeks following submitting a complete application) (The results be notified to candidates within 2-4 weeks following submitting a complete application) July - October IZU-YÖS 3 November Deposit Payments The non-refundable deposit must be paid within 2 weeks after conditional acceptance is sent. Enrolment November - December NOTE: (Candidates applying within country are registered until 15 December at latest). Language Placement / Proficiency Exams Class Adjustments (The student does take these exams or does present a valid language proficiency document is placed in A1 level of relevant Foreign Language Preparatory Class). Second Week of January (Will be announced in December/January)

title: Residence Permit
content: Residence Permit Residence Permit ????? ??????? Ikamet Izni Students enrolled in an academic program in Türkiye three months or longer are obliged to acquire a student per Law on Foreigners International Protection. The Student Residence Permit allows to stay in Türkiye during study period. All international students, regardless of their status, must apply student within a month of their arrival to Türkiye. To do this, need to make an online submit files. Our Office is responsible taking our students' files to Directorate General of Migration Management. Therefore, student must submit their documents to International Office within A WEEK after applying, in a complete accurate manner . Required Documents: Pink file (Legal Transactions File) Four (4) passport-size photographs Two (2) FILLED Declaration Form s Student Application Form obtained official website of Immigration Department with fingerprint criminal number -Copy of passport or equivalent documents (pages containing personal information) - Last entry stamp to Turkey (for first-time applications) - Copy of previous (for extension transfer applications) Student Certificate in Turkish (obtained through Campus System ) Document that shows information where reside: For extension requests: ? Residence document obtained ( E-Government ) For first-time applications: ? If are house owner : A copy of property deed ? If are a tenant : A copy of valid notarized lease agreement ? If residing in a student dormitory/hotel or similar : ? Document proving in a student dormitory (with QR code or "Signature Circulars" document) ? Or a document proving stay in hotel a receipt indicating accommodation payment ? If residing with a sponsor : ? A notarized commitment letter sponsor to student (if sponsor is married, a notarized commitment letter spouse as well) ? A document sponsor obtainable ( E-Government ) ? Birth certificate model sponsor if sponsor holds Turkish citizenship. ? Residence document obtained ( E-Government ) Valid health insurance: ? General health insurance (made within first three (3) months after entering country), ? Or Private Health Insurance covering period. Residence Permit Payment Receipt ( Payment be made online or at Tax Office) Addresses of foreigners UETS: * Passport foreign ID number * GSM number * You obtain it PTT branches after providing a valid email filling out UETS form Foreigners with an electronic signature be able to apply UETS online at https://www.etebligat.gov.tr/ . Any other document that may be requested by Immigration Office. Make sure to bring passport with to International Office. To Provide Fingerprints, Students Must Bring Following Documents:(If Its written in Application Form dont need to obtain it again) Student Application Form obtained website https://e-ikamet.goc.gov.tr/. Copy of passport or equivalent documents (pages containing personal information). Student Certificate - in Turkish (obtained through campus system electronically signed). And go to one of these centers: Directorate General of Migration Management in Istanbul (Istanbul Il Göç Idaresi Müdürlügü) Presidency of Working Group in Esenyurt (Esenyurt Ilçe Çalisma Grubu Baskanligi) Kumkapi Coordination Center (Kumkapi Koordinasyon Merkezi) Presidency of Working Group in Pendik (Pendik Ilçe Çalisma Grubu Baskanligi) Presidency of Working Group in Sultanbeyli (Sultanbeyli Ilçe Çalisma Grubu Baskanligi) Note: Files without fingerprints be separated as incomplete documents, files be returned submission after fingerprints are taken. For Students Under 18 Years Old: For those arriving with visa exemption or a visa a different purpose: Document containing parent/guardian information (birth certificate, family certificate, etc.). Consent letter parent/guardian/legal representative, a commitment letter third party provided by parent/guardian. For those arriving with a student visa: No consent letter or document containing parent/guardian information be required. For Erasmus students, situation is exactly same no different procedures be applied to them. RESIDENCE PERMIT CARD FEE PAYMENT You make payment online via Credit Card by following below instructions: 1. Visit Dijital Vergi Dairesi (gib.gov.tr) 2. Click on  Göç Idaresi Ikamet Tezkeresi Harci Ödeme  button. 3. Type Tahakkuk no (Accrue no) into  Basvuru Numarasi  box then verification code as seen below in  Güvenlik Kodu  box. Accrue no is indicated in online form (1st page). 4. Please download print receipt once are done with payment. If cannot pay online: You must first obtain a tax ID number then pay it at any Tax Office. The personnel might not be able to speak English. Therefore, bring this message to anyone, they help you: Merhaba! Ben Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim Üniversitesinden geliyorum. Yabanci Uyrukluyum. Ikamet kart ücreti ödemeye geldim. Yardiminiz icin tesekkurler. (Hello! I study at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University am a foreigner. I came here to pay my fee. Thank help in advance.) PRELIMINARY INFORMATION HOW TO FILL IN THE PRE-REGISTRATION FORM, ACTIVATION, VERIFICATION, RESIDENCE PERMIT TYPE SELECTION PAGE? HOW TO FILL IN THE PERSONAL INFORMATION PAGE? HOW TO FILL IN THE TRAVEL INFORMATION AND APPLICATION SHEET ON BEHALF OF A FOREIGNER? HOW TO FILL IN ABROAD AND Türkiye CONTACT INFORMATION? HOW TO FILL IN EMPLOYMENT INFORMATION, EDUCATION GRADUATION / CONTINUING, INCOME AND INSURANCE INFORMATION? HOW TO ENTER STUDENT DECLARATION AND RESIDENCE INFORMATION? HOW TO FILL IN THE ONGOING APPLICATION PROCEDURES PAGE? Important Notes: Turkish citizens / blue cardholders do not need to get a permit. An only be made after entering Türkiye registering at university. Residence applications made abroad are invalid. Students cannot leave Türkiye before obtaining document. Departing Türkiye before receiving results in rejection. Students are responsible prolonging their according to their study period if needed. Student passport must be valid at least 60 days beyond requested ending date of permit. Make sure passport is valid six months minimum. If already have a short-term permit, make a transfer application student permit. If are coming another university in Türkiye to Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, must update registration information within 20 days at Kumkapi Migration Coordination. It is essential to maintain accurate current information in university records at all times. This includes promptly updating at Student Affairs whenever any changes occur. Due to high number of applications, we suggest complete online in evening, out of working hours. Please keep a copy of form yourself so that track status. WHEN DO I GET MY RESIDENCE PERMIT CARD? The Immigration Administration is fully authorized to approve or reject to mail to you. The period between processing of receipt of may take up to 30-45 days. Once Immigration Office goes over files approves file, confirmation SMS / email approval be sent to you. After receiving message, be printed posted by Immigration Office. Your is delivered to contact by official carrier, PTT. You must be ready at provided in form to receive card. The mail carrier not hand over to anyone other than you. If postman cannot find at address, PTT return to Immigration Directorate. You see result on http: //e-ikamet.goc.gov.tr/Ikamet/DevamEdenBasvuruGiris only after evaluation of immigration authority. Login to system by entering mobile number or e-mail passport number that used in along with number. The result of evaluation (positive or negative) appear on screen. If is approved, click on "post tracking" link on same page to find out printing status of card. If has not been printed yet, barcode number give an error. If it is printed, follow delivery stage of according to barcode number. To receive card, make sure provide correct on form. After receiving card, please bring it to Student Affairs. Attention: You cannot leave Türkiye without obtaining card. If do so, receive a penalty depending on case; moreover, be cancelled by Immigration Office.

title: University-Industry Collaboration Meeting Held at IZÜ
content: University-Industry Collaboration Meeting Held at IZÜ 26.02.2025 IZÜ Hosted executives from Istanbul Chamber of Industry (ISO) and prominent figures from business world to srengthen university-industry collaboration. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ) hosted a significant meeting aimed at strengthening university-industry collaboration. The brakfast event was attented by Istanbul Chamber of Industry (ISO) Chairman Erdal Bahçivan, ISO Assembly President Ender Yilmaz, and several board members from both ISO and Istanbul Chamber of Industry Foundation (ISOV), along with prominent business figuers. Also present at meeting were Ilim Yayma Foundation President Bilal Erdogan, foundation executives, IZÜ Board of Trustees Chairman Özkan Göksal, Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, as well as IZÜ Secratary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir. The meeting emphasized that universities missions of producing scientific knowledge and training qualified professionals should align with needs of industry. Participants highlighted critical role of closer collaboration between industrial sector and universities in facilitating realization of innovative projects. It was also stated that meeting laid groundwork for new partnerships that will further strengthen interaction between industry and academia.

title: IZÜ Sports Assembly Held Its First Meeting
content: IZÜ Sports Assembly Held Its First Meeting 19.03.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University held first meeting of its Sports Assembly, which was established to encourage student participation in sports management. During meeting, attended by sports ambassadors, students requests, universitys current sports activities, and new projects were discussed. Following first meeting, assembly is expected to begin active work, and sports ambassadors are anticipated to represent student from their departments, making sports activities more effective. The IZÜ Sports Assembly provides students with opportunity to directly contribute to decision-making process in sports and to shape universitys sports culture together.

title: The IZÜ Family Came Together for Iftar
content: The IZÜ Family Came Together for Iftar 26.03.2025 In our iftar event organized to share the blessings and unity of the month of Ramadan, our academic and administrative staff, along with their valuable families, came together. Our Board Trustees Chairman Özkan Göksal, Board of Trustees Member Bekir Sakib Sahinoglu, Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, and Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, along with TÜBA President Prof. Dr. Muzaffer Seker and council members whom we hosted today on our campus, participated in the program where we shared the spiritual atmosphere of the month of Ramadan together. What remains from this meaningful night are beautiful memories and valuable moments spent together. We look forward to gathering again around many more beautiful tables

title: Academic Thesis Supervision Principals
content: ISTANBUL SABAHATTIN ZAIM UNIVERSITY GRADUATE EDUCATION INSTITUTE ACADEMIC THESIS SUPERVISION PRINCIPALS knowledge transfer research results in higher education are closely related to production of a Master's Doctorate thesis. In that sense, one of fundamental responsibilities of graduate students supervisors at a university is to guide properly students during research writing process, make output turn into a product. Supervision is to contribute not only to transfer of research results but also at same time to create an education modelling in name of supervisor-student relation. That is why in field, whether in masters or doctorate, creating a successful thesis, supervision is of crucial importance. Moreover, supervision is highly contributing both to education system professional personal development of owner of thesis. Thus, selection of supervision period is extremely important because with knowledge, experience, feedback, vision, supervisors help, becomes a pathfinder, a guide to students to search write a successful thesis. Regarding, it is vital to abide by principles about supervision below success of theses written in departments related to our institute. is primarily responsible being a pathfinder a guide in processes which are required a successful product by determining subject of thesis, conducting research, collecting analysing data, writing in accordance with Institute's writing rules. subject supervisors professional field be relevant. have enough competence to guide students research have enough knowledge of subject. put attention to students from beginning of consider students interests expectations. subject title be determined through a discussion between student, also by examining studies in Turkish Higher Education Council (YÖK) National Thesis Centre ( https://tez.yok.gov.tr/UlusalTezMerkezi/ ). subject title be short, precise, clear, understandable, different from other already done theses. titles Turkish English versions be coherent. Translations be in an language, not Google translate. guide, lead, help with background of research subject. be transparent, consistent, fair, neutral in practices. be a guide helper to in grading, analysing, interpreting data gained through research. is responsible quality of content, discussion, results of thesis. spare enough time student, answer his/her questions or demands constructively, be a pathfinder, contribute to his/her personal development. respect students ideas, do empathy, lead up to expedite students personal competency development. check whether it is written according to Institute's writing rules or not. If not, then facilitate to correct in compliance with writing rules. guide students to make learning process easier. make students improve critical-analytical thinking skills. make students develop independent research competence, critical-analytical thinking competence, help to gain an intellectual perspective. be sure that has conformed to IZU Thesis Project Writing Rules in writing process of thesis. go over which gets in a defence process through IZU Thesis Project Writing Rules scientific ethical principles, send an e-mail to Graduate Education Institute at least 15 workdays afore from anticipated defence day final draft control determination of average plagiarism. guide about controlling unit's feedback if there is, ensure that requested corrections or plagiarisms are edited by student. be extremely careful that grade point average (GPA) is at least 2.5/4 before entering to defence especially in Graduate (Master). After acceptance of control unit, be in collaboration with head of department, relevant administrative staff, about official date, place, jury of defence. be sure that corrects minor post-defence corrections through approved final draft by control unit do final controls of successful in required legal process its graduation. If there occurs a defence extension, at least 15 workdays afore, resend to determine final structural control plagiarism ratio with Graduate Education Institute control unit after approval, specify official defence date. In some processes, know her/his condition feel that she/he is led by can communicate with him/her easily. Before delivering thesis, head of department approve by doing last supervision/should fill final control form submit form with to Institute. Whether a master or doctorate thesis, facilitate to read defence processes on web site cautiously before delivering theses. CLICK FOR MASTER'S / DOCTORAL THESIS DEFENSE PROCESSES.

title: About 2023-2024 Fall Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule
content: About 2023-2024 Fall Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule 17.10.2023 Students who ask to apply for doctoral qualifying exam to be held in fall semester of 2023-2024 academic year are required to fill out Doctoral Qualifying application form at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login between October 30th-November 3rd, 2023 . Doctoral Qualifying Exams will be held on a date to be determined by relevant Head of Departments in December 2023 . The exams will be held at our Halkali campus. POINTS TO BE CONSIDERED 1- Students who have successfully completed their courses and seminar, have a weighted grade point average of at least 3.00 and above, and fulfill Foreign Language Proficiency can apply for doctoral qualifying exam. 2- Students admitted with a master's degree must take doctoral qualifying exam until end of fifth semester at latest in compliance with relevant regulations. 3- Students who ask to take Doctoral Qualifying Examination, must have a thesis supervisor and choose from Doctoral Qualifying Examination section at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/Login 4- Students who applied for Doctoral Qualifying Examination held in spring semester of 2022-2023 academic year and did not take exam or failed exam, are required to apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be considered unsuccessful and will be disenrolled from University. 5- Please click here for post-exam procedures of students who are successful in doctoral qualifying exam.

title: IMPORTANT ANNOUNCEMENT FOR THESIS MASTER'S STUDENTS WHOSE NORMAL-MAXIMUM STUDY PERIOD WILL EXPIRE
content: IMPORTANT ANNOUNCEMENT FOR THESIS MASTER'S STUDENTS WHOSE NORMAL-MAXIMUM STUDY PERIOD WILL EXPIRE 26.10.2023 In Master's programs of our Institute, supervisors of students, who have completed normal study period of four semesters or a maximum study period of six semesters (except Scientific Preparation, Suspension of Registration, Additional Time) as of end of Fall Semester of 2023-2024 academic year, are required to submit word version of to Control Team by end of working hours on Friday, at latest, December 22, 2023. Theses submitted after December 22 , 2023, will not be accepted. The defense forms of masters students who will graduate in Fall semester be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, January 05, 2024 . Students in this situation meet publication requirement before January 05, 2024 . The master students who will graduate in Fall semester take defense exam by Friday, February 09, 2024 . Deadline Submission to Control December 22, 2023 Deadline Submission of Defense Documents January 05, 2024 Publication Requirement Deadline January 05, 2024 Deadline Defense Exams February 09, 2024 Requirements Completion of Master's Students have at least seven courses one Seminar course (a total of 60 ECTS) together with a minimum GPA of 2.50 to take defense exam. In accordance with decision of IZU Senate dated 18.09.2017 numbered 2017/21; Students enrolled in master's doctoral programs within Graduate Education Institute as of spring semester of 2017-2018 academic year fulfill publication requirement. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) In master's program, stated determined by student together with supervisor be stated in . Checking Compliance of Master's with Plagiarism Writing Rules The primary responsibility preparation of in accordance with Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. The written in accordance with Writing Guidelines of Graduate Education Institute be sent by student to supervisor via e-mail. The supervisor send student's to relevant Control Officers via e-mail. Click here e-mail addresses of Control Officers. Theses that do not comply with Writing Guideline and/or those similarity reports exceeding 20% be corrected submitted within five days. The Control Team Members will not control more than three times. All responsibility in this regard belongs to supervisor student. It be noted that singular similarities not exceed 1% in plagiarism report. Supervisors submit documents of master students who will graduate to Graduate Education Institute via ÜBYS system. External advisors are required to submit documents of master students to Head of Department. Click here procedures that students advisors should do after Control Team approval. Maximum Period of Study Failure Students, who are not able to complete their until December 22, 2023, within their normal study period of four semesters extend their submission to Spring Semester of 2023-2024 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is valid Fall Semester of 2024-2025 Academic Year, second extension period. Students, who have failed in study or have not been able to take defense exam within maximum study period of six semesters, will be dismissed from University. According to 1st 2nd paragraphs of Article 9 of IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; (1) The duration of master's program with is four semesters, starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, program is completed in a maximum of six semesters. (2) The student who has failed to complete credit courses one Seminar course or has not been able to fulfill success criteria determined by University, who has failed in study or has not been able to take defense exam within maximum period of study will be disenrolled from University.

title: Submission Dates for Fall 2023-2024 Non-Thesis Master's Projects
content: Submission Dates Fall 2023-2024 Non-Thesis Master's Projects 26.10.2023 Non-thesis master's students who complete their study period by end of Fall Semester of 2023-2024 academic year have their supervisors submit in Word format to Graduate Education Institute by end of working hours on Friday, December 22, 2023. Late submissions will not be accepted. Non-thesis masters students who will graduate in Fall semester have Head of Department submit plagiarism report and plagiarism petition to Graduate Education Institute by latest Friday, January 26, 2024. Non-thesis masters students who will graduate in Fall semester submit their projects by Friday, January 26, 2024. Deadline Project Submission to Thesis Control December 22, 2023 Deadline Submission of Project Documents January 26, 2024 Deadline Project Submission January 26, 2024 Requirements Completion of Master's Project The students enrolled in non-thesis master's programs have at least ten courses (a total of 75 ECTS) together with a minimum GPA of 2.50 to graduate. In non-thesis master's program, projects title determined by student together with supervisor be stated in . Checking Compliance of Master's Project with Plagiarism and Thesis Writing Rules The student and supervisor have primary responsibility ensuring that projects are prepared in accordance with Graduate Education Institute Project Writing Guideline. The supervisors email to Thesis Control Team Members. Click here Thesis Control e-mail addresses. Projects that do not comply with Project Writing Guideline and/or those similarity reports exceeding 20% be corrected and resubmitted within five days. The Thesis Control Team Members will not control more than three times. All responsibility in this regard belongs to advisor and student. It be noted that singular similarities not exceed 1% in plagiarism report. The supervisors submit documents of non-thesis Masters students who will graduate to Graduate Education Institute via ÜBYS system. External thesis advisors are required to submit documents of master students to Head of Department. In Case of Project Cannot Be Completed and Failure Students who fail study or do not submit within three semesters will be dismissed from University. According to 1st paragraph of Article 14 of IZU Graduate Education and Training Regulations; (1) The duration of master's program with non-thesis is at least two semesters starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, and program is completed in at most three semesters,

title: Procedures to Transfer From The Program Without Thesis to the Program With Thesis
content: Procedures to Transfer From Program Without Thesis to Program With Thesis 1. Applications to transfer a masters without to a must be made within one week date final exam grades for fall semester are announced, and within one month date of announcement of final exam grades for spring semester . 2. A wishes to transfer to a must study at least one semester in non-thesis in which he is enrolled and must successfully complete all courses taken during relevant semester . 3. Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of courses taken in non-thesis in which is enrolled must be at least 2.50 out of 4.00 . (CGPA) can be increased recommendation of relevant and approval of Institute Board . 4. wants to transfer to must get at least 55 points Academic Personnel and Postgraduate Education Entrance Exam (ALES ). ALES scores are valid for 5 years. ALES score can be increased recommendation of relevant Department and approval of Institute Board. ALES 5. In order to transfer to programs where language of instruction is a foreign language, students must provide satisfactory language score documents taken Foreign Language Examination (YDS) or Higher Education Council Foreign Language Examination (YÖKDIL) or an equivalent score foreign language exam score accepted by ÖSYM or Foreign Language Proficiency by IZU, which is accepted by relevant . It is obligatory to get required score of B2 or 70/100 exam. validity period of foreign language exam to be held by IZU is two years . 6. time spent in previous of a is transferred a non-thesis to a is included in calculation of period of study . time spent in previous of is transferred to non-thesis is included in calculation of education period, but has finished his education period is given an additional semester to complete his courses and his project . 7. Students have transferred a to a non-thesis cannot transfer to a again . 8. A transferred a non-thesis to a must fulfil publication requirement of in order to graduate . publication can be one of following, provided that it is related to subject or program: A paper presented at national or international scientific meetings, An article published in a refereed/indexed journal, accepted for publication or a DOI number, A paper presented at symposiums organized by IZU in fields of Social sciences and Science. 9. transfers to must pay difference between fees of Master's Programs Thesis and Non-Thesis, determined by Board of Trustees for year in which s/he transfers to thesis. No refund is made to transfers a to a non-thesis program.

title: Important Announcement is for Ph.D. Students who are in the Thesis Period and whose Normal-Maximum Study Period will Expire
content: Important Announcement is Ph.D. Students in Period whose Normal-Maximum Study Period Expire 26.10.2023 In programs of our Institute, supervisors of students, have completed normal study period of eight semesters or a maximum study period of twelve semesters (except Scientific Preparation, Suspension of Registration, Additional Time) as of end of Fall semester of 2023-2024 academic year, required to send Word version of to Control Team by end of working hours on Friday, at latest, December 22, 2023. Theses submitted after December 22, 2023, be accepted. defense forms of students graduate in Fall semester be referred by Head of Department to Graduate Education until January 05, 2024, Friday . Students in this situation meet publication requirement before January 05, 2024 . students graduate in Fall semester take defense exam by Friday, February 09, 2024. Deadline Submission to Control December 22, 2023 Deadline Submission of Documents January 05, 2024 Publication Requirement Deadline January 05, 2024 Deadline Defense Exams February 09, 2024 Requirements Completion of a Students have at least seven courses one Seminar course (60 ECTS), a Ph.D. qualifying exam (30 ECTS), a proposal (30 ECTS) (a total of 120 ECTS) together a minimum CGPA of 3.00 to qualify to take dissertation exam. Students enrolled in programs fulfil at least one of publication requirements. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) Students, have submitted their proposal to Graduate Education through their supervisors on time, be able to take defense exam even if they have completed their thesis. Checking Compliance of Completed Plagiarism Writing Rules primary responsibility preparation of in accordance Writing Guidelines belongs to supervisor. Control Team members responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. written in accordance Graduate Education writing guide should be sent by to his/her advisor via e-mail. supervisor e-mail student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. theses that do comply Writing Guideline and/or those similarity reports exceeding 20% be corrected submitted within five days. Control Team Members control more than three times. All responsibility in this regard belongs to supervisor student. It be noted that singular similarities exceed 1% in plagiarism report. Supervisors submit documents of students graduate to Graduate Education via ÜBYS system. External advisors required to submit documents of students to Head of Department. Click here procedures that students advisors should do after Control Unit approval. Failure to Complete Maximum Period of Study in case of Failure Students, able to complete their until December 22, 2023, within their normal study period of eight semesters extend their submission to Spring semester of 2023-2024 academic year, required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is valid Spring Semester of 2024-2025 Academic Year, second extension period. students, have failed in study or have been able to take defense exam within maximum study period of twelve semesters, be disenrolled from University. According to IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; completion of ARTICLE 24  (1) A is expected to write her in accordance Graduate Education Writing Guideline defend in front of members. (2) submit to supervisor before defense. If receive corrections, she complete any requested corrections submit revised to supervisor before defense exam. supervisor is required to submit plagiarism report to Institute, along a written opinion that is defensible written in accordance Graduate Education Writing Guideline. If a is found to contain a significant degree of plagiarism, is sent to Board a decision, along excuse plagiarism. (3) At least three successful monitoring committee reports be submitted at intervals of no more than six months in order a to be completed. If monitoring committees held on time, be considered to have failed monitoring committee that term. (4) examination is appointed recommendation of supervisor Head of Department approval of Board. examination consists of five members, including advisor, three from monitoring committee, at least two from outside university. If supervisor is affiliated another university, examination be composed of one member from outside university four member from within university. Board decides whether advisor has a right to vote on examination jury. In case that advisor has a right to vote, examination consist of six members. second advisor may participate in without having a right to vote. (5) members meet within at least 15 days at most one month after is submitted to them. defense exam a presentation of a QA session. defense exams open to participation of listeners from faculty, postgraduate students, experts in field. (6) Following defense exam, members deliberate in a closed session to decide whether to accept, reject, or revise thesis. whose is accepted by examination considered to have passed. This decision is reported to by Head of Department in a written report within three days of defense. whose is rejected is dismissed from university. whose is revised is given up to six months to revise their thesis, after which they defend revised before same examination jury. whose revised is accepted is dismissed from university. Students admitted to programs a bachelor's degree unsuccessful in their awarded a non-thesis Master's degree, as stated in first paragraph of Article 16, upon request.

title: To the Attention of Students Who are in the Stage of Doctoral Dissertation Monitoring/Proposal
content: To Attention of Students Who are in Stage of Doctoral Dissertation Monitoring/Proposal 26.10.2023 defenses/dissertation meetings for Fall semester of 2023-2024 academic year must be held by end of working hours on Friday, at latest, December 29, 2023. Students who miss deadline will be considered unsuccessful in their proposals/dissertation stage. thesis documents of doctoral students in Fall semester must be submitted to Graduate Education Institute by Head of Department by Friday, January 05, 2024 , at latest. POINTS TO CONSIDER meets at least twice a year, once each between January-June July-December. submits a written report to members at least one month before meeting date. In this report, summary of studies carried out so far study plan to be carried out in next period are indicated. student's study is determined by as successful or unsuccessful. A who is found unsuccessful two times in a row or three times intermittently by is dismissed from University. thesis report minutes must be submitted by thesis supervisors to Graduate Education Institute with approval of Head of Department through ÜBYS system. supervisors from outside IZU, must contact relevant Head of Department to submit documents of their students. Doctoral students who have successfully completed courses, seminars, at least three meetings within their deadlines, have a weighted grade point average of 3.0 above, meet publication requirement can take defense exam. content: 2023-2024 Spring Semester Normal-Maximum Tuition Period Will Expire Important Announcement PhD Students 29.02.2024 In doctoral programs of our Institute , supervisors of students, have completed standard study period of eight semesters or a maximum study period of twelve semesters (excluding Scientific Preparation, Suspension of Registration, Additional Time) by end of Spring Semester of 2023-2024 academic year, are required to submit Word version of to Control Team by end of office hours on Friday, May 10, 2024, at latest. Theses submitted after May 10, 2024, will be accepted. defense forms of doctoral students will graduate in spring semester be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, May 31, 2024 . Students in this situation fulfill publication requirement before May 31, 2024. Doctoral students will graduate in spring semester take defense by Friday, July 5, 2024. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) primary responsibility preparation of in accordance Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. supervisor e-mail student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. theses that do comply Writing Guideline and/or those similarity reports exceed 20% be corrected submitted within five days. Control Team Members will control more than three times. All responsibility in this regard belongs to supervisor student. It be noted that singular similarities exceed 1% in plagiarism report. Supervisors submit documents of doctoral students will graduate to Graduate Education Institute via UBYS system. students, have submitted their proposal to Graduate Education Institute through their supervisors on time, will be able to take defense even if they have completed their thesis. students, are able to complete their until May 10, 2024, within their normal study period of eight semesters extend their submission to Fall Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (2) Before defense of doctoral in theses given correction, student completes correction submits it to advisor. advisor presents plagiarism report to institute a written opinion that is defensible has been written in accordance institute writing guide. In case of a real plagiarism in data in report, is sent to institute board of directors to be decided together its justification. (3) In order student's to be finalized, three successful monitoring committee reports be submitted at least every six months. If monitoring is done on time, student is deemed unsuccessful from monitoring result of that period. (4) Doctoral jury is appointed recommendation of advisor head of department approval of institute board of directors. jury consists of five faculty members, including three lecturers on student's monitoring committee including advisor., at least two of them from outside University If advisor is from another higher education institution, jury consist of one faculty member outside university four faculty members inside university. institute board of directors decides whether consultant has right to vote. If advisor does have right to vote, jury consists of six faculty members. In addition, second supervisor can take part in jury without right to vote. (5) members of jury gather at earliest fifteen days at latest one month from date is delivered to them, takes student to defense. defense consists of presentation of work question-answer section that follows. defense meetings are open to participation of lecturers, graduate students experts from field. This decision is notified to institute by department within three days following exam. student whose is unsuccessful rejected is dismissed from University. student, got a correction advice on his thesis, makes necessary corrections within six months at latest defends his before same jury. student fails in this defense is dismissed from University. title: Directive For the Acceptance of Scientific Articles as a Doctoral Thesis
content: Directive For Acceptance of Scientific Articles as a Doctoral Thesis T.R. ISTANBUL SABAHATTIN ZAIM UNIVERSITY DIRECTIVE FOR THE ACCEPTANCE OF SCIENTIFIC ARTICLES AS A DOCTORAL THESIS Goal Article 1 (1) This directive includes application procedures of publications to take place of doctoral dissertations for Ph. D. degree, as well as criteria of article published in one of following indexed categories such as SSCI, AHCI according to IZU regulation for Academic Appointment and Promotion. The directive also aims to encourage production of quality papers from doctorate dissertation with name of university and increase number of citations. Basics Article 2 (1) A doctoral student can submit at least three publications that s/he has prepared as a doctoral dissertation to be accepted instead of a doctoral dissertation . At least two of these publications in physical field and at least one in Social Sciences must have published in internationally recognized indexes such as SCI, SSCI, AHCI or have been accepted for publication (with a DOI number). These studies must be prepared during doctoral studies. (2) Dissertation study is expected to have a significant contribution to research and writing in publications. The contribution in publication in question must belong to student and student must be first author. (3) The name of dissertation supervisor can take place in publications as second author. (4) The publication (s) used in one dissertation cannot be used in another dissertation. (5) Dissertation Evaluation Committee decides on academic competence of three articles submitted instead of doctoral thesis. Application Article 4 (1) The student applies to Institute together with articles he/she has prepared and a letter from dissertation supervisor stating student's contribution to preparation and writing of articles. Operation Article 5 (1) This directive gets into effect when it is signed. Execution Article 6 (1) The provisions of this directive are executed by head of relevant department and director of Institute.
