title: The Owners of TÜBITAK Projects Were Honored
content: The Owners of TÜBITAK Projects Were Honored 28.04.2025 In the 2209-A University Students Research Projects Support Program, conducted by TÜBITAK's Scientist Support Programs Directorate (BIDEB), 13 out of 28 project applications from IZU were approved for support. At the ceremony attended by our Chairman of the Board of Trustees Özkan Göksal, Board of Trustees Member Idris Turan Ilter, Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, and Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, our academics and students whose projects were accepted were honored. During the program organized by the IZU Technology Transfer Office (TTO), congratulatory cards were presented to the project owners by the university administration.

title: The 1st International Congress on the Science of Teaching and Learning Has Begun
content: The 1st International Congress on Science of Teaching Learning Has Begun 28.04.2025 The "1st International Congress on Science of Teaching Learning," organized by IZU Center for Development Research of Teaching Learning (IZU ÖGEM), has begun at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The congress was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Faculty of Education Vice Dean ÖGEM Director Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kâmil Arif Kirkiç, as well as academics from both domestic international institutions. In his opening speech, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar drew attention to risks of artificial intelligence digitalization, emphasizing need to approach learning processes from multiple perspectives. Rector Acar stated: "Along with different technologies, risks have also emerged regarding personal development. With advent of artificial intelligence, we can no longer be sure to what extent scientific research is genuinely conducted by researchers themselves. Especially worldwide, youth unemployment rates are at least twice as high as adult unemployment rates. Smart technologies will also accompany our mental potential. It may become even harder to find jobs. Therefore, continuing our work with current methods in field of education does not seem feasible. Especially in this digital age, we must consider every possibility when it comes to learning." THE CONGRESS WILL CONTINUE ONLINE OVER THE WEEKEND The first speaker of day, Prof. Dr. Münevver Çetin, highlighted effects of digital transformation in higher education in her presentation titled "Digital Ecosystem in Higher Education: The Changing Role of Academics New Generation Teaching Methods." Prof. Dr. Tuncay Dilci, in his talk on "Problematic Use of Digital Objects," shared insights into conscious use of digital tools. Prof. Dr. Selçuk Besir Demir, in his presentation titled "Mistakes Solution Suggestions in Publishing Writing Process in SSCI-SCI Journals," shed light on academic publishing process. In parallel sessions held simultaneously, participants from various universities presented academic studies related to teaching learning processes. The congress will continue with online sessions on April 1920.

title: Cultural and Diplomatic Visits by Rector Acar in Indonesia
content: Cultural and Diplomatic Visits by Rector Acar in Indonesia 05.05.2025 As part of the Turkish Universities Education Fair held in Indonesia, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, piad a visit to the Yunus Emre Institute. During his meeting with officials from the Institute, prof. Dr. Acar discussed the enhancement of cultural and academic cooperation between Türkiye and Indonesia. Continuing his visit, the IZÜ Rector proceeded to the Turkish Embassy in Jakarta, where he met with the Ambassador of the Republic of Türkiye to Jakarta, Prof. Dr. Talip Küçükcan. The meeting included evaluations concerning Indonesian students pursuing their education in Türkiye.

title: IZÜ Hosts the Leaders of the Future
content: IZÜ Hosts the Leaders of the Future 05.05.2025 The MUN (Model United Nations) event, whose opening session was held at Basaksehir Mehmet Emin Saraç Anatolian Imam Hatip High School, is continuing at the IZÜ Campus. In the program, which began with an opening speech by our Vice Rector, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içer, high school students from both Türkiye and abroad are successfully participating in a simulation of the United Nations. Working in various committees such as the Organization of Turkic States, the Syrian Cabinet, and the Historical Crisis Committee, students are gaining valuable knowledge and experience in diplomacy, negotiation, and global affairs at an early age.

title: The Music Teacher Training School Commemorated at IZÜ on Its 100th Anniversary
content: Teacher Training School Commemorated at IZÜ on Its 100th Anniversary 05.05.2025 Teacher Training School Commemorated at IZÜ on Its 100th Anniversary Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ) commemorated centennial anniversary of Musiki Muallim Mektebi one of foundational institutions of Turkish music education through organization of 1st National Workshop on Teacher Training School in Its 100th Year. Held at Nizamülmülk Conference Hall, event brought together prominent academics, musicians, music educators. workshops honorary committee included IZÜ Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Ibrahim Güney, Ismail Küçük, Erhan Içener. executive committee was chaired by Süleyman Erguner. Among notable names on honorary board were Ali Uçan, Honorary President of Educators Association (MÜZED), Amir Ates, President of Üsküdar Society. Acar: Universities Have a Vital Role in Preservation of Turkish Joining event live from Indonesia, IZÜ Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized important role that universities play in preserving developing Turkish music. He stated: Especially in realm of Turkish music its subfields, there are many responsibilities that fall to us as universities. Musiki Muallim Mektebi began its activities as a pioneering institution in Republican era. As a university, we will continue to support contribute to this legacy. Güney: Our University Places Great Importance on Arts Speaking at opening of workshop, Vice Rector Dean of Faculty of Education Ibrahim Güney, a member of honorary board, referenced universitys history its decision four years ago to establish a Department of Music. He noted: This year, we are pleased to be graduating our first cohort. As a university, we place great emphasis on education in arts. Our music department is a vital part of this vision. Erguner: Turkish Carries a 3,000-Year-Old Cultural Heritage Süleyman Erguner, Chair of Department of Fine Arts Education Head of Education Division at IZÜ, emphasized significance of Musiki Muallim Mektebi . Stressing that music teaching is not merely a profession but also a means of cultural transmission, he remarked: Music teachers help reveal potential within our children. Not every child will become a musician, but every child can develop a love appreciation for music. This is immensely valuable for progress of society. Turkish music carries legacy of a cultural tradition dating back 3,000 years encompassing musical heritage of Huns, Göktürks, Seljuks, Ottomans, Republic of Türkiye. Nationwide Academic Participation organizing committee included respected scholars from various institutions: Ibrahim Güney Süleyman Erguner (IZÜ), Ali Uçan (MÜZED), Cenk Güray (Hacettepe University), Erhan Özden (Ankara University of Fine Arts), Evren Bilge Kutlay (IZÜ), Gözde Çolakoglu Sari (Istanbul Technical University), Hakan Cevher, Mustafa Uslu (Marmara University), Ugur Alpagut (MÜZED), Ugur Türkmen (Çanakkale Onsekiz Mart University), Lecturer artist Süleyman Senel (ITU), Bedirhan Büyükduman (IZÜ), who also served as vice-chair of organizing committee. Seven Thematic Sessions on Education workshop consisted of seven sessions addressing topics such as historical development of music education, its current status, instrumental training, teacher preparation process, curriculum challenges. A session comparing music education programs in Türkiye to those abroad highlighted issues of merit, curriculum design, instructional materials. Key current topics such as national entrance exam threshold (TYT) impact of National Education Academy on student quotas were discussed in fourth session. fifth session focused on role quality of instrumental instruction in teacher training. final session provided a comprehensive evaluation of all previous discussions outlined framework for final report. Scientific Committee Ensured Academic Rigor scientific committee, comprising leading scholars such as Ibrahim Güney, Süleyman Erguner, Evren Bilge Kutlay, Bedirhan Büyükduman (IZÜ), Gözde Çolakoglu Sari Lecturer Süleyman Senel (ITU), Ali Uçan Ugur Alpagut (MÜZED), Erhan Özden (Ankara University of Fine Arts), Cenk Güray (Hacettepe University), Mustafa Uslu (Marmara University), Ugur Türkmen (Çanakkale Onsekiz Mart University), Hakan Cevher, played a key role in ensuring academic depth interdisciplinary breadth of event. To enrich spiritual atmosphere of workshop, IZÜ Choir performed all ten stanzas of Turkish National Anthem live, accompanied by original composition by Osman Zeki Üngör.

title: The Parliament Simulation Was Just Like the Real Thing
content: The Parliament Simulation Was Just Like Real Thing 07.05.2025 Parliament Simulation at Zaim Was Just Like Real Thing Organized by our Law Club, Zaim Parliament Simulation was held in Nizamülmülk Conference Hall. The event brought together eager to experience legislative processes of Grand National Assembly of Türkiye. During program, took on role of members of parliament, proposing bills engaging with legislative process. Held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, simulation provided participants with a parliament experience that felt incredibly real. Among attendees were AK Party Istanbul Deputy Av. Nurettin Alan, President of Bar Association No. 2 Av. Yasin Samli, IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, IZU Faculty of Law Vice Dean Dr. Halit Serhan Ercivelek, along with many professionals from fields of law, academia, politics. ALAN: PARLIAMENT IS THE CENTER OF CURRENT POLITICS In his opening speech, AK Party Istanbul Deputy Av. Nurettin Alan stated that should become "legal engineers" who actively shape legislation. He continued: Simulations like these dont just help you learn existing laws they help you become individuals who discuss, improve, guide future legislation. Parliament is also center of current politics. Everything that concerns society is discussed there. In fact, many of headlines you see in news come from parliament. Historically, we are children of a glorious civilization. However, this civilization has recently been obscured. You, youth, are ones who will blow truth into ashes reignite its fire. SAMLI: LAW IS NOT JUST LEGISLATION IT IS JUSTICE Bar President Av. Yasin Samli recalled past changes in Turkish Penal Code: At one point, even peaceful protests were tried as attempts to overthrow constitutional order. Even high school demanding right to education were prosecuted with potential death sentences. It wasnt easy to correct these injustices. Thats why understanding essence of law focusing on justice is so crucial. Samli emphasized that law should not be seen as limited to written rules: A regulation can be legal but still unfair. Law, at its core, is what is just. Law is a scale. IÇENER: WE MUST RAISE INDIVIDUALS WHO PRODUCE KNOWLEDGE, NOT JUST CONSUME IT IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener highlighted value of simulation-based learning: These simulations allow to learn not only through theory but also through role-playing, position-taking, direct debate. When actively engage, learning becomes much more lasting. He added, This level of interest gives me hope. Todays youth are no longer satisfied with just studying they question ideologies, world systems, values of civilization. One foot must stay rooted in our land, other must explore world. Thats how a global perspective is formed. Though AI, social media, rapid information tools save time, they can weaken mental depth. We expect our to be not just consumers of knowledge, but also its producers. ERCIVELEK: OUR PARLIAMENTARY TRADITION GOES BACK TO CENTRAL ASIA Dr. Halit Serhan Ercivelek, Vice Dean of IZU Faculty of Law, spoke on historical roots of parliament concept: When we talk about parliament, we usually refer to Western examples. But long before that, in Turkish states of Central Asia, there was a tradition of kurultay assemblies that convened twice a year to elect rulers decide on wars. This is a value rooted in our cultural memory. Parliament is not just an institution it represents collective thinking decision-making. STUDENTS EXPERIENCED THE LEGISLATIVE PROCESS Chaired by Güzide Arslan, a third-year Law student president of Law Club, Zaim Parliament Simulation included workshops, party meetings, committee discussions, general assembly sessions. These activities allowed to gain a deeper understanding of legislative processes. The event helped young participants develop skills in expression, debate, critical thinking on social issues.

title: The Golden Age of Islamic Civilization Discussed at IZU
content: The Golden Age of Islamic Civilization Discussed at IZU 07.05.2025 The Golden Age of Islamic Civilization Discussed at IZU The "Andalusian Panel," jointly organized by IZU History Club, Miskatla Geçerken Student Community, Halil Inalcik Center for Historical Social Studies, was held at Nizamülmülk Conference Hall. The panel was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Agirakça, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Cumhur Ersin Adigüzel, as well as academics students. The event began with a recitation from Qur'an, followed by an opening speech from Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar. In his remarks, he drew attention to challenges currently faced by Muslim societies, using historical legacy of Al-Andalus as a point of reflection. Prof. Dr. Acar stated: The Islamic state that existed in Andalusia for 800 years played a vital role not only in fields of science culture but also in shaping Western civilization. However, today, traces of this cultural heritage have largely disappeared from Andalusian lands. This tragic ending should serve as a lesson. The current approach of Western world toward Muslim communities in both Turkey Europe is not much different from historical policies toward Andalusia. The genocide in Bosnia attempts to reshape Islam in Albania are examples of this. We must view our differences as strengths unite around shared values. History should be a source of insight, painful experiences of past must not be forgotten. Andalusia is not just a memory of past; it is a legacy that reminds us of our responsibilities for future. SPEECHES BY AGIRAKÇA AND ADIGÜZEL Prof. Dr. Ahmet Agirakça, in his presentation titled Physicians of Al-Andalus, discussed contributions of Andalusian doctors to both Islamic world Western medicine. Assoc. Prof. Dr. Cumhur Ersin Adigüzel, in his talk titled Al-Andalus as a Cradle of Islamic Civilization, examined political, cultural, intellectual structure of Andalusia.

title: Prof. Dr. Yusuf Dinç Appointed as Consultant to UN Islamic Fund
content: Prof. Dr. Yusuf Dinç Appointed as Consultant to UN Islamic Fund 07.05.2025 IZU faculty member Prof. Dr. Yusuf Dinç has been appointed as a consultant to the IOM Islamic Philanthropy Fund, established within the United Nations International Organization for Migration (IOM), which will carry out work in the field of zakat and sadaqah for the benefit of disadvantaged individuals. Prof. Dinç, who has contributed to the fund since its project phase, will undertake a consultancy role focused on ensuring that the funds activities and operations comply with Islamic finance standards and on improving donor relations. We wish our professor success in his new role.

title: "Golden Inspiration Awards Found Their Winners
content: "Golden Inspiration Awards Found Their Winners 07.05.2025 Golden Inspiration Awards Found Their Winners Our university brought together outstanding individuals with inspiring success stories at 2025 Golden Inspiration Awards ceremony. Held at Abdullah Tivnikli Hall, event was organized as part of ÜNIDES project in cooperation with IZU Center for Management, Entrepreneurship Leadership Studies, Department of Health, Culture Sports (SKS), Entrepreneurship Club. The ceremony was hosted by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük. In his opening speech, Rector Acar emphasized that entrepreneurship is not only an economic but also a strategic social power. He said: Entrepreneurship is not just about producing goods services, but also about taking risks to add value to society. The entrepreneurs awarded here today not only shape business world but also contribute to our countrys economic independence. Entrepreneurs carry income, livelihood, future of everyone they employ on their shoulders. Supporting them is not just an economic, but also a moral responsibility. Entrepreneurship is key to a strong future. AWARDS WERE PRESENTED TO REPRESENTATIVES FROM VARIOUS SECTORS INCLUDING BUSINESS, SPORTS, ART, AND TECHNOLOGY At Golden Inspiration Awards, inspiring figures from many fields such as business, media, sports, art were honored. The Businessperson of Year Award was given to MÜSIAD President Mahmut Asmali. The Journalist of Year Award went to CNN Türk Special News Chief Fulya Öztürk. Ömer Nart, CEO of WORLD EF, received Entrepreneur of Year Award, while national taekwondo athlete Kübra Dagli was awarded Athlete of Year. In media category, News Channel of Year Award was presented to TVNET received by Deputy General Manager Serhat Ibrahimoglu. For his contributions to field of technology, Hakki Alkan, Founder of ShiftDelete.net, received Digital Content Creator of Year Award. ShipEntegra CEO Ali Ceylan was honored with Brand of Year Award, Ibrahim Enes Duruay, President of HEMPA Childrens Association, received Social Initiative of Year Award. In addition, AKIB Coordinator President Veysel Memis was awarded Exporting Institution of Year, Coprint3D co-founders Fatih Kazim Duymaz Hüseyin Özen received Startup of Year Award. In arts category, actor Celal Al, who portrayed character "Adsiz Bey" in TRT 1 series Selahaddin Eyyubi , was selected as Actor of Year.

title: Hearts Came Together at the Same Table at IZU
content: Hearts Came Together at the Same Table at IZU 26.03.2025 At IZU, a large-scale iftar program was organized in collaboration with the Ahdimiz Kudüs Platform and Hayrat Foundation. The event, held in the Inner Courtyard of the Historical Building, was attended by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Director of the SKS Department Hülya Sekerci, and President of the Ahdimiz Kudüs Platform Muhammet Fatih Koyun, along with many students.

title: Visit from INTI International University to IZU
content: Visit from INTI International University to IZU 11.04.2025 We were delighted to host INTI International University, one of Malaysias distinguished higher education institutions, following our initial engagement at the EURIE 2025 Summit. INTI's International Relations and Cooperation Centre Directors, Prof. Tezara Cionita and Dr Choo Wou Onn, met with our Vice Rector, Prof. Dr Ismail Küçük. During the visit, a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed, expressing mutual intent to collaborate on student exchanges, joint academic programmes, and research initiatives.

title: The IZU Family Came Together fort he Eid Celebration Ceremony
content: The IZU Family Came Together fort he Eid Celebration Ceremony 16.04.2025 The Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) community came together to celebrate Eid al-Fitr. The traditional Eid greeting ceremony was held in the universitys Historic Museum Hall was attended by our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar; Vice Rector, Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener; Secratary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir; as well as faculty members administrative staff. Speaking at the Eid greeting ceremony, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized that holidays are significant occasions that strengthen unity solidarity. He stated : In recent times, we have witnessed an increase in the pressures exerted by imperialist Powers, particularly on Muslim societies. In this context, it is of utmost importance for Islamic countries communities to remain united. We must uphold our solidarity the spirit of brotherhood with strength determination. Rector Acar concluded his remarks by expressing his hope that Eid al-Fitr would bring peace tranquility to all of humanity. He said : I wish for this holiday to be a means of goodness. I hope we are granted many more holidays to come, celebrated in health, happiness, kindness with our loved ones,  extended his thanks to the attended his thanks to the attendees. Following Rector Acars speech, the ceremony concluded with members of the IZU community exchanging Eid greetings.

title: Measuring and Assessing Learning Outcomes in Higher Education ~ (Faculty of Business and Management Sciences)
content: Measuring and Assessing Learning Outcomes in Higher Education ~ (Faculty of Business and Management Sciences) 16.04.2025 On April 1516, IZU CELT organized a training program on measuring and assessing learning outcomes in higher education for the academic staff of the Faculty of Business and Management Sciences. Led by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Kamil Arif Kirkiç, the sessions explored practical strategies for assessing learning outcomes in higher education, providing participants with practical approaches and examples to support effective measurement and evaluation processes.

title: Rector Acar and Prof. Bayindir Reappointed as Members of the Presidential Policy Board
content: Rector Acar and Prof. Bayindir Reappointed as Members of the Presidential Policy Board 17.04.2025 By the decision of our President, H.E. Recep Tayyip Erdogan, our Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar has been reappointed as a Member of the Presidential Board of Education and Training Policies, while Prof. Dr. Servet Bayindir, a faculty member at the Faculty of Business and Management Sciences, has been reappointed as a Member of the Presidential Board of Economic Policies. The decision regarding the appointments was published in the Official Gazette dated April 10, 2025, and has entered into force. We extend our best wishes for continued success to our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, and our faculty member, Prof. Dr. Servet Bayindir, and hope that their valuable contributions will continue.

title: IZU at the 10th EURIE Summit
content: IZU at 10th EURIE Summit 16.04.2025 The 10th EURIE Eurasia Higher Education Summit, organized by Eurasian Universities Union, commenced at Istanbul Lütfi Kirdar International Convention Exhibition Center. Bringing together universities, academics, Industry representetiv from around world, summit hosted over 2,000 participant from 65 countries. The opening ceremony of program was attended by Prof. Dr. Erol Özvar, President of Council of Higher Education; Davut Gül, Governor of Istanbul; Dr. Zambry Abdul Kadir, Minister of Higher Education of Malaysia; Dr. Fanta Aw, CEO of North American Association of International Educators (NAFSA); Martin Hope, Global Director of British Council; Sekib Avdagiç, Chairman of Service Exporters Association Istanbul Chamber of Commerce; Mustafa Gültepe, President of Turkish Exporters Assembly. Özvar: Universities Must Engage in International Collaboration Speaking at opening of summit, President of Council of Higher Education (YÖK), Prof. Dr. Erol Özvar, stated that EURIE has become a vital platform not only at regional level but also on a global scale for fostering dialogue, partnerships, sustainable relations among higher education institutions. Emphasizing that education builds strong bridges between countries, Prof. Özvar remarked: "Today, universities are no longer confined to delivering academic knowledge alonethey are increasingly confronted with expanded roles responsibilities, including digital transformation international collaboration." IZU Took Its Place at Summit Representing Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, Office of International Students Relations participated in summit, where participants exchanged insights on current developments in field of international higher education. On behalf of our university, meetings were held with institutions from various countries to explore opportunities for academic collaboration student exchange programs.

title: Kizilay Volunteers Gathered at IZÜ
content: Kizilay Volunteers Gathered at IZÜ 17.04.2025 Turkish Crescent Volunteers Gathered at IZÜ The Turkish Crescent's Küçükçekmece Branch brought together IZÜ students and Crescent volunteers on our campus. The "Student Table" event, held at Historical Museum Hall, gathered participants around theme of kindness. The event was attended by Istanbul Deputy Governor Mustafa Kaya, IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Crescent Board Member Emre Koç, Turkish Crescent Küçükçekmece Branch President Lawyer Fatih Kerimoglu, and many volunteers. KAYA: RED CRESCENT HAS ALWAYS BEEN BY THE SIDE OF SOCIETY In his opening speech, Istanbul Deputy Governor Mustafa Kaya emphasized Turkish Crescents strong stance in social solidarity. Kaya said, "The Crescent is a longstanding institution that has always stood by society. Especially in recent years, it has increased its efforts to stand by both oppressed and victims. The assistance provided through volunteers forms meaningful dimension of these efforts." ACAR: OUR PRIORITY IS TO RAISE OUR STUDENTS WITH THE UNDERSTANDING OF VOLUNTEERISM IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar spoke about importance university places on volunteer activities, saying: "We are a foundation university, and such activities are very valuable to us. Spreading awareness of volunteerism and ensuring that our students are raised with this understanding is among our priorities. Our differences are our wealth. Volunteering is a powerful value that holds this diversity together." KOÇ: VOLUNTEERING IS A PART OF OUR LIVES Turkish Crescent Board Member Emre Koç pointed out transformative power of volunteering in personal development. He shared that he had participated in volunteer activities during his student years, stating: "I also encountered volunteering when I was a student. I started this journey with a student movement in Levent. Volunteering is a part of our lives." KERIMOGLU: WE ARE WRITING BEAUTIFUL STORIES IN THE SKY Lawyer Fatih Kerimoglu, President of Turkish Crescent Küçükçekmece Branch, emphasized importance of social assistance activities carried out with young volunteers, saying: "If life is a movie, we are determined to play our role in best way possible. We are writing beautiful stories in sky."

title: IZU Signed a Memorandum of Understanding with krirk University
content: IZU Signed a Memorandum of Understanding with krirk University 26.02.2025 IZU hosted officials from Krirk University, one of Thailands leading higher education institutions. The delegation, led by Prof, Dr. Jaran Maluleem, Vice Rector of krirk University, was welcomed by Prof, Dr. Ismail Adak, Chairman of the Founding Board of Trustees of IZU, Prof, Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice-Rectors Prof, Dr.Ibrahim Güney, Prof Dr.Ismail Küçük, Prof, Dr. Erhan Içener, and IZÜ Secretary-General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir. During the meetings, an agreement was signed to strengthen academic and cultural cooperation between krirk University and IZÜ. The agreement outlines plans for mutual visits, joint research, and the organization of joints seminars and conferences. The International Success of Krirk University. Krirk University, which holds a leading position in international studies within the ASEan region, ranks 35th globally, 7th in Asia, and 1st in Thailand in the field of Arts and Design according to the QS Asia University rankings 2023. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University holds a significant position among higher education institutions in Turkey by hosting the highest number of Thai students. This collaboration strengthens academic ties between Turkey and Thailand while offering new opportunities in international education.

title: The IZU 2024-2025 Academic General Assembly Meeting Took Place
content: The IZU 2024-2025 Academic General Assembly Meeting Took Place 27.02.2025 The Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University 2024-2025 Academic General Assembly Meeting was held at the Abdullah Tivnikli Conferance Hall. The event was attented by IZU Rector Prpf. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, as well as IZU Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir and academic staff. In his speech at the program, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar reviewed the academic year. He provided information about the Universitys goals for the upcoming period, sharing statistical data on student figures, international agreements, IZUs position in university rankings, YÖKAK accreditation, faculties, departments, academic incentives, institutional awards, rankings, and academic works. The event concluded with the presentation of certificates to academics who received academic incentive awards, promotions, title changes, and appointments.

title: Andalusian Hajj Pilgrims: "We Want to Cross the Bosphorus on Horseback and Stroll in Sultanahmet"
content: Andalusian Hajj Pilgrims: "We Want to Cross Bosphorus on Horseback Stroll in Sultanahmet" 27.02.2025 Four Andalusian Muslims, traveling an 8,000-k,lometer rout to mecca on horseback, have arrived in Istanbul. Hosted by IZÜ, Abdullah Rafael Hernandez Mancha, Abdelqader Harkassi Aidi, Tarek Rodriguez Fernandez, Bouchaib Jadil shared inspiring journey with IZÜ students. They plan to spend Ramadan in Istanbul and, if possible, cross bridge connecting two continents on horseback, visit Hagia Sophia, stroll through Sultanahmet. During stop in Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU), pilgrims met with students in an event attended by Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük. IZÜs International Students Relations Coordinator, Ilhan Yalim, who had followed pilgrims for a month, expressed honor of hosting them in Istanbul. Yalim stated, Istanbul is significant stop on journey. KOUNOGLU. ISTANBUL REMAINS AN IMPORTANT STOP FOR PILGRIMS Prof. Dr. Hüseyin Hüsnü Koyunoglu from IZÜ emphasized Istanbuls historical significance in pilgrimage journeys, mentioning that pilgrims from regions like Uzbekistan India used to stop in Istanbul for Surre processions. Istanbul Historically a host for pilgrims, continues to be a central stop for those on way on Mecca, known as Dersaadet (Gate of Blessing). ACAR: THE LEGACY OF ANDALUSIAN CIVILIZATION IS STILL PRESENT IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmed Cevat Acar expressed his pleasure in hosting Andalusian Muslims Highlighted continuing legacy of Andalusian civilization. He noted signitificance of pilgrimage journey, stating that legacy of a long- gone civilization still influences present, espacially through Spains stance on current global issues like Palestinian situation. 35 YEARS OF DREAMING OF THE PILGRLMAGE Dr. Abdullah Rafael Hernandez Mancha shared his 35-year- long dream of making pilgrimage on horseback, a tradation he felt connected to through his study of Quran. He decided to undertake journey after a successful geography exam, converting to Islam in process. After retiring from Seville University, he embarked on this journey with fellow pilgrims Abdelqader Harkassi Aidi, Tarek Rodriguez Fernandez, Bouchaib Jadil, starting from Spain four months ago. JOURNEY THROUGH EUROPE INSTEAD OF NORTH AFRICA Mancha explained that they initially planned to travel via North Africa but shifted to Europe due to road issues in Morocco Algeria. Their journey through Italy, Bosnia, other countries has been filled with memorable experiences, including meeting an Italian nationalist who eventually softened his stance on Islam. OUR CLOTHES ARE WITNESSES TO OUR JOURNEY The pilgrims set off with limited funds, but received support from local Muslims along way. Mancha highlighted significance of worn clothes, which have become symbols of journey. He also expressed plans to spend Ramadan in Istanbul, exploring city on horseback visiting iconic sites like Hagia Sophia Sultanahmet. WE CANT BRING OUR HORSES BACK TO SPAIN Mancha revealed that while they had trained extensively for journey, they wont be able to bring horses back to Spain due to European Union regulations. After completing pilgrimage, they will fly back to Spain, leaving horses behind. AIDI: WHO ELSE BUT CRAZY SPANIARDS? Abdelqader Harkassi Aidi, a member of team, humorously likened journey to Don Quixote's adventures. He shared how journey, though tough, has been guided by Allah, he felt honored to be part of it. JADIL JOINS THE TEAM Bouchaib Jadil, who joined group while working at Dr. Manchas house, spoke about his role in logistics how journey has been most significant experience of his life.

title: The President of YTB, Abdullah Eren, Met with IZÜ students
content: The President of YTB, Abdullah Eren, Met with IZÜ students 03.03.2025 In event titled YTB Its Activities,organized by IZÜ Politics Club, president of presidency for Turks Abroad Related Communities (YTB), Abdullah Eren, met with students. The event, held at Nizamüimülk Conference Hall of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, was attended by Rector of IZÜ, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, as well as Secretary General of IZÜ, Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, along with academicians a large number of students. ACAR: TURKEY RANKS AMONG THE TOP 10 COUNTRIES HOSTING INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS In his opening speech, IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized in increasing significance of international relations academic mobility in a globlizing world. Noting that Turkey has achieved a prominent position in terms of number of international students, Rector Acar stated: Today, Turkey is among top 10 countries hosting international students. It is of great importance for Turkey to harness it is potential in this field, especially in regions we refer to as our heartland, among others. In this regard, Presidency for Turks Abroad related Communities plays a crucial role. EREN: TURKEY IS NOW MORE ACTIVE IN INTERNATIONAL ISSUES The speaker of program, Abdullah Eren, President of Presidency for Turks Abroad Related Communities (YTB), began by providing information about YTB. Eren described Turkeys rise in foreign policy over past 20 years with following words: The Presidency for Turks Abroad Related Communities (YTB) was established in 2010. The establishment of this institution is not a coincidence. There is a concept called state intelligence; it refers to a structue that acts according to circumstances makes long-terms plans. Over last 20 years, Turkey has experienced a significant paradigm shift in its foreign policy. Now, Turkey follows a policy that aims to play a more active role in international issues to have a say in global events.

title: Senior Bureaucrats Meet with IZU Students
content: Senior Bureaucrats Meet with IZU Students 05.03.2025 The Career Chat with Managers program was held at IZU. Prof. Dr. Selim Çapar, President of AREM of Ministry of Interior of Republic of Turkey, saffet Arikan Bedük, President of Governors Association, met with students. The event focused on career paths in fields of management bureaucracy. The Career Chat with Managers program, organized by Political Science Club of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, brought together students interested in management bureaucrary with experienced professionals. The seminar featured Prof. Dr. Selim Çapar, President of AREM at Ministry of Interior of Republic of Turkey, Saffet Arikan Bedük, President of Governors Association, as speakers. Prior to program, bureaucrats who visited our university gathered at our campus, hosted by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener. After a breakfast discussion on education bureaucracy, seminar moved to Seminar Hall 2 for Career Chat with Managers session. SERVING THE STATE AND THE NATION IS OUR GREATEST IDEAL In his opening speech, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Vice Rector of IZU, emphasized that bureaucracy is a critical field for students of public administration political science, saying: Most of you are young. Among us are students from Department of political Science Faculty of law. As a graduate of International Relations Department, I would like to say that when we started university, we generally had two career paths in mind. For students of Political Science Public Administration, one of most preferred career paths was becoming a district governor or governor, while for students of International Relations, Ministry of Foreign Affairs was typically most prominent. In recent years, National Intelligence Organization (MIT) has also become an area of interest for students. Our friends in Faculty of Law naturally tend to pursue careers in judgeship public prosectuion. When we were students, serving state nation was our greatest ideal. Listening to our guests today benefiting from their experiences will be a valuable gain for all of you. THE GOVERNORSHIP AND DISTRICT GOVERNORSHIP ARE CALLING PROFESSIONS After opening speech, one of programs guests, Saffet Arikan Bedük, President of Governors Association, gave a speech titled A Career from Local Government to Governorship." Bedük emphasized that public administration is a field that requires responsibility, stating: "The Turkish nation has an important heritage with its deep history rich culture. At core of this heritage are management approach strong leaders. The elements that ensure peace, security, welfare of people are well-functioning management systems. The opportunities provided by our universities state are not limited to Turkey alone. By following developments in technology, science, other areas worldwide bringing them to our country, we also bear a great responsibility. The most important structure that offers us these opportunities is state. Governorship district governorship are calling professions." Prof. Dr. Selim Çapar, President of AREM at Ministry of Interior of Republic of Turkey, presented a detailed analysis in his speech titled "From District Governorship to Senior Bureaucracy." He provided detailed insights on career planning in state administration process of rising in bureaucracy. Prof. Çapar also shared a roadmap for IZU students, outlining steps they should follow. The event, which allowed students to get a closer look at career opportunities in public administration, concluded with a Q&A session.

title: About 2024-2025 Spring Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule
content: About 2024-2025 Spring Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Schedule 10.03.2025 Students who are willing to apply for PhD qualifying exam to be held in spring semester of 2024-2025 academic year are required to fill out PhD Qualifying application form at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login between March 10-14, 2025 . PhD Qualifying Exams will be held on a date to be determined by relevant Head of Departments in June 2025 . The exams will be held at our Halkali campus. POINTS TO BE CONSIDERED 1- Students who have successfully completed their courses and seminar, have a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.00 and above, and fulfill Foreign Language Proficiency can apply for doctoral qualifying exam. 2- Students admitted with a master's degree must take doctoral qualifying exam until end of fifth semester at latest in compliance with relevant regulations. 3- In order for students to take PhD Qualifying Exam, they must have a thesis supervisor and choose from PhD Qualifying Exam section at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/Login 4- Students who applied for PhD Qualifying Examination held in spring semester of 2023-2024 academic year and did not take exam or failed exam, are required to apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be considered unsuccessful and will be disenrolled from University. 5- Please click here for post-exam procedures of students who are successful in PhD qualifying exam.

title: IZÜ Students Came Together at the Brotherhood Iftar
content: IZÜ Students Came Together at Brotherhood Iftar 19.03.2025 The IZÜ Brotherhood Iftar brought our students together at same table once again this year. The iftar program, organized under coordination of Health, Culture, and Sports Deparment with contributions of our student clubs, took place in Historic Buildings Inner Courtyard. The event attended by our Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, began with a Quran recitation. Together with call to prayer at sunset, students shared blessings of Ramadan and broke their fasts with prayers. After iftar, evening prayer performed with congregation crowned program. The event, where hundreds of students gathered at same table, left behind vibrant memories.

title: A Visit to Our University by the IHH search and Rescue Team
content: A Visit to Our University by IHH search Rescue Team 19.03.2025 Orhan parlak, Head of search Rescue Training Unit of Humanitarian Relief Foundation (IHH), visited Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. During visit, collaboration support processes for search rescue teams planned to be established at our university were discussed. In meeting hosted by Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Dean of Faculty of Sports Sciences Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit Serarslan, Assistant Dean Dr.Abdüsselam Köse, necessary steps for rapid effective intervention in disasters were evaluated. Discussions were held on potential collaboratins regarding training, equipment procurement, field applications required to establish a search rescue team within university. IHH search Rescue officials expressed their readiness to provide support in raising disaster awareness preparing for possible emergency situations.

title: International Academic Meeting at IZÜ
content: International Academic Meeting at IZÜ 19.03.2025 IZÜ hosted a delegation from Al Marisah Madani University in Indonesia. The delegation, under the guidance of the Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences, Prof. Dr. Ayse Nefise Bahçecik, visited IZÜ Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük. During the visit, academic collaborations, joint projects, and educational opportunities were discussed, university laboratories were toured, and academic meetings were held. Additionally, Dr. Andi Nur Aisyah gave a lecure on Anti-Inflammatory Treatment in Cancer Patients to students of the Faculty of Health Sciences at IZÜ. An international agreement was signed with Al Marisah Madani University in Indonesia on September 13, 2023. This visit marked an important step in implementing the agreement, developing international cooperation, and strengthening cultural ties.

title: IZÜ Signs Memorandum of Understanding with American Islamic College
content: IZÜ Signs Memorandum of Understanding with American Islamic College 29.01.2025 January 23, 2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ) hosted representatives from American Islamic College as part of its international academic collaboration efforts. The delegation was welcomed by IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar and Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener . The visit included discussions on IZÜs academic programs and institutional vision, with the participation of American Islamic College President Dr. Timothy J. Gianotti and Prof. Dr. Abdullah Yavas from the University of Wisconsin-Madison . During the meetings, which were also attended by IZÜ academics, a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed between IZÜ and American Islamic College . The agreement outlines mutual visits, joint research projects, and the organization of collaborative seminars and conferences , strengthening academic ties between the institutions.

title: Dr. Fatih Hasdemir has started his duties as the Secretary General at IZÜ
content: Dr. Fatih Hasdemir has started his duties as Secretary General at IZÜ 05.02.2025 03.02.2025 On his first day at IZÜ, Dr. Hasdemir met with administrators of university's administrative units. Our Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rector, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, wished him success in his new role. Born in 1965 in Erzurum, Dr. Hasdemir completed his undergraduate education in Department of Economics at Faculty of Economics Administrative Sciences, Uludag University (1987). He pursued his graduate doctoral studies in United Kingdom. Between 1995 2002, Dr. Hasdemir served as a faculty member Assistant Dean at Department of Economics, Faculty of Economics Administrative Sciences, Balikesir University. He worked at Energy Market Regulatory Authority Prime Ministry Privatization Administration. From 2005 to 2011, Dr. Hasdemir served as President of National Agency. He then served as Deputy Undersecretary of Ministry for EU Affairs from 2011 to 2013, as Deputy Permanent Representative of Turkey to EU from 2013 to 2016. Between 2016 2020, he worked as Deputy Undersecretary of Ministry of Development. Most recently, Dr. Hasdemir served as Secretary General of Fatih Sultan Mehmet Foundation University from 2020 to 2024. As of today, he has assumed role of Secretary General at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University.

title: A Delegation From IZÜ Visits Umm Al-Qura University in Mecca
content: A Delegation From IZÜ Visits Umm Al-Qura University in Mecca 10.02.2025 The Delegation from Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University visited Umm Al-Qura University in Mecca as part of their engagements in Saudi Arabia. Representing IZÜ during the visit were Ilhan Yalim, Coordinator of international Students and Relations, and Mehmet Savur, Promotion Officer. Meetings were held with Dr.Abdulrahman Al-Qarni, Dean of the Arabic Language Institute at Umm Al-Qura University in Mecca, Dr.Abdulrahman Al-Jahni, Vice Dean, and Prof. Dr. Hasan Buhari, Imam and Khatib of Masjid al-Haram. The disccusions focused opportunities for academic and cultural collaboration, including strengthening academic ties between two universities, expanding student faculty exghange programs, and initiating joint projects.

title: Important Announcement for Non-Thesis Master's Students of the 2024-2025 Spring Semester
content: Important Announcement Non-Thesis Master's Students of 2024-2025 Spring Semester 11.02.2025 Important Announcement Non-Thesis Master's Students of 2024-2025 Spring Semester In non-thesis master's programs of our Institute supervisors of students who have completed their study period by end of 2024-2025 academic year's Spring semester are required to submit Word version of to control email addresses by end of office hours on Friday, May 16, 2025 , at latest. title: IZU Doctoral Programs Academic Training Scholarship
content: IZU Doctoral Programs Academic Training Scholarship 26.07.2024 IZU Doctoral Programs Academic Training Scholarship Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Will provide Academic Training Scholarships to 15 doctoral students. Application Requirements - Apply for a Doctoral Program at IZU, - Not be employed in any job provide documentation for it, - Be willing to participate full-time in university's educational, teaching, research activities, - Have a minimum score of 80 from ALES, 156 from GRE, or 590 from GMAT. Foreign students are not required to have ALES, GRE, or GMAT scores; however, if they have GRE or GMAT scores, these will be converted to ALES scores for evaluation, - Have a minimum undergraduate GPA of 3.00 out of 4.00 a minimum graduate GPA of 3.50 out of 4.00, - For programs in Turkish, candidate must have a minimum score of 70 from YDS/YÖKDIL or an equivalent international foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM in relevant foreign language. For programs conducted partially or entirely in a foreign language, candidate must have a minimum score of 80 from YDS/YÖKDIL or an equivalent international foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM in relevant foreign language. Scholarship Details, Cash Amount, Number of Recipients, Duration - Coverage: Tuition fee meal fee - Mandatory General Health Insurance - Accommodation Meal Fee: If students stay in IZU Guesthouses or dormitories affiliated with Founding Foundation - Scholarship Amount: 25,000 TL - Number of Recipients: A total of 15 doctoral students, with 10 from sciences 5 from social sciences - Programs: All Doctoral Programs - Funding Sources: Scholarships will be provided by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Ilim Yayma Foundation - Duration: For normal duration of study (4 years) - Conditions: To use Academic Training Scholarship, student must be accepted into applied program enroll in relevant program. Selection Criteria For Turkish Citizens: - 25% Scientific Exam - 30% GPA - 10% Language Score - 10% ALES - 25% Interview For International Students: - 35% Scientific Exam - 35% GPA - 30% Interview - Selection Committee: Scholars will be selected by a committee consisting of two academicians determined by IZU one person determined by Ilim Yayma Foundation. How to Apply Candidates who wish to apply for scholarship must send their undergraduate graduate transcripts, language certificate, ALES/GRE/GMAT certificate, an SGK document showing that they are not employed in any workplace to ayb@izu.edu.tr by end of business hours (17:00 Istanbul time) on August 7, 2024. Application Schedule Application Dates for Academic Training Scholarships July 25 - August 7, 2024 Announcement of Pre-Evaluation Results August 9, 2024 Scholarship Scientific Evaluation Exam Date August 13, 2024 Interview Date August 15, 2024 Announcement of Results August 16, 2024

title: IZU Successfully Completed ISO 27001 Audit
content: IZU Successfully Completed ISO 27001 Audit 26.07.2024 IZU Successfully Completed ISO 27001 Information Security Management System Audit IZU has successfully completed ISO 27001 Information Security Management System audit. This important process has once again confirmed compliance of information security management system with standards. IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener highlighted importance of ISO 27001 audit, stating, "Such efforts allow us to maintain high standards of information security within institution and enable us to improve ourselves. Adhering to standards like ISO 27001 strengthens culture of information security and contributes to institutional development." During audit process, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's information security procedures, risk management, and data protection policies were thoroughly examined. The results obtained once again demonstrated high standards of university in this area. Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener emphasized importance university places on such efforts and expressed his gratitude to entire team for their contribution to this success. In 2018, university was awarded ISO 27001 Information Security Management System Certificate and continues to work with goal of continuous development and improvement in field of information security, undergoing internal and external audits every year. Maintaining continuity of ISO 27001 Information Security Management System certificate is of great importance as a testament to commitment in this direction.

title: IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS
content: IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS 12.08.2024 IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS (Second Application) Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Will grant 15 PhD scholarships with aim of training academics APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS Applying to a doctoral program at IZU, Applicants must document not being employed in any jobs, Applicants are expected to be willing to participate full-time in university's educational, instructional, research activities. Applicants must have a minimum score of 80 on ALES, minimum score of 156 on GRE, or a minimum score of 590 on GMAT. International students are not required to submit any ALES, GRE, or GMAT scores. If applicants have GRE or GMAT scores, their application will be evaluated after converting them to ALES scores. Applicants must have a minimum CGPA of 3.00 out of 4.00 in their undergraduate degrees a minimum CGPA of 3.50 out of 4.00 in their master's degrees. In Turkish language programs, applicants must have a minimum score of 70 on YDS/YÖKDIL for relevant foreign language or an internationally recognized foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM. In fully or partially foreign language programs, applicants must have a minimum score of 80 on YDS/YÖKDIL for relevant foreign language or an internationally recognized foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM. SCHOLARSHIP INFORMATION Tuition free meal Compulsory General Health Insurance For students staying at IZU Guesthouses or dormitories affiliated with Founding Foundation: Accommodation meal expenses Monthly stipend of 25.000 TL A total of 15 PhD students will be awarded scholarships (10 from Natural Sciences 5 from Social Sciences) Application is open for all PhD Programs at IZU. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Ilim Yayma Foundation will grant scholarships. Scholarship/Stipend will be given during regular period of PhD study (4 years) In order to be entitled to Academic Training Scholarship, student must be accepted to programme applied for enrolled in relevant programme. EVALUATION CRITERIA For Turkish applicants: 25% Scientific Exam, 30% CGPA, 10% ALES, 10% Language Score 25% Interview. For international applicants: 35% Scientific Exam, 35% CGPA, 30% Interview The scholarship evaluation committee comprises two academics from IZU one authorized member from Ilim Yayma Foundation. HOW TO APPLY? International candidates should submit their undergraduate graduate transcripts, language certificate, ALES/GRE/GMAT score (if they have one), a document or statement indicating they are not employed in Türkiye to email address ayb@izu.edu.tr by end of office hours (17.00 - Istanbul time) on August 23, 2024 . TIMETABLE for APPLICANTS APPLICATION DEADLINE FOR IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS August 12-23, 2024 ANNOUNCEMENT OF THE RESULTS OF INITIAL EVALUATION August 27, 2024 SCHOLARSHIP SCIENTIFIC EVALUATION EXAM DATE August 29, 2024 INTERVIEW DATE September 2, 2024 ANNOUNCEMENT OF THE RESULTS September 3, 2024

title: IZU's 10th Graduation Ceremony Held with Great Enthusiasm
content: IZU's 10th Graduation Ceremony Held with Great Enthusiasm 27.07.2024 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University held its 2023-2024 academic year graduation ceremony. The ceremony, held at IZU Halkali central campus, saw attendance of Minister of Trade Prof. Dr. Ömer Bolat, Chairman of Board of Trustees of Ilim Yayma Foundation Necmeddin Bilal Erdogan, Chairman of Board of Trustees of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Özkan Göksal, Rector of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, along with academics administrative staff. The unforgettable event at IZU Stadium began with Turkish National Anthem a moment of silence, followed by a recitation from Quran after families graduating students had taken their seats. In his opening speech, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar addressed eager graduates their families, emphasizing importance of being a part of IZU its educational quality. Acar offered advice for future highlighted significance of day, saying, "I thank our esteemed professors families who have contributed to success of our students. I congratulate our dear graduates on their graduation. May your path be clear your successes continuous." Chairman of Board of Trustees, Özkan Göksal, began his speech by congratulating graduates. He noted rise of Ilim Yayma Foundation in fields of science education, stating, "Our university, established 14 years ago, has made most of this period. We will continue to elevate quality of our university, which is among best educational institutions in our country." Necmeddin Bilal Erdogan, Chairman of Board of Trustees of Ilim Yayma Foundation, shared his excitement pride, saying, "As Ilim Yayma Foundation, we will continue to work to grow our cornerstone university, which has been developing excellent individuals for over 70 years. I wish all our students success." Minister of Trade Prof. Dr. Ömer Bolat, who graced ceremony with his presence, also inspired graduates with his remarks. Bolat said, "As a former academician at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, seeing our graduates today makes me proud." Following speeches, gifts were presented to dignitaries. After gift presentations photo sessions, top-ranking students took stage. Gifts were given to top students of each department, plaques were awarded to those who ranked highest, doubling joy. The moments when diplomas were awarded were truly memorable. As each department was called to stage one by one, families experienced a separate sense of pride. As countdown for graduation celebration began, entire hall held its breath. The graduation joy reached its peak when students tossed their caps into air. On this unforgettable day, graduates experienced excitement of stepping into their new lives, while their families friends shared in their happiness. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University once again emphasized its belief in future successes of its graduates by celebrating their achievements.

title: Official Visit to the Jordanian Ministry of Higher Education
content: Official Visit to the Jordanian Ministry of Higher Education 13.08.2024 We are pleased to share that on 8 August 2024, a delegation from Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, including our International Office Coordinator Mr. Ilhan YALIM Islamic Economics Law Masters graduate Tariq Ismail Yousuf ALSAYED AHMA, met with Dr. Aseel AlMuhaisen, Director of the Ministrys Accreditation Bureau, other key officials from The Ministry of Higher Education Scientific Research-Jordan. This visit was intended to enhance cultural academic connections between our institutions to discuss the accreditation recognition processes for our university. We would like to extend our heartfelt thanks to the Jordanian Ministry of Higher Education for their support cooperation in facilitating this important visit. This collaboration underscores the importance of our university's role the value of our alumni.

title: Appreciation from Turkish Embassy: Dr. Mehmet Zeki Serdaroglu Emphasized the International Success of IZÜ"
content: Appreciation from Turkish Embassy: Dr. Mehmet Zeki Serdaroglu Emphasized International Success of IZÜ" 23.08.2024 We had honour of hosting Dr. Mehmet Zeki Serdaroglu, Religious Services Counsellor from Embassy of Republic of Türkiye in Dushanbe, at our university. title: Successful Completion of Arabic Language Camp in Jordan
content: Successful Completion of Arabic Language Camp in Jordan 19.08.2024 We are pleased to announce the successful completion of the Arabic Language Camp, held from 7 July to 10 August in Jordan, in collaboration with our university Akdemistanbul Language Courses. Approximately 20 undergraduate postgraduate students from various departments of our university participated in the Jordan Arabic Language Camp, where they had the opportunity to enhance their language skills. In addition to language training, students enjoyed university visits, tourist excursions, various activities, all of which they expressed great satisfaction with. The Coordinator of International Students Relations, Mr. Ilhan YALIM, who attended the Certificate Ceremony approved by The World Islamic Sciences Education University of Jordan, extended his gratitude to our universitys Rectorate Board of Trustees for their support in realizing this program. IZU students who successfully completed the course received their certificates expressed their interest in having similar programs opportunities in different countries languages.

title: New Era in Special Education
content: New Era in Special Education 25.09.2024 An orientation and introductory meeting was held for new students joining Special Education Department. During meeting, department's mission and career opportunities it offers to students were presented. An orientation and introductory meeting was held for new students joining Special Education Department. The meeting, aimed at facilitating students' adaptation to university life, was presided over by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Nüket Afat, Head of Special Education Department. In a friendly atmosphere with participation of academic and administrative staff, students had opportunity to learn about core principles of Special Education Department and opportunities they will encounter in their professional development journey. Presentations were made regarding department's approach to education and future career possibilities, and academic staff answered students' questions. Assoc. Prof. Dr. Nüket Afat wished new students a healthy, successful, and happy university life, noting that this process would mark beginning of a bright future for them.

title: Academic Writing Training at IZÜ
content: Academic Writing Training at IZÜ 02.10.2024 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University hosted an 'Academic Writing' training as part of Erasmus+ Core Project, conducted by Dr. Mustafa Özgenel from Department of Educational Sciences. In morning session, Dr. Özgenel addressed common mistakes encountered in academic writing process. He emphasized importance of selecting right journal and submission process, along with critical roles of peer review and ethical considerations in scientific process, providing participants with valuable tips from his own experiences. In afternoon session, an academic writing workshop was led by Dr. Osman Özdemir from Turkish Education Department. The workshop featured various activities focused on creating sections of articles using brainstorming techniques.

title: IZU Club Opening Took Place in a Festival Atmosphere
content: IZU Club Opening Took Place in a Festival Atmosphere 23.10.2024 The "14th Club Opening Festival" held at our university was a vibrant affair. While our clubs showcased their activities at various booths, participants enjoyed workshops, theater performances, and play areas. The event, which took place at the Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Stadium, provided students with unforgettable moments. The organization, attended by our Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar and Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, featured student clubs setting up booths for new members. During the event organized by the Directorate of Student Affairs, Rector Acar visited the booths, engaging with students and learning about their club activities. The festival, which included the presentation of awards to clubs by Rector Acar and Vice Rectors Prof. Güney and Prof. Içener, created a true festival atmosphere with its workshops, theater performances, and play areas. The event concluded with a concert.

title: The Importance of Organ Donation Discussed at IZÜ
content: The Importance of Organ Donation Discussed at IZÜ 20.11.2024 The IZÜ Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Nursing, organized an "Organ Donation Seminar" as part of Organ Donation Week. The program featured representatives from Mehmet Akif Ersoy Thoracic, Cardiovascular Surgery Training Research Hospital as speakers. They provided attendees with information on importance of organ donation, donation process, raising public awareness. The "Organ Donation Seminar," organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Nursing, was held in Seminar Room 2. The event was attended by Faculty Dean, Prof. Dr. Ayse Nefise Bahçecik, representatives from Ministry of Health's Istanbul Mehmet Akif Ersoy Thoracic Cardiovascular Surgery Training Research Hospital, who provided attendees with comprehensive information. In her opening speech, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Zülfünaz Özer emphasized life-saving role of organ donation need to increase public awareness on matter. Özer stated, "Organ donation is one of most sacred ways for a person who has passed away to give life to another. One person's decision can change dozens of lives rekindle hope." Organ Transplant Coordinators from Mehmet Akif Ersoy Hospital, Specialist Nurse Gamze Ataman Yildiz Nurse Sidik Biricik, shared insights on organ donation transplantation processes. Yildiz explained legal aspects of organ donation, transplantation process, requirements for donation, brain death, donor care. She highlighted that Turkey is a global leader, particularly in live donor donations. As part of event, a booth set up by Ministry of Health allowed university students to fill out forms to become organ donors.

title: IZÜ hosted the Special Education Workshop
content: IZÜ hosted Special Education Workshop 27.11.2024 The "Educational Diagnosis Guidance Workshop," organized by Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education, was hosted by IZÜ. The event, held in Museum Hall, was attended by IZÜ Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Istanbul Provincial Director of National Education Assoc. Prof. Dr. Murat Mücahit Yentür, RAM representatives from various districts. The workshop featured discussions roundtable sessions on accurate diagnostic methods, guidance strategies, development of individualized education plans for individuals with special educational needs. The "Educational Diagnosis Guidance Workshop," organized by Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education, was held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Museum Hall. The event, attended by IZÜ Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney, Provincial Education Director Assoc. Prof. Dr. Murat Mücahit Yentür, RAM representatives from various districts, focused on improving diagnosis guidance for students with special educational needs. Over two days, participants shared insights discussed strategies to enhance education quality. Prof. Güney expressed pride in hosting workshop highlighted its role in advancing special education models. "WE AIM FOR ZERO ERRORS IN SPECIAL EDUCATION" On second day of workshop, Istanbul Provincial Education Director Assoc. Prof. Dr. Murat Mücahit Yentür thanked IZÜ for hosting, calling it a meaningful venue. He stressed importance of consistent quality in special education across Istanbul, stating, "We aim for zero errors in special education. Our children are invaluable, so are you. Better communication with parents of special needs children is essential, issues must be shared to improve. This workshops outcomes will be critical in shaping future of special education."

title: The Great Plane Tree of Turkish Music Commemorated at IZÜ on the 50th Anniversary of His Passing
content: The Great Plane Tree of Turkish Music Commemorated at IZÜ on 50th Anniversary of His Passing 27.11.2024 The "Pole of Ney" Ulvi Erguner was commemorated at IZÜ on 50th anniversary of his passing. The program, titled "The Ney Virtuoso Ulvi Erguner: Transcending Time with His Sound Passion," featured a concert by his son, ney virtuoso IZÜ faculty member Süleyman Erguner, alongside vocal instrumental artists. The event, which showcased Erguner family compositions spanning generations, was highlighted by touching memories from Korean War. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University held a program titled "The Ney Virtuoso Ulvi Erguner: Transcending Time with His Sound Passion," honoring life contributions of Ulvi Erguner, known as Kutb-i Nâyi (Pole of Ney Virtuosos). Led by Süleyman Erguner, event included stories a commemorative concert. In his opening speech, IZÜ Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar highlighted importance of preserving Turkish music amidst rise of foreign influences emphasized IZÜs efforts to support it through education research. THE ERGUNER FAMILY'S MUSICAL LEGACY At an event attended by IZÜ Vice Rectors Ibrahim Güney Erhan Içener, IZÜ faculty member ney virtuoso Süleyman Erguner reflected on lasting influence of his father, Ulvi Erguner, grandfather, Süleyman Erguner. There is an Erguner school in ney, passed down through generations, he said. My father, Ulvi Erguner, was a pioneer, bringing Sufi music to radio paving way for others. A follower of Mevlanas teachings, he expanded Sufi music ney tradition both on stage in academia. A MEMORY FROM THE KOREAN WAR: THE GAZI NEY Süleyman Erguner shared a moving story from his father Ulvi Erguners time during Korean War. He recounted how his father played ney during a month-long sea voyage to accompany Turkish soldiers, offering them comfort amid hardships of war homesickness. The neys sound became a source of solace morale for troops throughout journey, he said. A ney damaged in an explosion during war became known in music circles as gazi ney (veteran ney), which Erguner later repaired. Years later, he had honor of playing it again at a special invitation from Korea. The event continued with a concert featuring compositions from Erguner family select works of Turkish music. Süleyman Erguner was accompanied by Mehmet Öncel, TRT vocalist Furkan Biçer, others, offering music lovers a journey through Turkish music's rich heritage. Ulvi Erguners legacy provided an emotional cultural experience for all in attendance.

title: Visit to IZU by Dr. Ali al-Qaradaghi, the President of the Union of Muslim Scholars
content: Visit to IZU by Ali al-Qaradaghi, President of Union of Muslim Scholars 20.12.2024 Sheikh Ali Al-Qaradaghi, President of Union of Muslim Scholars, paid a visit to Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) on Friday, December 13, 2024, upon invitation of IZU International Research Center of Islamic Economics Finance (IRCIEF). Prof. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Rector of IZU, warmly welcomed Sheikh Al-Qaradaghi in his office. The importance of building a bridge between academic practical dimensions of Islamic economics finance was emphasized during meeting at Rectorate. Prof. Abdulmuttalip Arpa, Head of Department of Islamic Economics Law (Arabic), also attended meeting. Within scope of Sheikh Al-Qaradaghi's visit, a series of academic sessions on Islamic economics finance were held. In first session, moderated by Asst. Prof. Ghaith Mahaini, current developments in field of Islamic economics finance status of educational curricula were discussed with academics graduate students. In second session, open to public moderated/translated by Prof. Servet Bayindir, Director of IRCIEF, Sheikh Al-Qaradaghi delivered a conference titled "Islami Economics Finance at Crossroads of Fiqh Economics". The conference ended with presentation of gifts a group photo session.

title: ELT Club Welcome Event: Embracing New Beginnings!
content: ELT Club Welcome Event: Embracing New Beginnings! 23.12.2024 This years ELT Club Welcome Event , organized by the English Language Teaching (ELT) program, was held with great enthusiasm and high participation from our students. Designed to warmly welcome our new students into the program, the event provided an excellent opportunity for them to connect with each other and meet our faculty members.
