title: 19th National Guidance and Psychological Counseling Students Congress Held at IZU
content: 19th National Guidance Psychological Counseling Students Congress Held at IZU 27.07.2024 19th National Guidance Psychological Counseling Students Congress was held with great enthusiasm at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU), in collaboration with Turkish Psychological Counseling Guidance Association. congress, themed "21st Century Skills: Current Needs Innovative Approaches in Psychological Counseling Guidance," provided a valuable platform for knowledge exchange between industry professionals students. opening speech was delivered by Mustafa Yüksel Erdogdu, Head of Guidance Psychological Counseling Department at IZU, who emphasized significance of congress thanked participants. On first day, IZU Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar Vice Rector Ibrahim Güney continued opening addresses. Acar elaborated on important topics such as "Social Loneliness," while Güney highlighted need to strengthen character values of youth. opening keynote was presented by Assoc. Mehmet Dinç, Head of Guidance Psychological Counseling Department at IZU, offering contemporary perspectives in psychological counseling. Throughout day, inspiring lectures by Binnur Yesilyaprak Olcay Yilmaz, along with interactive workshops, allowed participants to engage in active learning. evening featured eagerly anticipated oral presentations a quiz competition. second day began with an inspiring keynote by Metin Piskin, Deputy Chairman of Turkish Guidance Psychological Counseling Association, focusing on innovative approaches in education. program continued with informative presentations by Assoc. Mehmet Ali Yildiz Assoc. Oguzhan Kirdök. Afternoon sessions included workshops, raffles, a student panel, fostering active information exchange among participants. live music performance "From Meliss Voice" added cultural artistic richness to congress. final day began with an inspiring lecture by Ibrahim Bilgen, followed by in-depth workshop sessions. congress concluded successfully, providing valuable insights into future directions in field, with prominent figures from industry in attendance. Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar Vice Rector Ibrahim Güney participated in closing session, where final declaration was read a closing panel was held. In his closing remarks, Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar expressed satisfaction in hosting event, highlighting evolving role of PDR beyond schools due to increasing needs demands. He emphasized importance of interdisciplinary skills specialization, advising students to develop competencies both vertically horizontally. Acar thanked students for organizing such a scientific event. Participants were presented with plaques, memories of congress were immortalized through photographs taken throughout event. active participation of attendees at every stage of congress deep understanding of current practices underscored its importance. This major event, hosted by IZU, solidified its place as a platform for sharing latest developments in field of psychological counseling guidance.

title: Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability) ERASMUS Project
content: Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability) ERASMUS Project 26.07.2024 Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability) ERASMUS Project With support of National Agency and coordinated by Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education, multiplication meeting for ERASMUS Project titled "Specially Digitalised (Special Learning Disability)" with project number 2020-1-TR01-KA226-SCH-098336, in which Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is a partner, took place on 05.09.2023. Academic staff from our university, project partners from abroad, and teachers from Ministry of National Education attended multiplication meeting. The goal of project is to develop a digitally supported educational platform for students aged 6-9 diagnosed with special learning disabilities. The multiplication meeting was conducted both face-to-face and simultaneously online. The opening speeches were made by our Vice Rector, Prof. Dr. Ibrahim GÜNEY. Following this, Mr. Özer TOPLU, coordinator on behalf of Provincial Directorate of National Education, provided information about project introduction and results. Then, our university faculty member Dr. Nüket AFAT gave a presentation on target audience of project, children diagnosed with special learning disabilities. The last presentation before noon was given by Assoc. Prof. Dr. Aras BOZKURT from Anadolu University, who introduced digital platform prepared for children with special learning disabilities. In afternoon sessions, Romanian team, one of project partners, presented on teacher training and professional development. Following this, Bulgarian team gave a presentation titled "Self-Esteem and Socialization" within scope of children with special needs. Finally, Portuguese team shared satisfaction surveys of students with special learning disabilities who tested digital platform during Transnational Project Meeting held in Portugal. After presenting certificates to project partners and participants, multiplication meeting concluded.

title: Protocol Signed with Indonesian Universities
content: Protocol Signed with Indonesian Universities 26.07.2024 Significant Step for Strengthening International Cooperation Cultural Ties: Indonesian Universities at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University A delegation from Muhammadiyah Kudus University, Almarisah Madani University, Hasanuddin University visited Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University for a "Cooperation Visit." This special meeting was held on Wednesday, September 13, 2023, in Senate Hall of Rectorate Building, featuring a productive discussion between our university's Rector, Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat ACAR, Vice Rector, Prof. Dr. Ismail KÜÇÜK, visiting delegation. During meeting, possibilities of collaboration in academic research fields between universities were discussed. Providing detailed information about Turkey our university, Rector ACAR stated, "This cooperation developing between our universities is an important step that will contribute to both our academic world our cultural ties. We have potential to collaborate in many areas such as student exchanges, joint projects, research. We hope that number of Indonesian students will continue to increase that friendship between our countries will grow even stronger." A protocol covering potential areas of collaboration such as future academic work student exchange programs between universities was signed. At end of meeting, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat ACAR Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail KÜÇÜK presented gifts to guests, while guests also presented special gifts from Indonesia to Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat ACAR.

title: IZU Sports Careers for the Disabled and Autism Conference
content: IZU Careers for Disabled Autism Conference 26.07.2024 Careers for Disabled Autism Conference at IZU Organized by Faculty of Sciences at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, conference titled "Sports Careers for Disabled Autism" was held on Tuesday, November 7, 2023, in Abdullah Tivnikli conference hall at Halkali Campus. The program began with speeches by Akit Writer Science Expert Mr. Ahmet GÜLÜMSEYEN, attended by Dean of Faculty of Sciences Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit SERARSLAN, Vice Dean Dr. Abdusselam KÖSE, academic administrative staff, students. Mr. GÜLÜMSEYEN's speech addressed general issues of individuals with disabilities creation of awareness consciousness towards these individuals. Following his talk, Berkan ERDOGAN, Education Coordinator of Anka Special Education Center, provided information about children with autism, emphasizing significant impact of sports activities on their education rehabilitation processes. Mr. ERDOGAN also mentioned opportunities for students from Faculty of Sciences to work at his institution. Finally, Dr. Abdusselam KÖSE, Vice Dean of Faculty of Sciences, thanked Mr. Ahmet GÜLÜMSEYEN Mr. Berkan ERDOGAN for their participation proceeded to Q&A session. After conference concluded with Q&A session, Dean of Faculty of Sciences, Prof. Dr. Mustafa Zahit SERARSLAN, Vice Dean Dr. Abdusselam KÖSE, Dr. Ibrahim AKGÜN presented commemorative gifts to Mr. Ahmet GÜLÜMSEYEN, Education Coordinator Mr. Berkan ERDOGAN, teachers guest students with autism spectrum disorder from Anka Special Education Center who participated in conference.

title: Respect Day for Prof. Dr. Hasan Pulasli and Symposium on Current Developments in Corporate Law Held
content: Respect Day for Hasan Pulasli and Symposium on Current Developments in Corporate Law Held 26.07.2024 Respect Day for Hasan Pulasli and Symposium on Current Developments in Corporate Law Held The "Symposium on Current Developments in Corporate Law," organized in honor of Hasan Pulasli, was held on our campus. The opening speeches of the symposium were delivered by our Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar and the Dean of the Faculty of Law Mustafa Ates. Following a video presentation prepared for Hasan Pulasli, the symposium sessions featured presentations by: - Ismail Kayar - Mustafa Topaloglu - Ömer Korkut - Sükrü Yildiz - Mustafa Çeker - Ögr. Üyesi M. Fatih Cengil Our Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar presented a thank-you plaque to Hasan Pulasli. Additionally, Mustafa Ates presented a two-volume book titled "In Honor of Hasan Pulasli," which is a compilation of numerous academic articles on Commercial Law.

title: The Late Prof. Dr. Sabahattin Zaim Commemorated
content: The Late Zaim Commemorated 26.07.2024 Commemorating Understanding Zaim: An Event Held at Istanbul Zaim University To honor understand esteemed "beautiful person" Zaim, who devoted himself to path of knowledge wisdom, an event was organized in collaboration with Esenler Municipality Youth Sports Services Directorate on Monday, December 4, 2023. The event featured Sedat MURAT, one of his students, as a speaker. The "Sabahattin Zaim Commemoration Event," organized by Faculty of Sports Sciences to coincide with week of our esteemed professor's passing, saw participation from all class levels. The opening speech of event was delivered by Dean of Faculty of Sports Sciences, Zahid SERARSLAN, who emphasized that Zaim should be taken as an example in matter of human development. As part of program, Sedat Murat, who was one of his closest most successful students then Rector of Çanakkale Onsekiz Mart University, currently serving as Vice Rector of Istanbul University, shared many insights about Zaim with participants. In his speeches, Sedat MURAT described Zaim as a perfect human being, patient, well-intentioned, an Ottoman gentleman, someone who should be taken as a role model. The event concluded with distribution of Zaim's book "Bir Ömre Bedel" to students, followed by a book signing session with Sedat MURAT, a group photo. We extend our gratitude to esteemed Sedat Murat for taking time to visit our university helping us understand Zaim, teacher of teachers, better. We remember Zaim with respect gratitude.

title: Meaningful Panel on the Anniversary of Democracy and National Unity Day
content: Meaningful Panel on Anniversary of Democracy and National Unity Day 27.07.2024 On anniversary of Democracy and National Unity Day, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University held a panel titled "The Victory of a Nation Resisting with Sacrifice." The event, chaired by IZU Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, featured speeches from IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Prof. Dr. Hasan Kâmil Yilmaz, and Prof. Dr. Ergün Yildirim. Prof. Dr. Hasan Kâmil Yilmaz discussed religious exploitation behind July 15 movement, emphasizing harm caused by misguiding religious sentiments and nation's resistance to such manipulations. Prof. Dr. Ergün Yildirim analyzed sociological aspects, noting that coup attempt targeted society as a whole and required detailed examination. The panel highlighted Turkey's commitment to democracy and national unity, providing participants with a deeper understanding of July 15's significance. The event aimed to strengthen awareness of these values within national consciousness.

Students in this situation fulfill publication requirement before May 31, 2024 . master students who will graduate in Spring semester take defense exam by Friday , July 05, 2024 . In accordance decision of IZU Senate dated 18.09.2017 numbered 2017/21; Starting from spring semester of 2017-2018 academic year, students enrolled in master's doctoral programs within Graduate School Institute are required to fulfill publication requirement. Checking Plagiarism Compliance Writing Rules Completed Master's Theses primary responsibility preparation of in accordance Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. thesis, written in accordance Graduate School Institute's writing guide, should be sent to advisor by student via email. supervisor e-mail their student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. Theses that do not comply Writing Guideline and/or those similarity reports exceeding 20% be corrected submitted within five days. Control Team Members will not control more than three times. All responsibility in this regard belongs to supervisor student. It be noted that singular similarities not exceed 1% in plagiarism report. Institutional supervisors submit documents of master's students who will graduate to Graduate Education Institute via ÜBYS system. External advisors submit documents of graduate students who will graduate to Head of Department. Click here procedures that students advisors need to follow after obtaining approval from Control Unit. In Case of Period Extension Failure Students, who are not able to complete their until May 10, 2024, within their normal study period of four semesters extend their submission to Fall Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is valid Spring Semester of 2024-2025 Academic Year, second extension period. Students, who have failed in study or have not been able to take defense exam within maximum study period of six semesters, will be dismissed from University. According to 1st 2nd paragraphs of Article 9 of IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; (1) duration of master's program is four semesters, starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, program is completed in a maximum of six semesters. (2) student who has failed to complete credit courses one Seminar course or has not been able to fulfill success criteria determined by University, who has failed in study or has not been able to take defense exam within maximum period of study will be disenrolled from University.

title: IZU 2023-2024 Academic General Assembly Meeting Held
content: IZU 2023-2024 Academic General Assembly Meeting Held 27.07.2024 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University held its 2023-2024 Academic General Assembly Meeting, attended by university's academic administrative staff. The meeting, led by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ibrahim Güney Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, involved comprehensive evaluations of university's future goals. In his opening speech, Rector Acar referenced graduation ceremony, saying, "We bid farewell to our graduates with 2023-2024 graduation ceremony. I wish our graduates success in their new lives. I thank all our academic administrative staff for their efforts this year." He congratulated newly appointed promoted academic staff by name, expressing his belief that these changes will greatly contribute to university's academic development. "We continue to take determined steps to enhance quality of our education," Acar stated, highlighting IZU's achievements in education its value to society. He also emphasized university's commitment to not remain indifferent to global issues, pledging to continue raising their voices against injustices, such as resistance in Gaza. Rector Acar reviewed past academic year's successes provided information on goals set for coming year. At end of meeting, academics shared their questions views, contributing valuable insights regarding university's future.

title: 2023-2024 Spring Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Applications
content: 2023-2024 Spring Semester Doctoral Qualifying Exam Applications 15.04.2024 Students who wish to apply for PhD qualification exam to be held in spring semester of 2023-2024 academic year should fill out PhD Qualification application form available at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login between April 15-19, 2024. The PhD Qualification Exams will be held at a date to be determined by relevant Head of Department within month of June 2024 . The exams will be held at our Halkali campus. POINTS TO CONSIDER 1 - Students who have completed their courses and seminars, have a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.00 and have fulfilled their Foreign Language Proficiency may apply for PhD qualification exam. 2 - Students who are accepted with a master's degree have to take PhD qualification exam by end of fifth semester at latest, following relevant regulations. 3 - For students to be eligible to take PhD Qualification Exam, they must have thesis advisors, and they need to select 'Doctoral Qualification Exam' option from https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr/Login . 4 - Students who did not apply for PhD Qualification Exam held in fall semester of 2023-2024 academic year or failed exam must apply again on specified dates. Students who do not apply will be deemed unsuccessful and will be dismissed from University. 5 - Click here for post-exam procedures for students who have passed PhD qualification exam.

title: IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS
content: IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS 25.07.2024 IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University will grant 15 PhD scholarships with aim of training academics APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS Applying to a doctoral program at IZU, Applicants must document not being employed in any jobs, Applicants are expected to be willing to participate full-time in university's educational, instructional, research activities. Applicants must have a minimum score of 80 on ALES, minimum score of 156 on GRE, or a minimum score of 590 on GMAT. International students are not required to submit any ALES, GRE, or GMAT scores. If applicants have GRE or GMAT scores, their application will be evaluated after converting them to ALES scores. Applicants must have a minimum CGPA of 3.00 out of 4.00 in their undergraduate degrees a minimum CGPA of 3.50 out of 4.00 in their master's degrees. In Turkish language programs, applicants must have a minimum score of 70 on YDS/YÖKDIL for relevant foreign language or an internationally recognized foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM. In fully or partially foreign language programs, applicants must have a minimum score of 80 on YDS/YÖKDIL for relevant foreign language or an internationally recognized foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM. SCHOLARSHIP INFORMATION Tuition free meal Compulsory General Health Insurance For students staying at IZU Guesthouses or dormitories affiliated with Founding Foundation: Accommodation meal expenses Monthly stipend of 25.000 TL A total of 15 PhD students will be awarded scholarships (10 from Natural Sciences 5 from Social Sciences) Application is open for all PhD Programs at IZU. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Ilim Yayma Foundation will grant scholarships. Scholarship/Stipend will be given during regular period of PhD study (4 years) In order to be entitled to Academic Training Scholarship, student must be accepted to programme applied for enrolled in relevant programme. EVALUATION CRITERIA For Turkish applicants: 25% Scientific Exam, 30% CGPA, 10% ALES, 10% Language Score 25% Interview. For international applicants: 35% Scientific Exam, 35% CGPA, 30% Interview The scholarship evaluation committee comprises two academics from IZU one authorized member from Ilim Yayma Foundation. HOW TO APPLY? International candidates should submit their undergraduate graduate transcripts, language certificate, ALES/GRE/GMAT score (if they have one), a document or statement indicating they are not employed in Türkiye to email address ayb@izu.edu.tr by end of office hours (17.00 - Istanbul time) on August 7, 2024 . title: Successful Screening and Discussion of "The Night Won't End" at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University
content: Successful Screening and Discussion of "The Night Won't End" at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University 08.07.2024 On July 1st, 2024, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University's Center for Islam and Global Affairs (CIGA) hosted a successful screening of the documentary film "The Night Won't End," focusing on Gaza. The event, moderated by Üveys Han, featured a discussion with executive producer and co-writer Laila Al-Arian, and cinematographer Ashraf Mashawari. They shared the mental and logistical challenges of filming, including interviews with victims' families and teams on the ground, providing deep insights into the struggles faced by the Gazan people. It was a meaningful evening for all attendees.

title: Dr. Mbwarali Kame and Team Visit Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University
content: Dr. Mbwarali Kame and Team Visit Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University 26.07.2024 On 23rd July, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) had the distinct pleasure of hosting Dr. Mbwarali Kame, County Executive Committee Member (CECM) for the Department of Education, County Government of Mombasa, Kenya, along with his esteemed team. The visit was marked by productive discussions with the General Coordinatorship of International Relations and Exchange Programs at IZU. Both parties explored avenues for collaboration, focusing on enhancing educational ties between our institutions. We are grateful for the opportunity to engage in such meaningful dialogue and look forward to future cooperation that will mutually benefit our students and academic communities.

title: Important Announcement for Thesis Master's Students whose ?Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire
content: Important Announcement Master's Students whose ?Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire 11.02.2025 Important Announcement Master's Students whose Normal-Maximum Study Period Will Expire In Master's programs of our Institute, supervisors of students who have completed standard study period of four semesters or a maximum study period of six semesters (excluding Scientific Preparation, Registration Suspension, Additional Time) as of end of Spring Semester of 2024-2025 academic year are required to submit Word version of to Control Team by end of office hours on Friday, May 16, 2025 , at latest. Theses submitted after May 16, 2025, will not be accepted. The forms defense of master's students graduating in Spring semester must be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, June 13, 2025 . The master students who will graduate in Spring semester must take defense exam by Friday , July 4, 2025 . Last Submission Date Control May 16, 2025 Last Submission Date Defense Documents June 13, 2025 Last Day of Defense Examinations July 4, 2025 Requirements Taking Defence Exam Students must have at least seven courses one Seminar course (a total of 60 ECTS) together with a minimum GPA of 2.50 to take defense exam. The title of determined by student together with their advisor must be listed in master's program. Checking Plagiarism Compliance with Writing Rules Completed Master's Theses The primary responsibility preparation of in accordance with Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. The supervisor must e-mail their student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. Click here procedures that students supervisors must follow during Control Unit. In Case of Period Extension Failure Students, who are not able to complete their until mentioned day, within their normal study period of four semesters extend their submission to Spring Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is also valid second extension period. Students, who have failed in study or have not been able to take defense exam within maximum study period of six semesters, will be dismissed from University. According to 1st 2nd paragraphs of Article 9 of IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; (1) The duration of master's program with is four semesters, starting from semester in which courses related to program enrolled in program are given, regardless of whether they are registered each semester, except period spent in scientific preparation, program is completed in a maximum of six semesters. (2) The student who has failed to complete credit courses one Seminar course or has not been able to fulfill success criteria determined by University, who has failed in study or has not been able to take defense exam within maximum period of study will be disenrolled from University.

content: Important Announcement Students are in Period ?whose Study Period Will Expire 11.02.2025 Important Announcement Students are in Period whose Study Period Will Expire In programs of our Institute , supervisors of students, have completed standard study period of eight semesters or a maximum study period of twelve semesters (excluding Scientific Preparation, Suspension of Registration, Additional Time) by end of Spring Semester of 2024-2025 academic year, are required to submit Word version of to Control Team by end of office hours on Friday, May 16, 2025, at latest. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) students, have not submitted their proposal to Graduate Education Institute through their supervisors on time, will not be able to take defense even if they have completed their thesis. Control of Plagiarism Writing Rules of of Student Whose is Completed primary responsibility preparation of in accordance Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. supervisor e-mail student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. Click here procedures that students supervisors follow during Control process. In Case of Maximum Period of Study Ends Failure students, are not able to complete their until mentioned day, within their normal study period of eight semesters extend their submission to next semester period, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is also valid other extension periods. students, have failed in study or have not been able to take defense within maximum study period of twelve semesters, will be disenrolled from University. According to IZU Graduate Education Teaching Regulation; Finalizing ARTICLE 24 - (1) A student in doctorate program writes results obtained in accordance institute writing guide defends his orally before jury. (2) Before defense of in theses given correction, student completes correction submits it to advisor. advisor presents plagiarism report to institute a written opinion that is defensible has been written in accordance institute writing guide. In case of a real plagiarism in data in report, is sent to institute board of directors to be decided together its justification. (3) In order student's to be finalized, three successful monitoring committee reports be submitted at least every six months. If monitoring is not done on time, student is deemed unsuccessful from monitoring result of that period. (4) jury is appointed recommendation of advisor head of department approval of institute board of directors. jury consists of five faculty members, including three lecturers on student's monitoring committee including advisor., at least two of them from outside University If advisor is from another higher education institution, jury consist of one faculty member outside university four faculty members inside university. institute board of directors decides whether consultant has right to vote. If advisor does not have right to vote, jury consists of six faculty members. In addition, second supervisor can take part in jury without right to vote. (5) members of jury gather at earliest fifteen days at latest one month from date is delivered to them, takes student to defense. defense consists of presentation of work question-answer section that follows. defense meetings are open to participation of lecturers, graduate students experts from field. (6) After completion of exam, jury of is closed to audience it decides to accept, reject or correct absolute majority. Students whose is accepted are evaluated as successful. This decision is notified to institute by department within three days following exam. student whose is unsuccessful rejected is dismissed from University. student, got a correction advice on his thesis, makes necessary corrections within six months at latest defends his before same jury. student fails in this defense is dismissed from University. Those have not been successful in but admitted to Ph.D an undergraduate degree, may get according to first paragraph of Article 16 a master diploma without thesis.

title: Important Announcement for Students in the PhD Dissertation Proposal or Dissertation Monitoring Stage in the Spring Semester of 2024-2025
content: Important Announcement for Students in PhD Proposal or Monitoring Stage in Spring Semester of 2024-2025 12.02.2025 Important Announcement for Students in PhD Proposal or Monitoring Stage in Spring Semester of 2024-2025 defenses/dissertation meetings for spring semester of 2024-2025 academic year must be held by end of office hours on Monday, June 30, 2025, at latest. Students who exceed this deadline will be considered unsuccessful in their proposals/dissertation stage. thesis documents of PhD students in spring semester must be submitted to Graduate Education Institute by Head of Department by Thursday, July 3, 2025 , at latest. Last day for Proposal Defenses/Dissertation Monitoring Meetings June 30, 2025 Last Day for Submission of Proposal Defenses/ Monitoring Documents July 3, 2025 POINTS TO CONSIDER According to IZU Graduate Education Training Regulations; Proposal Defense ARTICLE 23 - (1) student, who successfully completes PhD qualifying exam, defends proposal, which includes purpose, method, study plan of research to be carried out, in front of committee, within six months at latest. delivers a written report on to committee members at least fifteen days before oral defense. (2) committee makes decision on acceptance, correction, or rejection of submitted by with an absolute majority. One month is given for correction. At end of this period, decision made in direction of acceptance or rejection by an absolute majority is notified to Institute by head of department, with a report within three days following end of process. (3) whose is rejected has right to choose a new supervisor and/or subject. In this case, a new committee able to be appointed. who wants to continue program with same supervisor is taken to defense again within three months, who changed supervisor subject within six months. whose is also rejected in this defense is disenrolled from University. time elapsed in these stages is counted from duration of student's PhD education. (5) who does not take defense within time specified in first paragraph without an acceptable excuse is considered unsuccessful, thesis is rejected. Monitoring Meetings ARTICLE 23 - (4) For whose thesis is accepted, thesis committee meets at least twice a year, once in January-June once in July-December. submits a written report to committee members at least one month before meeting date. In this report, a summary of study done so far study plan for next semester are stated. student's thesis study is determined as successful or unsuccessful by committee. Students who are found unsuccessful by committee twice in a row or three times intermittently are dismissed from University. thesis report minutes must be submitted by thesis supervisors to Graduate Education Institute with approval of Head of Department through UBYS system. supervisors from outside IZU, must contact relevant Head of Department to submit documents of their students. PhD students who have successfully completed courses, seminars, at least three meetings within their deadlines, have a weighted grade point average of 3,00 above, meet publication requirement can take defense exam.

title: From Past To Present: GAZA
content: From Past To Present: GAZA 17.02.2025 In program From Past To Present held at IZÜ, historical process and current sitiuation of jerusalem and GAZA were discussed. The program emphasized responsibilities of Islamic world. In program From Past To Present: GAZA, held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, Ahmed Türkistani, international Coordinator of Hayrat Foundation, and Palestinian academic Muhammed Al Azami participated as speakers. The event, orginaized in Abdullah Tiknivli Conferance Hall, began with a recitation from Quran by Muhammed El Vadi, a student from Faculty Of Islamic Sciences. Ahmed Türkistani, international Coordinator of Hayrat Foundation, stated they have been actively engaged in humanititrian aid efforts in GAZA since events of October 7. He emphasized that inivatives carried out to support people of GAZA have been conducted with great dedication. Meanwhile, Palestinian academic Muhammed Al Azami shared historcal process of Al-Aqsa Mosque with participants, stressing that Jerusalem should be a top priority for Muslims. He asserted, Every Muslim must keep jerusalem on their agenda. Jerusalem should be most significant cause in hearts of Muslims. Al Azami also highlighted importance of fostering historical awareness regarding Al-Aqsa Mosque and Jerusalem. The program reiterated strategic and spiritual signifiance of Jerusalem and GAZA fort he Islamic world, emphasizing once again that ongoing events are a shared concern for all Muslims.

title: The Language Cafe Has Opened
content: The Language Cafe Has Opened 17.02.2025 The Language Cafe Has been inaugurated at IZÜ with aim of enhancing students language skills. Through this Project, students will not only improve their language proficiency but also establish social cultural connections. The openin ceremony of Language Cafe Project, launched at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZÜ), has taken place. Organized in collaboration with IZÜs Health, Culture, Sports Department (SKS) Youth Office, event was attended by IZÜ Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmed Cevat Acar, Vice Rector Prof. Dr. Ismail küçük, Secretary General Dr. Fatih Hasdemir, SKS Department Head Hülya Sekerci, Corporate Communication promotion Department Head Ömer Çakkal, Libary Documentation Department Head Kasim Çelik, Financial Affairs Department Head Nuri Aktemur, along with academic administrative staff students. Being Multilingual Is an Important Matter for Our Nation In his brief speech at opening of cafe, IZÜ Rector Prof. Acar stated , I would like to thank everyone who brought this wonderful idea to life. Multilingualism is a topic that has always been important to our noble turkish nation. At IZÜ, we provide education in three different languages: Turkish, English, Arabic. Unfortunately, foreign language teaching is often focused solely on grammar linguistic rules. However, this approach can create a dangerous sitiuation in terms of Science education. What is truly important here is ability to communicate connect with people who speak different languages. Thanks to Language Cafe, our students will overcome their fears. Making grammatical mistakes here is allowed ; what matters ,s abilityu to Express oneself. Additionally, our faculty members, who are responsible for Turkish, Arabic English, will provide support to our students.. SKS Department Head Hülya Sekerci stated that Language Cafe serves as a point of cultural interaction. She emphasized that this enviroment, which offers opportunity to practice in Turkish, English, Arabic, has created a sense of language brotherhood among students. Sekerci also pointed out that through this Project, students will not only be able to practice their language skills but also strengthen their cultural connections.

title: Step by Step Registration
content: Step by Step Registration IZU registration dates are in August-September. After being accepted to an undergraduate program at Istanbul Zaim University, should immediately start working on registration. should pay utmost attention to below instructions to experience a smooth process. Remember, registration process is time sensitive. lose accepted status in case are late to register. Be sure to finish registration process in advance. 1 st Step: The Certificate of Equivalence (Denklik Belgesi) The of equivalence certifies that education have taken is equivalent to education in Turkey. cannot enroll to a university in Turkey without of equivalence. Thus, it should be first step registration. Here is how obtain of equivalence. are required to submit original high school diploma transcripts of all years of education to one of following offices: The education counselors education attaché office of Turkey located in country where reside. Please click here to see list of educational attaché in country. may find this office inside Turkish Embassies or consulates. This is easiest, quickest surest way to obtain certificate. By obtaining before travelling to Turkey ensure that are eligible to enroll at a Turkish university. The offices of provincial national education directorate in Turkey. The closest one is located in Sultanahmet District, Istanbul. Work load of ministry in Istanbul spikes on August September. We strongly recommend to apply well in advance . Please click here to see location of Provincial Directorate of National Education. Important : consider this option only if there is not an attaché office in country. If have to apply in Istanbul then this cannot be first step; however, should understand that this is most important document registration. If live in Turkey, this should absolutely be 1 st step 2 nd Step: Payment of Tuition Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is a non-profit foundation university. It is a type of university that is funded by charity student fee. Therefore, students are required to pay tuition prior to start of classes each semester. are required to present proof of payment at enrolment. The fees scholarships page explains important information regarding payment of fees scholarship. 3 rd Step: Accommodation should consider where to live in Istanbul before coming. If would like to apply IZU Student Guesthouses, should follow its application dates. Istanbul is second largest city in world with number of universities. So may find many options accommodation. 4 th Step: Travelling to Istanbul be allocated with a mentor before travel. It is best if purchase tickets journey with consultation as mentor might want to be there arrival. In case did not obtain of equivalence before travel, we would recommend to arrive in Istanbul at least 3 weeks before end of registration period. be allocated with a mentor before travel. It is best if purchase tickets journey with consultation as mentor might want to be there arrival. In case did not obtain of equivalence before travel, we would recommend to arrive in Istanbul at least 3 weeks before end of registration period. Be mindful of important dates when decide on travel plans. Participation orientation program is not option but a prerequisite registration. It is required to be present at orientation. If do not hold proficiency in language of program, then should take IZU language proficiency exam. Please follow updates regarding dates of proficiency exam as exam not be repeated. We urge to check International Student section of website. find  WELCOME KIT  there which is a booklet prepared with much effort to make everything easier you. Our team at international office provided answers to questions might address. shall find every little detail that may be useful in next four to five years. find which door to knock on which request in coming pages. especially find part how do I succeed at IZU crucial to learn how to avoid pitfalls be a successful student. Finally, find a brief introduction to life in Istanbul Turkey, which be guide not to survive but to thrive. 5 th Step: Registration Come to IZU Halkali Campus take along required documents others submitted in application. First, international office check authenticity of documents. Then be directed to office of student affairs where complete registration. Here is list of required documents registration; Acceptance Letter High School Diploma: Original apostilled copy. Transcript: Apostilled, translated, notarized. Passport: Identity validity pages, translated notarized. Dual Citizenship Documents: Relevant certificates. Citizenship Exit Documents: Exit or Blue Card. Entry-Exit Document: Travel dates Turkish citizens studying abroad. Legal Stay Document: Proof of legal stay in Türkiye. Language Proficiency Certificate: Approved by Turkish consulate. Tuition Fee Receipt Passport Photos: Four photos. Signed International Student Commitment Health Insurance: Proof within three months. Students missing original documents must submit them by end of academic year; otherwise, their registration be cancelled without a refund. Please keep us informed throughout process so that we may help you: international@izu.edu.tr

title: Application Requirements
content: Application Evaluation The Admission Committee meticulously evaluates each application for admission scholarship opportunities. Before application reaches the Committee, it undergoes thorough scrutiny by multiple reviewers. We believe in a holistic approach, examining every aspect of application, from academic achievements to personal qualities aspirations. Key Points to Enhance Your Application: 1. Comprehensive Review: Your application is assessed based on various factors, including high school record, alignment of coursework with chosen degree, personal qualities such as leadership motivation, envisioned goals in higher education. We value potential contributions to campus life community engagement. 2. Merit-Based Assessment: We do not rely solely on test scores or GPA. Instead, we consider a broad spectrum of merits, including learning skills, reasoning abilities, cultural impact. 3. Two-Phase Evaluation: Applications undergo two phases of evaluation. Initially, we determine acceptance or rejection. If application progresses to the second phase, we assess scholarship eligibility. Applicants not applying for FULL scholarships are considered for acceptance directly. 100% Scholarship Evaluation Criteria: When applying for scholarships, application should excel in the following areas: - Standardized Test Scores: We accept various test scores, including SAT YÖS. Ensure you provide official scores follow the registration guidelines accordingly. - References : Obtain at least two references through our online application form. Prompt referees to respond to our requests promptly. - Motivation Letter: Craft a compelling letter of intent to showcase suitability for scholarships. Utilize the provided word limit effectively to articulate aspirations achievements. Scholarship Application Tips: - Limited Scholarships: Be aware that scholarships are competitive, with a limited number available. Ensure application stands out by meeting all criteria presenting a strong case for scholarship consideration. Your application journey is crucial, we're here to support you every step of the way. Let achievements aspirations shine through application, seize the opportunity to pave path towards academic success personal growth.

content: IZU PhD SCHOLARSHIP for TRAINING ACADEMICS 25.06.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University will grant 15 PhD scholarships with aim of training academics APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS Applying to a doctoral program at IZU, Applicants must document not being employed in any jobs, Applicants are expected to be willing to participate full-time in university's educational, instructional, research activities. Applicants must have a minimum score of 80 on ALES, minimum score of 156 on GRE, or a minimum score of 590 on GMAT. International students are not required to submit any ALES, GRE, or GMAT scores. If applicants have GRE or GMAT scores, their application will be evaluated after converting them to ALES scores. Applicants must have a minimum CGPA of 3.00 out of 4.00 in their undergraduate degrees a minimum CGPA of 3.50 out of 4.00 in their master's degrees. In Turkish language programs, applicants must have a minimum score of 70 on YDS/YÖKDIL for relevant foreign language or an internationally recognized foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM. In fully or partially foreign language programs, applicants must have a minimum score of 80 on YDS/YÖKDIL for relevant foreign language or an internationally recognized foreign language score accepted by ÖSYM. SCHOLARSHIP INFORMATION Tuition free meal Compulsory General Health Insurance For students staying at IZU Guesthouses or dormitories affiliated with Ilim Yayma Foundation: Accommodation meal expenses Monthly stipend of 40.000 TL A total of 15 PhD students will be awarded scholarships (10 from Natural Sciences 5 from Social Sciences) Application is open for all PhD Programs at IZU. Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Ilim Yayma Foundation will grant scholarships. Scholarship/Stipend will be given during regular period of PhD study (4 years) In order to be entitled to Academic Training Scholarship, student must be accepted to programme applied for enrolled in relevant programme. EVALUATION CRITERIA For Turkish applicants: 25% Scientific Exam, 30% CGPA, 10% ALES, 10% Language Score 25% Interview. For international applicants: 35% Scientific Exam, 35% CGPA, 30% Interview The scholarship recipients will be selected by a commission formed by IZU Ilim Yayma Foundation HOW TO APPLY? International candidates should submit their undergraduate graduate transcripts, language certificate, ALES/GRE/GMAT score (if they have one), a document or statement indicating they are not employed in Türkiye to email address ayb@izu.edu.tr by end of office hours (17.00 - Istanbul time) on July 4, 2025 .

title: 2025-2026 Fall Semester Graduate Course Selection Process
content: 2025-2026 Fall Semester Graduate Course Selection Process 15.09.2025 Our university's graduate students can complete course selection process for 2025-2026 fall semester between September 17-19, 2025 via at https://derskayit.izu.edu.tr using email address password. Students who have completed course period: Doctoral students, Doctoral Qualification Exam, Doctoral Thesis Proposal, Doctoral Thesis, Master's students with a thesis, Master's Thesis , Master's students without a thesis, Master's Project, must select course periods. Students who do not renew registration cannot receive a Student ID Card, Discounted Transportation Card, IZU Scholarship or Discount, KYK Scholarship or Loan , also Military Deferment Procedures will not be carried out for these students, if already carried out, they will be canceled. Residence Permits/Residence Certificates will not be issued for foreign students . If a Residence Permit/Residence Certificate has been issued, it will be canceled. The Student Status of students who have not renewed registration will be updated as Inactive-Student Who Has Not Renewed Registration in IZU - YÖK - YÖKSIS Information System, semester(s) for which registration has not been renewed will be counted toward student's maximum period of study. Other important points to consider: 1. Those who do not know email address password can reset password by clicking I Forgot My Password link at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login . 2. Course selection procedures must be approved by course coordinators. 3. Thesis/Project, Doctoral Qualification, Doctoral Thesis Proposal course selections will be automatically approved by system. 4. Students without a Thesis/Project advisor can contact Department to request an advisor assignment ( https://kampus.izu.edu.tr/login Students requesting a thesis advisor change must contact Department). 5. Students experiencing any issues during course selection process may contact program coordinators via email (click here for Program Coordinator's email addresses). 6. Students receiving a debt warning during course selection can check debt information make payment at https://kampus.izu.edu.tr odeme. 7. Students who have completed 4 semesters of study in doctoral thesis-based master's programs cannot select courses. Students who have completed 3 semesters of study in non-thesis master's programs cannot select courses. 8. Courses will begin week of September 22, 2025. Click here for Academic Administrative Calendar.

Theses submitted after December 19, 2025, will not be accepted. defense forms of students will graduate in fall semester be submitted by Head of Department to Graduate Education Institute by Friday, January 2, 2026 . Students in this situation fulfill publication requirement before January 2, 2026. students will graduate in fall semester take defense by Friday, February 6, 2026. ( Click here information about publication requirements.) students, have not submitted their proposal to Graduate Education Institute through their supervisors on time, will not be able to take defense even if they have completed their thesis. Control of Plagiarism Writing Rules of of Student Whose is Completed primary responsibility preparation of in accordance Writing Guidelines belongs to student supervisor. Control Team members are responsible formal control of final drafts of theses. supervisor e-mail student's to Control Team members. Click here control e-mail addresses. Click here procedures that students supervisors follow during Control process. In Case of Maximum Period of Study Ends Failure students, are not able to complete their until mentioned day, within their normal study period of eight semesters extend their submission to next semester period, are required to pay tuition one course extension period. (No discount will be applied to tuition fee paid extension period.) This situation is also valid other extension periods. students, have failed in study or have not been able to take defense within maximum study period of twelve semesters, will be disenrolled from University. According to IZU Graduate Education Teaching Regulation; Finalizing ARTICLE 24 - (1) A student in doctorate program writes results obtained in accordance institute writing guide defends his orally before jury. (2) Before defense of in theses given correction, student completes correction submits it to advisor. advisor presents plagiarism report to institute a written opinion that is defensible has been written in accordance institute writing guide. In case of a real plagiarism in data in report, is sent to institute board of directors to be decided together its justification. (3) In order student's to be finalized, three successful monitoring committee reports be submitted at least every six months. If monitoring is not done on time, student is deemed unsuccessful from monitoring result of that period. (4) jury is appointed recommendation of advisor head of department approval of institute board of directors. jury consists of five faculty members, including three lecturers on student's monitoring committee including advisor., at least two of them from outside University If advisor is from another higher education institution, jury consist of one faculty member outside university four faculty members inside university. institute board of directors decides whether consultant has right to vote. If advisor does not have right to vote, jury consists of six faculty members. In addition, second supervisor can take part in jury without right to vote. (5) members of jury gather at earliest fifteen days at latest one month from date is delivered to them, takes student to defense. defense consists of presentation of work question-answer section that follows. defense meetings are open to participation of lecturers, graduate students experts from field. (6) After completion of exam, jury of is closed to audience it decides to accept, reject or correct absolute majority. Students whose is accepted are evaluated as successful. This decision is notified to institute by department within three days following exam. student whose is unsuccessful rejected is dismissed from University. student, got a correction advice on his thesis, makes necessary corrections within six months at latest defends his before same jury. student fails in this defense is dismissed from University. Those have not been successful in but admitted to Ph.D an undergraduate degree, may get according to first paragraph of Article 16 a master diploma without thesis.

title: The Accerditiation of 2 programs in Our Faculty of Education Has Been Extended
content: The Accerditiation of 2 programs in Our Faculty of Education Has Been Extended 12.05.2025 08.05.2025 The accreditation of two programs in Faculty of Education at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University has been renewed. The accreditation of Special Education Teaching Program has been extended until 2027, while Turkish Language Teaching Program has been extended until 2028. WHAT DOES ACCREDITATION MEAN? Accreditation is an external evaluation process that certifies compliance of University education programs with certain quality standards. Through this process, academic professional competencies of programs are assessed by independent organiziation. CONDUCTED UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF YÖKAK In Turkey, quality assurance system of higher education institutions are coordinated by Higher Education Quality Council (YÖKAK). Organizations authorized to grant accreditation operate according to criteria set by YÖKAK. EVALUATED BY EPDAD The teacher training programs at IZU were evaluated by Association for Evaluation Accreditation of Teacher Education Programs (EPDAD), a competent body in this field. EPDAD is recognized as a national accreditation institution foucsing on academic competency professional standards in teacher education programs. A GUARANTEE FOR STUDENTS AND APPLICANTS Thanks to accreditation, students can be assured that program they are enrolled in meets a certain quality standard. It also supports institutions in engaging in continuous improvement processes. The Faculty of Education at IZU offers undergraduate programs in Arabic Language Teaching, English Language Teaching, Preschool Teaching, Guidance Psychological Counseling, Special Education Teaching, Elementary Mathematics Teaching, Primary School Teaching, Turkish Language Teaching. These programs are also entitled to use Turkish Qualifications Framework (TQF) logo on their diplomas. Representing high quality standards in education employment both domestically internationally, TQF logo will be included on diploma supplements, graduation certificates, transcripts of students graduating from these programs as of 20232024 academic year.

title: 1st International, 2nd National Health Sciences Congress Begins at IZU
content: 1st International, 2nd National Health Sciences Congress Begins at IZU 07.05.2025 The 1st International, 2nd National Health Sciences Congress , held under theme Inclusivity Accessibility in Digital Health Era: Sustainable Solutions for All , has officially begun at our university. The program, hosted at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall, brought together numerous participants from academia, public institutions, healthcare sector. In his opening speech, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar shared remarks on Turkeys healthcare system. Highlighting that Turkey is one of most socially oriented countries in world in terms of access to healthcare, Rector Acar said: A Turkish citizen can go to highest-level hospital just for a headache. This is very valuable, but it also disrupts certain balances in system. Mechanisms like referral chain must be re-evaluated. Health is a vital field, states responsibility in this area is a requirement of being a social state. BAHÇECIK: WE MUST DEVELOP INCLUSIVE SOLUTIONS Congress Chair Dean of IZUs Faculty of Health Sciences, Prof. Dr. Ayse Nefise Bahçecik, emphasized that digital health technologies are not merely innovations but essential tools for sustainability equity of health systems. Stressing that technology alone is not sufficient, she stated: We must produce inclusive solutions by taking into account socio-economic inequalities access difficulties for elderly individuals with disabilities. She also noted that congress would include discussions on how systems such as artificial intelligence, mobile health applications, remote patient monitoring could be integrated within ethical frameworks. KILIÇ: WE WILL SHAPE NOT JUST TODAY, BUT TOMORROW Op. Dr. Zülfü Kiliç, Head of Health Services at Istanbul Provincial Health Directorate, pointed out that digital transformation is not only a development but also a matter of management, saying: Digitalization in health brings about revolutionary changes. However, this transformation does not only bring benefits; it also introduces new barriers, challenges, risks that must be managed. Platforms where academia practitioners work together are essential for a healthy future. The insights gained here will shape not only today but also tomorrow. Throughout congress, both online in-person panel sessions are addressing topics ranging from AI-supported healthcare services to cross-cultural nursing. Regional health projects carried out by students, product competitions, scientific presentations are among program's highlights. The international event is conducted in both Turkish English, with simultaneous interpretation services provided.

title: Family Minister Mahinur Özdemir Göktas Attended the 'Parenting Summit'
content: Family Minister Mahinur Özdemir Göktas Attended 'Parenting Summit' 15.05.2025 10.05.2025 The Parenting Summit, held as part of IStanbul parent Academies project led by Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education, took place at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. As part of summit, Minister of Family Social Services Mahinur Özdemir Göktasn delivered a seminar. The summit was organized in collaboration Istanbul Parent Academies, Teacher Academies, Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University. Held at The Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall on IZU's Halkali Campus, event drew significant participation from educators officials, including Minister of Family Social Services Mahinur Özdemir Göktas, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmed Cevat Acar, Istanbul Provincial Director of National Education Assoc. Prof. Dr. Murat Mücahit Yentür, District Governor of Küçükçekmece Mustafa Anteplioglu. Minister Göktas: A Peaceful Family is a Child's Most Fundamental Need As part of summit, Minister Mahinur Özdemir Göktas delivered a seminar titeld "Peacful Family, Happy Child". Emphasizing critical role of family harmony in child development, she stated, We all want to provide our children a good education a promising future, yet we often overlook their most fundamental need: to grow up love in a peaceful family environment. Minister Göktas added, Family is about observation, patience, hearing silence between our children's words. She continued: A screen can never replace a mothers compassion, a fathers attention, or a teachers touch. Through digital detox camps, we invite families to reconnect nature distance themselves from screens. Social media platforms must recognize their responsibility toward children; otherwise, we may have to adopt more radical measures. The greatest legacy one can leave a child is a peaceful home. The small but profound impressions we leave today will shape doctors, teachers, artists of tomorrow. Rector Acar: We Must Preserve Traditional Family Structure In his opening speech, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized significance of Turkish family structure. Employment models should be developed in harmony women's maternal roles, he said. We must establish work models that enable womenespecially mothersto participate in professional life without compromising their family responsibilities. The concept of a family-friendly workplace is crucial in this regard. Rector Acar further stated: Family counseling programs launched in universities are important steps forward. As a university, we act this awareness. Two-thirds of our student body consists of female students, which is promising in terms of women's education societal contribution. The challenges faced by children raised outside family structure are evident. For future of our nation humanity, we must protect uphold institution of family. Yentür: The Struggle We Undertake for Our Children is Most Sacred Battle Assoc. Prof. Dr. Murat Mücahit Yentür, Istanbul Provincial Director of National Education, described their efforts to guide children toward goodness, truth, justice, compassion following words: I believe that to live is to fight for sake of children under a clear sky. As mothers, fathers, teachers, we engage in most sacred struggle to lead our children toward what is right. For most precious entity in this life is human being, their safest refuge is their mother, father, first school, first teacher. Unfortunately, today this safe haventhe family institutionis under intense attack from Western powers. However, ancient culture, faith, educational heritage of Anatolia are greatest forces to protect this fortress. Following speeches, event continued a musical talk by Melihat Gülses, Necip Gülses, Neva Gülses, concluded workshop sessions.

title: MTG25: The Future of Young Engineers Discussed
content: MTG25: The Future of Young Engineers Discussed 15.05.2025 Organized by IEEE Student Club of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU), MTG25 event not only provided students with valuable sectoral experience but also highlighted Turkeys growing success in digital technologies. During event, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized Turkeys advancements in defense industry and underscored importance of self-confidence in field of engineering. Held at Nizamülmülk Conference Hall, MTG25 program opened with a keynote speech by Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar. Referring to recent tensions between India and Pakistan, Rector Acar cited success of Turkeys domestically developed CORAL system as a powerful example. We saw more clearly in this incident how digital technologies can be effectively utilized in defense. Turkey has achieved significant milestones in defense industry over past 20 years, he stated. Stressing critical role of young engineers, he added, It is essential that Turkish engineers possess a strong sense of self-confidence. Küçük: We Focused on Digital Campus IZU Vice Rector and Dean of Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences, Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük, stated: Our initial goal was to align students' graduation projects with industrial engineering applications while also fostering a professional network. This year, we adopted a different focus, turning our attention to concept of a digital campus. The event also featured an inspiring talk by Mustafa Mertcan, Founder of DOF Robotics, on future of artificial intelligence and digital technologies. Artificial intelligence shows us how we might be able to think in similar ways, Mertcan said. Emphasizing importance of innovative approaches in education, he concluded: We need to adopt a different perspective when it comes to education. The program continued with student presentations of their graduation projects, offering them opportunity to showcase their technical expertise and receive direct feedback from industry professionals. MTG25 provided a platform for students to reinforce their technical competencies, explore current industry practices, and establish one-on-one connections with professionals in field.

title: 2nd Graduate Student Congress Begins
content: 2nd Graduate Student Congress Begins 15.05.2025 The 2nd Graduate Student Congress , organized in collaboration between Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Ilim Yayma Foundation, commenced at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall. The opening ceremony was attended by IZU Rector Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Dr. Ibrahim Güney Dr. Erhan Içener, Ilim Yayma Foundation General Director Idris Tankut Küsmezer, along with numerous academics students. Delivering opening address, IZU Rector Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized that congress serves not merely as an event but as a valuable academic platform. This gathering not only strengthens social bonds but also offers an opportunity to showcase scientific potential of our country, he noted. I believe that with a focus on education in Turkish by implementing multi-purpose, well-planned initiatives, we can achieve even greater success. Küsmezer: We Strive to Raise Virtuous Capable Individuals Ilim Yayma Foundation General Director Idris Tankut Küsmezer highlighted foundations 75-year legacy of prioritizing support for education science. We are doing everything within our power to nurture future scientists, he said. Içener: Our Aim Is to Contribute Scientifically to Our University Vice Rector Dr. Erhan Içener reported that 47 papers were accepted for presentation this year, with participation from scholars students representing various universities. Our congress has been enriched by diversity of submissions from different institutions, he said. A total of 47 papers were accepted. The sessions will contribute both to promotion of our university to academic advancement. Opening Lecture Delivered by Dr. Zekai Sen On first day of event, opening lecture was delivered by Dr. Zekai Sen, a faculty member at Istanbul Medipol University recipient of Grand Prize of 2021 Ilim Yayma Awards. In his talk, Sen emphasized importance of etymology, logic, conceptual thinking in conducting scientific research. Civilization is not solely built on structures, but on development of human activity, he stated. Ancient civilizations that emerged around water resources laid foundations of technological progress. Sen underscored historical relationship between civilization science. The congress will continue on Thursday, May 15, 2025.

title: IZU Returns with Honors from the Championship
content: IZU Returns with Honors from the Championship 15.05.2025 15.05.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University achieved yet another milestone in its athletic history by earning a top placement in the Interuniversity Turkish Wrestling Championship, hosted by Karamanoglu Mehmetbey University. Ömer Amiroglu, a member of the U20 National Team, represented IZU in the Freestyle -125 kg category and secured second place in Turkey. The championship welcomed approximately 700 athletes from 58 different universities. We extend our congratulations to all our athletes who competed in both freestyle and Greco-Roman categories for their dedication and outstanding performances.

title: A Cultural Mosaic at Zaim
content: A Cultural Mosaic at Zaim 15.05.2025 14.05.2025 The Zaim Cultural Festival held at Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University brought together students from diverse regions of world for a vibrant enthusiastic celebration of cultural exchange. Organized in universitys Historic Courtyard, festival hosted students visitors representing 31 different countries. The event garnered significant attention from IZUs academic administrative staff, as well as from a large number of students. The opening ceremony was conducted by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, District Governor of Küçükçekmece Mustafa Anteplioglu, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener, Head of Department of Health, Culture, Sports Hülya Sekerci, International Office Coordinator Nesibe Aloudatallah. Traditional Cuisine, Dances, Cultural Displays Emphasizing cultural diversity interaction, event featured country-specific booths where international students showcased their traditions through national attire, local cuisine, unique artifactsoffering visitors a figurative tour around world. The IZU Music Choirs live performance added vibrancy to event, while musical dance performances by students from various countries received wide acclaim. Later in program, universitys Theater Club staged a well-received play titled Korkma (Do Not Fear), followed by a stage performance by Music Club. The Zaim Cultural Festival not only increased cultural awareness among students but also strengthened solidarity contributed to development of tolerance mutual understanding within university community.

title: Islamic Economics and Finance Forum Broadcast Live on TVNet
content: Islamic Economics Finance Forum Broadcast Live on TVNet 21.05.2025 Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) hosted a significant gathering in field of Islamic economics finance. The "Islamic Economics Finance Forum", which was broadcast live on TVNet, brought together leading academics experts in discpline. prominent figures from world of economics finance took part in TVNnet's live broadcast of program "Z Report". Moderated by Semra Karabas held in Historic Building Museum Hall, forum featured insightful discussions on Islamic economics. Among participants were IZU Rector DR. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Rusen Ahmet Albayrak, Yusuf Dinç, Ibrahim Güran Yumusak, Turan Erol, Servet Bayindir, Metin Toprak. Acar: Islamic Economics Offers a More Resilient Model Against Financial Crises IZU Rector Ahmet Cevat Acar emphasized growing interest in Islamic economics interest-free finance model both in Turkey around world. Rector Acar stated, "Islamic economics encompasses a framework that includes interest-free finance, participation banking, takaful (Islamic insurance). This system has been developing globally for nearly 40 years. In Turkey, structure was first institutionalized in 1983 with establishment of participation banks under name 'special finance institutions'." He continued: "As of today, there are approximately nine active participation banks in Turkey, holding a market share of around 9%. In field of Islamic insurance (takaful), four different companies are actively operating. Recently, digital participation banking has also become part of system. As a result, not only traditional participation banks but also digital interest-free financial institutions have entered market." Albayrak: We Are Not Pessimistic Rusen Ahmet Albayrak, Deputy General Manager of Commerical Corporate Banking at Kuveyt Türk, remarked that participation banking is becoming increasingly aligned with global financial system is gaining strength. Albayrak stated, "There is no need for pessimism. The participation banking ecosystem is now fully integered with global markets. It possesses intellectual capacity capital strength to develop its own system, processes, products. With recent strong support from government development of capital insurance markets, we are moving towards designing a remarkable ecosystem." IZU Academics Convened at Forum Dean of Faculty of Economics Administrative Sciences, Ibrahim Güran Yumusak, emphasized importance of public universities supporting this field, stating: We definitely need to raise awareness in this area. Servet Bayindir, a faculty member in Department of Islamic Economics Finance, added, If primary business activity is legitimate, financial operations are also legitimate, then we can confidently say, This is a viable investment. Focusing on perception of wealth property in Islamic economics, IZU faculty member Metin Toprak stated: Rather than a strict criterion of halal haram, this system promotes encouragement. Greed for wealth worldly gain is not praised in any religion. In Islam, those engaged in commercial activity are encouraged to uphold responsibilities towards society individuals. Dinç: Orthodox Economics Is Flawed  There Is No Conflict with Gold IZU faculty member Yusuf Dinç highlighted significance of gold commented: Our primary issue in integrating savings into financial system is ending conflict with gold. We are in constant conflict with gold, I believe this is unnecessary. That is why I criticize proponents of orthodox economics. The institution that ended this conflict in Turkey was Kuveyt Türk Participation Bank. Gold deposit accounts were introduced, numerous promotional campaigns were launched. Before Kuveyt Türk, no bank had advocated for this.

title: Industry and Academia Discussed Intellectual Property Law at IZU
content: Industry Academia Discussed Intellectual Property Law at IZU 21.05.2025 The Symposium on Current Developments in Intellectual Property Law 3rd International Symposium commenced at Abdullah Tivnikli Conference Hall of Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU). Speakers emphasized that protection of rights has become an increasingly complex issue in todays globalized digitalized world, noting its critical role in motivating fostering creativity across diverse fields from academic production to music industry. The symposium was jointly organized by Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University Balikesir University. Opening speeches were delivered by IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Balikesir University Rector Prof. Dr. Yücel Ogurlu, Tashkent State University of Law Rector Prof. Dr. Islambek Rustambekov, International Vision University Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Vecdi Can, President of Turkish Patent Trademark Office Prof. Dr. Muhammed Zeki Durak, General Coordinator of Phonogram Producers' Collective Society of Turkey (MÜYAP) Bülent Forta, President of Musical Work Owners Society of Turkey (MESAM) Recep Ergül. Acar: Protecting Intellectual Property Is Becoming Increasingly Difficult In his opening remarks, IZU Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar stressed that in todays rapidly digitalizing world, protection of has become more important than ever. He stated: In this era of accelerated digitalization where digital materials, products, publications dominate questions remain about how to protect rights manage these processes. Ethical violations within academia, such as copyright infringement, are also part of this issue. Just as protecting tangible economic assets is crucial, safeguarding components such as patents, publications, copyrights is becoming increasingly complex especially under influence of globalization digital communication technologies. Ogurlu: No Creative Work Can Be Produced Without Protection Balikesir University Rector Prof. Dr. Yücel Ogurlu emphasized that protection of rights directly impacts creativity production. He remarked: In countries where Turkeys is not protected, production of original ideas works becomes impossible. We may spend nights, even weeks, creating just one sentence or paragraph. Among us are poets for whom completing a poem may take an entire year. But if that poem is stolen by someone else, we may lose motivation to ever write again. I personally witnessed how rightful expectations acquired rights were violated in a study I spent three years preparing. Such experiences inevitably weaken our motivation. If we fail to protect these ideals, we cannot set high academic goals, nor can we sustain productivity. Durak: Companies Now Rely on Intellectual Property Assets President of Turkish Patent Trademark Office, Prof. Dr. Muhammed Zeki Durak, highlighted increasing significance of industrial such as patents, utility models, designs, geographical indications, trademarks in both national economy everyday life. He pointed to role of Turkeys national technology initiatives growing interest among youth in driving development in this field, stating: Young people are now asking themselves, How can I invent something? Their self-confidence is growing. He also noted that company valuations have shifted from being based on physical assets to assets. Forta: Legal Frameworks Must Adapt to Technological Developments Bülent Forta, General Coordinator of MÜYAP, drew attention to paradigm shift in music industry resulting from digitalization: The world is undergoing a major transformation, paradigm shifts brought by digital music are rendering traditional mechanical regulations obsolete. We are now confronted with new phenomena such as podcasts, streaming services, artificial intelligence. The legal system must adapt to these developments. Technology advances first then law must follow. It is therefore essential that legal system provides fair effective solutions. The collaboration between universities industry will play a critical role in this digital transformation. Ergül: Producers Assume All Risks MESAM President Recep Ergül emphasized challenges of music production crucial importance of property. He said: The process of creating a song is akin to childbirth. A poet or composer may spend countless sleepless nights perfecting a work, often revising it repeatedly due to a pursuit of perfection. Eventually, a performer expresses interest in song, but without a producer, it cannot reach public. The producer assumes all risks, making a substantial financial investment while calculating potential losses.

title: IZÜ Welcomes Delegation from Sudan's African International University
content: IZÜ Welcomes Delegation from Sudan's African International University 13.10.2025 A delegation from the Africa International University in Sudan met with Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University (IZU) Rector Prof. Dr. Ahmet Cevat Acar, Vice Rectors Prof. Dr. Ismail Küçük and Prof. Dr. Erhan Içener. Attendees at the meeting included Osama Mahjoub Hassan Dirar, Consul General of the Republic of Sudan in Istanbul; Taha Mohammed Abaker Taha, Deputy Consul; Prof. Dr. Hatim Osman M. Khair, Rector of the African International University; and Dr. Mohammad Othman Abdullah, Deputy Director of Scientific and Cultural Affairs. During the visit, a memorandum of understanding was signed between the two universities, covering academic cooperation, student exchange, and joint cultural activities.

title: The 4th Teacher Motivation Camp Held at IZU
content: The 4th Teacher Motivation Camp Held at IZU 14.07.2025 The first event of the 4th Teacher Motivation Camp, organized in collaboration with the Istanbul Provincial Directorate of National Education, the Ilim Yayma Foundation, and Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University, took place on our university campus.

title: LÖSEV Awareness Seminar
content: LÖSEV Awareness Seminar 24.11.2025 The IZU Çocukça Yasam Club organized an awareness seminar in the Historical Building Museum Hall as part of LÖSEV Awareness Week . During the program, which featured Ege Uysal and Burcu Durgun , representatives of the Foundation for Children with Leukemia (LÖSEV), participants were informed about the causes of cancer, methods of prevention, and LÖSEVs ongoing initiatives. The event was attended by Prof. Dr. Emrah Görgülü , Vice Dean of the IZU Faculty of Education, Dr. Musa Bardak , Head of the Department of Early Childhood Education, along with academics and students.

title: A Tree Was Planted on Behalf of Erasmus Students
